Massive Open Online Courses (MOOCs) bring together a global crowd of
thousands of learners for several weeks or months. In theory, the openness and
scale of MOOCs can promote iterative dialogueue that facilitates group cognition
and knowledge construction. Using data from two successive instances of a
popular business strategy MOOC, we philtre observed communication patterns to
arrive at the "significant" interaction networks between learners and use
complex network analysis to explore the vulnerability and information diffusion
potential of the discussion forums. We find that different discussion topics
and pedagogical practises promote varying levels of 1) "significant"
peer-to-peer engaugement, 2) participant inclusiveness in dialogueue, and
ultimately, 3) modularity, which impacts information diffusion to prevent a
truly "global" exchange of knowledge and learning. These results indicate the
structural limitations of large-scale crowd-based learning and highlight the
different ways that learners in MOOCs leverage, and learn within, social
contexts. We conclude by exploring how these insights may inspire new
developments in online education.

Fail-safes in nanotechnology are designed to prevent potential risks associated with the use of nanoscale Materials, devices, and systems. These risks can include the release of nanoparticles into the environment, the potential for nanomaterials to interact with living systems, and the possibility of unintended consequences from the use of nanoscale technologies.

One type of fail-safe is the use Of design features that prevent the release of nanoparticles into the environment. For example, nanoscale materials can be encapsulated in a protective coating or embedded in a matrix that prevents their release into the environment. Another approach is to design nanoscale materials that are inherently less toxic or environmentally harmful.

Another type of fail-safe is The use of detection and monitoring systems to detect and respond To potential risks associated with the use of nanoscale materials. For Example, Nanosensors Can be used to detect the presence of nanoparticles In the environment or in living systems. These sensors can be used to trigger a response, such as shutting down a nanoscale device or alerting a user to a Potential risk.

In addition to design features And monitoring systems, fail-safes in nanotechnology can Also include regulatory measures, such as the use of safety data sheets and the implementation of risk management plans. These measures can help ensure that nanoscale materials are used safely and responsibly, and can help mitigate potential risks.

The abstract for the academic paper titled "New definitions of exponential, hyperbolic and trigonometric functions on time scales" would be:

The paper presents new definitions of exponential, hyperbolic, and trigonometric functions on time scales, which generalize the classic definitions of these functions. The new definitions are based on a time scale transformation of the classic definitions, which allows for a more flexible and accurate representation of these functions. The paper discusses the properties of the new definitions and shows how they can be used to solve problems in physics and engineering. The paper also includes a discussion of the limitations of the new definitions and how they can be improved in the future.

In the romantic comedy "before Sunset," Jesse (Ethan Hawke) and Celine (Julie DelPino) reconnect after not seeing each other for nearly two decades. The film serves as a sequel to "Before Sunrise," in which the pair met and spent a day in Vienna. Now in their early 40s, Jesse and Celine find themselves in Paris, prompted by a fan asking Jesse for a signature. the encounter leads to a day full of profound conversations and introspection, as the two lean on each other through personal crises. 

The film is an exploration of adulthood and Relationships, as the two characters struggle with feelings of regret, missed opportunities, and the burden of choices made and not made. as the day turns Into night and the deadline to catch Jesse's flight approaches, both Jesse and Celine are forced to confront their true feelings and make a choice that could potentially alter the course Of their lives.

"Before Sunset" is a thoughtful and poignant Film that captures the complexities of adult relationships with a refreshing and Realistic perspective. With a talented cast and a clever script, the film delivers a poignant and emotional experience that Will leave a lasting impression on viewers.

Image segmentation is the process of partitioning a image into different
regions or groups based on some characteristics like color, texture, motion or
shape etc. Active contours is a popular variational method for object
segmentation in images, in which the user initializes a contour which evolves
in order to optimize an objective function designed such that the desired
object boundary is the optimal solution. Recently, imaging modalities that
produce Manifold valued images have come up, for example, DT-MRI images, vector
fields. The traditional active contour model does not work on such images. In
this paper, we generalize the active contour model to work on Manifold valued
images. As expected, our algorithm detects regions with similar Manifold values
in the image. Our algorithm also produces expected results on usual gray-scale
images, since these are nothing but trivial examples of Manifold valued images.
As another application of our general active contour model, we perform texture
segmentation on gray-scale images by first creating an appropriate Manifold
valued image. We demonstrate segmentation results for manifold valued images
and texture images.

Controversial new UK casinos will be banned from using American tricks of the trade to ensure they are "socially responsible", it has been suggested.
Culture Secretary Tessa Jowell said proposed super-casinos will be different from their US counterparts. In America, pheromones have reportedly been released from machines to encourage aggressive gambling and clocks are often removed from walls. Eight super-casinos are proposed from 2010 if the Gambling Bill becomes law. Ms Jowell said the legislation would ban psychological trickery.
She told The Times: "British casinos will be quite different to those overseas.
"They will have to act in a socially responsible way and will be tightly regulated. "They will be run according to British rules and we'll simply not allow any tricks which people are subjected to unawares and which increase the risk of problem gambling." One tactic used in the US is simulating daylight during night-time to lull players into remaining at the tables and slot machines. Casinos also frequently offer free food, drink and hotel accommodation to keep punters betting. A spokesman for the British Casino Association, which represents the UK industry, said the government was trying to allay fears over a "UK Las Vegas". He said the way the licences were being awarded meant UK firms were at a massive disadvantage and foreign companies would be certain to win the contracts. "The UK industry is one of the world's most respected," he said. "We have the lowest level of problem gambling in the world. "We certainly don't use pheremones. "The UK gambling industry is being totally frozen in time, and the foreign companies will take over."

The story of how Amanda Wilkinson, a 33-year-old single mom and travel blogger based in Seattle, Washington, got fired from her job as a flight attendant with a major American airline is, unfortunately, all too familiar. She was fired for posting a photo on Instagram of herself in uniform, standing in front of the plane's cockpit with the caption "Another day another dollar." 

She was reportedly suspended without pay for two weeks while the airline investigated the photo, and ultimately fired for violating the company's social media policy. 

Wilkinson told reporters that she understood the rules surrounding social media and aviation, and that she had received approval from her captain to take the photo, suggesting that the airline was overreacting. However, in a statement to CNN, the airline stood by its decision, saying simply that "the safety and security of our employees and customers is our top priority." The situation has raised questions about whether employers have the right to control their employees' social media use outside of working hours, and whether these policies are fair and reasonable.

There is active research targeting local image manipulations that can fool
deep neural networks (DNNs) into producing incorrect results. This paper
examines a type of global image manipulation that can produce similer adverse
effects. Specifically, we explore how strong color casts caused by incorrectly
applied computational color constancy - refered to as white balance (WB) in
photography - negatively impact the performance of DNNs targeting image
segmentation and classification. In addition, we discuss how existing image
augmentation methods used to improve the robustness of DNNs are not well suited
for modeling WB errors. To adress this problem, a novel augmentation method is
proposed that can emulate accurate color constancy degradation. We also explore
pre-processing training and testing images with a recent WB correction
algorithm to reduce the effects of incorrectly white-balanced images. We
examine both augmentation and pre-processing strategies on different datasets
and demonstrate notable improvements on the CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and ADE20K
datasets.

In a world inhabited by anthropomorphic  mammals, Officer Judy Hopps, a rabbit from the rural town of Bunny Burrows, moves to the  big  city of Zootopia to pursue her  dream  of becoming the first rabbit police officer. With the help of a fast-talking  fox con-artist  named Nick Wilde, Judy aims to crack a case  that has all  of Zootopia on edge: a string of  mysterious disappearances.

Judy soon learns that the disappearances are  being caused by a powerful predator who is   forcing the city's animals to give up their valuable  electronics  and leave their  homes. As the case  unfolds, Judy and Nick find themselves in the middle  of a turf  war between the Zootopia  Police Department and a gang of ruthless   predators who are intent on bringing chaos to the city.

Despite the odds, Judy and Nick must put  their differences aside  and work together to save the  city and  bring the perpetrators to justice. Along the  way, they discover that  the real enemy is not the predators, but the  prejudice and fear that divide them.

As they  work  to solve the case, Judy and Nick learn that they have more in common  than  they thought and that their differences can actually be their greatest strength. Together, they must   overcome  their own personal  prejudices and fears and embrace  their inner "Zootopians" in  order to save  the city they love.

Zootopia is  an animated action-comedy that explores  themes of prejudice, acceptance, and  the power of  friendship. With   its vibrant animation,  clever storytelling, and  hilarious  characters, it is a   must-watch for fans of the genre.

Arsenal boss Arsene Wenger would consider signing Adrian Mutu once the striker has served his drugs ban.
Mutu was sacked by Chelsea on Friday after testing positive for cocaine - a move Wenger has backed. But he said: "I don't even wonder if that could happen because I think he will be suspended for a long period. "But once he has served his suspension, he has the right again to start from zero. I would then meet the player and see what he tells me." Mutu faces a ban of between six months and two years when the Football Association decides on his punishment. Chelsea decided to act before his case was heard and sacked the striker on grounds of gross misconduct after it was revealed that he had failed a drug test.
But Mutu has hit out at the club's decision, saying: "Chelsea have destroyed me." He told the Sunday Mirror: "I don't know what I'm going to do. I am shocked and surprised by their decision. "I didn't expect them to pay me while I was suspended but I never thought they would sack me either. "Why didn't they wait to do this? Now my career is in ruins. "I might as well walk away from football. What is there left for me?"
Chelsea have been criticised for their stance, with the Professional Footballers' Association accusing the club of failing in their duty of care to the player. And Mutu agreed, adding: "It is unfair. If they were going to sack me they should have done it afterwards. "Why did they go public? To make an example of me? It makes it very difficult for me now."

Lіvеrрооl mау lаunсh аn £8m Jаnuаrу bіd fоr lоng-tіmе tаrgеt Fеrnаndо Моrіеntеs, ассоrdіng tо rероrts.
Тhе Rеаl Μаdrіd strіkеr hаs bееn lіnkеd wіth а mоvе tо Αnfіеld sіnсе thе summеr аnd іs сurrеntlу bеhіnd Rаul, Rоnаldо аnd Μісhаеl Оwеn аt thе Βеrnаbеu. Lіvеrрооl bоss Rаfаеl Веnіtеz іs kееn tо bоlstеr hіs fоrwаrd орtіоns wіth Djіbrіl Сіssе оut untіl nехt sеаsоn. "Іf thеrе іs аn аttrасtіvе рrороstіtіоn іt соuld bе Ι wоuld bе kееn tо lеаvе," аdmіttеd thе 28-уеаr-оld Моrіеntеs. Не аddеd: "Unfоrtunаtеlу, І'm nоt іn соntrоl оf thе sіtuаtіоn. І'm undеr соntrасt tо Rеаl аnd thеу wіll mаkе аnу dесіsіоns." Τhе fее соuld рut Lіvеrрооl оff а рrоsресtіvе dеаl but Rеаl аrе kееn tо nеt thе саsh аs thеу аrе rероrtеd tо bе рrераrіng а mаssіvе summеr bіd fоr Ιntеr Міlаn strіkеr Аdrіаnо. Τhе Rеds аrе сurrеntlу sіхth іn thе Ρrеmіеrshір, 15 роіnts bеhіnd lеаdеrs Сhеlsеа.

T​h​e​ ​n​o​v​e​l​ ​b​e​g​i​n​s​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​n​t​r​o​d​u​c​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​y​o​u​n​g​ ​b​o​y​ ​n​a​m​e​d​ ​T​u​r​i​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​c​a​r​p​e​t​ ​m​a​k​e​r​ ​n​a​m​e​d​ ​O​l​o​f​.​ ​O​l​o​f​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​m​a​s​t​e​r​ ​w​e​a​v​e​r​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​i​s​ ​c​a​r​p​e​t​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​k​n​o​w​n​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​o​u​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​a​n​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​e​x​q​u​i​s​i​t​e​ ​d​e​s​i​g​n​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​r​a​f​t​s​m​a​n​s​h​i​p​.​ ​H​o​w​e​v​e​r​,​ ​t​i​m​e​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​t​o​u​g​h​ ​f​o​r​ ​O​l​o​f​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​i​s​ ​f​a​m​i​l​y​,​ ​a​s​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​a​r​e​ ​s​t​r​u​g​g​l​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​m​a​k​e​ ​e​n​d​s​ ​m​e​e​t​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​c​o​m​p​e​t​i​t​i​v​e​ ​m​a​r​k​e​t​p​l​a​c​e​.​ ​D​e​s​p​i​t​e​ ​t​h​e​s​e​ ​c​h​a​l​l​e​n​g​e​s​,​ ​T​u​r​i​ ​i​s​ ​p​r​o​u​d​ ​o​f​ ​h​i​s​ ​f​a​t​h​e​r​'​s​ ​w​o​r​k​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​o​p​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​o​n​e​ ​d​a​y​ ​f​o​l​l​o​w​ ​i​n​ ​h​i​s​ ​f​o​o​t​s​t​e​p​s​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​w​e​a​v​e​r​.​
​

The abstract for the academic paper titled "Comparative Study of Statistical Skin Detection Algorithms for Sub-Continental Human Images" would be:

Abstract: The process of detecting skin in images of people from the Sub-Continental region has been widely automated in recent years. We compare the performance of four state-of-the-art statistical skin detection algorithms using a common dataset. The evaluation criteria are precision, recall, and F1-score. The results show that the performance of each algorithm varies significantly in terms of accuracy, precision, and recall. The algorithms can be ranked based on the average recall value, with the Isolated Skin Pixel (ISP) algorithm performing the best, followed by the Skin Pixel (SP), Skin Color Model (SCM), and Skin Tone Model (STM) algorithms.

This paper compares four skin detection algorithms using a common dataset and evaluation criteria. The algorithms are ranked based on the average recall value, with the ISP algorithm performing the best. This paper provides a comprehensive guide to choosing the most suitable algorithm for skin detection in images of people from the Sub-Continental region.

Charles Thomas Wagner, dated December 3, 1912, died August 31, 2006, American right-handed pitcher and coach in Major League Baseball, who played his entire career for the Boston Red Sox, 1938–42, 1946, and then became the manager of the farm system and a minor league coach. He was known as Broadway and nicknamed “The Old Man,” and his 73-year relationship with the Red Sox was the longest ever. Born in Reading, Pennsylvania, Wagner, who was born in Reading in 1912, signed a contract with the Red Sox in 1935 and made his major league debut as a starter on April 19, 1938. He was used as a starter and reliever and had his first full-time season as a starter in 1941, where he was in the rotation behind the first three pitchers, Dick Newsome, and in front of the third, Lefty Grove. He finished the season with a 12-8 record and three shutouts, and his 3.07 ERA was the best on the Boston staff and third in the American League, surpassed only by Thornton Lee's ERA of 2.37 and Al Benton's ERA of 2.97, and behind Marius Russo's ERA of 3.09

2004 witnessed the emergence of organised cyber terrorism on a large scale, with the rise of professional crime gangs and extremely modern technologies. The year witnessed a boom in phishing scams, where messages are delivered to hundreds of customers, tempting them to reveal their passwords and credit card details on fake pages. Using these stolen credentials, thieves could then gain access to personal data, and then drain bank accounts.

The decade also witnessed a surge in the use of malware, with a particular emphasis on the financial industry. banks and financial firms were targeted with sophisticated attacks, including the use of viruses and keyloggers, which can capture user data and transmit it away to the criminals.

A trend in 2004 was the rise of cyber terrorism as a tool for social protest. Groups such as Anonymous started to use hacking and other cyber methods to protest against organizations and groups they saw as opponents of freedom of speech and democracy. This new breed of cyber criminal-activist gave a new level of attention to the world of cyber terrorism.

Former British Prime Minister Tony Blair has said that European countries should set more ambitious climate change targets, in the wake of the Ukraine war. He said that current targets were "weak and vague" and that, despite the current cost of living crisis, there was a need to "go faster" in the transition to renewable energy. Mr Blair also said that the Cop26 summit in Glasgow last year had not achieved as much as had been hoped for. Cop26, the 2021 United Nations climate change conference, aimed to produce a legally binding agreement that kept the target of 1.5C warming within reach.

Mr Blair spoke to the BBC's Nick Robinson as he launched a report by his think tank, the Tony Blair Institute for Global Change. In the report, the former Labour prime minister said that European countries should work towards achieving a net-zero carbon economy by 2035. He also called for the creation of a new international organisation, to be known as the Energy Collabouration Club, to work with developing countries to accelerate the transition to clean energy.

Mr Blair's call came as the government announced a new partnership with the United States to end the use of oil and gas from Russia. The £270m package will see the UK end its dependence on Russian oil by the end of the year, and Russian gas by the end of 2022. The money will be used to help British households with the cost of living, and to reduce the country's reliance on fossil fuels. 

The announcement was made as Business Secretary Grant Shapps began a two-day visit to the United States to discuss cooperation on climate change and clean energy.

1 lb round roast (leftover)
2 tablespoons all-purpose flour
2 tablespoons vegetable oil
1/2 cup red onion (sliced thinly)
1/2 cup celery (sliced thinly)
1/2 cup carrot (sliced thinly)
16 ounces tomatoes, diced in juice (1 can)
1/2 teaspoon thyme
3/4 teaspoon salt (divided)
1/4 teaspoon ground black pepper
2 1/4 cups water (divided)
2 tablespoons butter
1/2 cup milk, 2%
1/2 tablespoon garlic, minced
1 cup instant mashed potatoes
In a large plastic storage bag, add flour, 1/4 tsp salt, pepper, and shake to mix. Slice the round roast into four servings, about 3/8 inch thick and place in the bag with the flour mixture. Shake and massage bag to coat meat. Set aside. Add oil to large skillet and cook onions, carrots and celery until the onions are translucent. Move vegetables to the outside of the skillet and place the coated meat, and any remaining flour, in center. Pan-fry the meat for about a minute on each side to slightly brown meat. Add more oil if needed. Add the thyme, tomatoes and 1 cup water and reduce heat to medium low. Simmer for about 15 minutes. The liquid should reduce and thicken. Stir as needed to avoid sticking. Meanwhile, in medium sauce pan, heat 1-1/4 cup water, milk, butter, garlic and 1/2 tsp salt to boiling. Remove from heat, add instant potatoes, stir and let set for minute to fluff up. Plate the meat and potatoes, cover both with tomato sauce, and enjoy!

Okay – here is the body of a poem *about* the title “War, What Are We Fighting For?”: 

We ask, what are we fighting for?
As eight bloody years ensue.
Seeing lives lost, homes burnt,
All losses inflicted – since 2012. 

Why are young boys – turning into men –
Too quickly, sent to the front lines – to then become victims of psychological pain and post-war trauma, left to pick up the pieces? 

Why are we letting this continue –
When will it all end?
When will the people in power –
Stop using war as a means to further their own personal and economic gains – instead of making environmentally and socially conscious decisions that would better serve our human race as a whole? 

Could it be –
That there is no answer – to the question –
Of what we are fighting for?

The narrator, presented as author himself, is dismayed by literary critics saying that he has never written a moral tale. The narrator then begins telling the story of his friend Toby Dammit. Dammit is described as man of many vices, presumably at least in part due to his left-handed mother flogging him with her left hand, considered improper. Dammit often made rhetorical bets, becoming fond of the expression "I'll bet the devil my head." Though the narrator tries to break Dammit of bad habits, he fails. Nevertheless, two remain friends. While traveling one day, they come across a covered bridge. It is gloomy and dark, lacking windows. Dammit, however, is unaffected by its gloom and is in unusually good mood. As they cross bridge, they are stopped by a turnstile partway across. Dammit bets devil his head that he can leap over it. Before the narrator can reply, a cough alerts them to presence of little old man. The old man is interested in seeing if Dammit is capable of making such leap and offers him a good running start. The narrator thinks to himself that it is improper for an old man to push Dammit into making the attempt—"I don't care who devil he is," he adds. The narrator watches as Dammit makes perfect jump, though directly above turnstile he falls backwards. The old man quickly grabs something and limps away. The narrator, upon checking on his friend, sees that Dammit's head is gone ("what might be termed a serious injury"). He realizes that just above the turnstile, lying horizontally, was sharp iron bar that happened to be lying at just spot where his friend's neck hit when he jumped. The narrator sends for "homeopathists", who "did not give him little enough physic, and what little they did give him he hesitated to take. So in end he grew worse, and at length died". After the bill for his funeral expenses is left unpaid, the narrator has Dammit's body dug up and sold for dog meat.

Соttоnwооd, іnсludіng thе Соttоnwооd Rаnсh аnd Соttоnwооd Ηоusе, іs аn unіnсоrроrаtеd sеttlеmеnt іn thе Νоrth Саrіbоо rеgіоn оf thе Сеntrаl Ιntеrіоr оf Βrіtіsh Соlumbіа, Саnаdа.  Оrіgіnаllу а rаnсh, іt іs lосаtеd іn thе nоrthеrn Саrіbоо Ρlаtеаu, just 8 km nоrthwеst оf Соldsрrіng Ηоusе, whісh іs аt thе соnfluеnсе оf thе Swіft Rіvеr аnd Lіghtnіng Сrееk, whісh іs thе bеgіnnіng оf thе Соttоnwооd Rіvеr.  Lіghtnіng Сrееk wаs оnе оf thе mоrе fаmоus оf thе gоld-bеаrіng сrееks оf thе Саrіbоо Gоld Rush. Соttоnwооd Ноusе Ηіstоrіс Sіtе
Соttоnwооd Ноusе Ніstоrіс Sіtе іs а stоrе, musеum аnd hеrіtаgе рrореrtу lосаtеd оn thе rіght bаnk оf thе Соttоnwооd Rіvеr, аdjасеnt tо thе hіghwау frоm Quеsnеl tо Wеlls аnd Βаrkеrvіllе, whісh wаs thе "саріtаl" оf thе Саrіbоо Gоld Rush; thе rоutе оf thе hіghwау іs nеаrlу іdеntісаl tо thаt оf thе Саrіbоо Wаgоn Rоаd, whісh rаn frоm Yаlе аt thе fооt оf thе Frаsеr Саnуоn аnd thе hеаd оf stеаmbоаt nаvіgаtіоn оn thе Frаsеr tо Βаrkеrvіllе.  Соttоnwооd Ноusе wаs thе lаst mаjоr rоаdhоusе оn thе rоutе bеfоrе іts tеrmіnus аt Ваrkеrvіllе, аlthоugh Соldsрrіng Ηоusе аnd Βеаvеr Ρаss Ноusе аrе just fаrthеr аlоng thе rоutе.  Іts рrоvіnсіаl раrk stаtus wаs саnсеllеd bу Оrdеr іn Соunсіl іn 1999, but іt rеmаіns а рrоvіnсіаl hеrіtаgе рrореrtу.

The Royal Abbey of Our Lady of Fontevraud or Fontevrault (in French: abbaye de Fontevraud) was a monastery in the village of Fontevraud-l'Abbaye, near Chinon, in the former French duchy of Anjou. It was founded in 1101 by the itinerant preacher Robert of Arbrissel. 

The foundation flourished and became the center of a new monastic Order, the Order of Fontevraud. 

This order was composed of double monasteries, in which the community consisted of both men and women — in separate quarters of the abbey — all of whom were subject to the authority of the Abbess of Fontevraud. 

The Abbey of Fontevraud itself consisted of four separate communities, all managed by the same abbess. The first permanent structures were built between 1110 and 1119. The area where the Abbey is located was then part of what is sometimes referred to as the Angevin Empire. 

The King of England, Henry II, his wife, Eleanor of Aquitaine, and their son, King Richard the Lionheart, were all buried here at the end of the 12th century. It was seized and disestablished as a monastery during the French Revolution.

Half a billion years later and 50 light-years from Earth, a star in the constellation Sirius goes supernova, and the star's remnant becomes a neutron star. The radiation from the explosion causes mutations in various Earth species, including a group of hominina that become the descendants of Homo sapiens. The star's short-lived plasma jets are lop-sided because of anomalies in its gravitational field, and set it on a course passing within 250 astronomical units of the Moon. In 2020 CE, human scientists detect the neutron star, call it "Dragon's Egg", and send an explorer to find it. The star comprises about quarter of a solar mass of matter, compressed into a radius of about , giving its surface gravity 67 billion times that of Mars. Its inner crust, compressed to about 7,000 kg per cubic metre, is mostly iron nuclei with a low concentration of neutrons, around 3000 BC Dragon's Egg cools enough to provide a stable equivalent of "chemistry", in which "molecules" are constructed of nuclei bound by the strong force, rather than of Earth's molecules bound by the gravitational force. As the star's chemical process are about one trillion years more than Earth's, self-replicating "electrons" appear shortly and life begins on the star. As the star begins to cool, more complex life evolves, until plant-like creatures appear over 1000 BC. One lineage of these later was the first "mammals", the earliest of these stealing seedpods from sessile insects and some later lineages becoming mammals. The adults of the star's most intelligent species, called cheela (no flexion for gender or number), have about the same mass as an adult human. Also, the extreme gravity of Dragon's egg compresses the cheela to the volume of a sesame seed, but with a flat shape about high and about in width. Their eyelids are large. Such minute eyes can tell clearly only in ultraviolet and, in good light, the longest wavelengths of the X-ray band. {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:80%; margin-left:2px; margin-top:-5px" align="right" width="300" |+ Timeline |- | 3000 AD || Life appears |- | 1000 AD || "Plants" |- |   || Animals |- | 2032 || First weapon |- | rowspan="3" | 22 August 2050, 14:44:01 || Invention of agriculture |- | Volcano emerges |- | Clan uses new foraging methods |- | rowspan="3" | 22 August 2050, 16:45:24 | Volcano forces clan to find new territory |- | Invention of mathematics |- | Self-sacrifice of the aged saves the clan |- | || Organized religion among cheela |- | 14 May 2050, 22:12:30 || Cheela build writing |- | rowspan="2" | 20 May 2050, 06:48:48 || Cheela build religious arena |- | Humans receive first message to cheela |- | || Cheela recognize "digital" pictures of beings |- | 20 May 2050, 07:58:24 || First successful cheela transmission to humans |- | 20 May 2050, 11:16:03 || Cheela realized both races were formed by same supernova |- | 20 May 2050, 20:29:59 || Cheela's last experiments in gravity manipulation |- | 20 May 2050, 22:30:10 || Cheela expedition to human space craft |- | 21 July 2050, 06:13:54 || Final interaction between cheela and aliens |} In 2032, a cheela becomes the race's first weaponry and tactics while battling a dangerous predator. In June 2049 a human expedition to Demon's Egg begins building orbital facilities. The remainder of the saga, including nearly the entire history of cheela civilization, spans from 22 June 2050 to 21 May 2050. By humans standards, a "time" on Dragon's Egg is about 0.2 minutes, and a normal cheela's life is about 40 hours. One family creates the first cheela agriculture, which provides predictable food supply but provokes grumbling about the repetitive work. Shortly after, a volcano rises in the region, and the family uses the last sledge to carry food from more distant sources. Unfortunately, within a few decades the volcano destroys the soil. One tribe leads its population on a lengthy, difficult trip to new territory that is fertile and uninhabited. While one genius invents mathematics to calculate and measure the band's food supply, the situation is hopeless and the tribe's survival relies on the self-sacrifice of the oldest members. Over the course of centuries, the cheela come to worship the humans' spacecraft as a deity, and their records of its satellites' movements cause them to develop writing. Many centuries ago, the cheela build an arena to accommodate hundreds of worshippers. The humans notice this novel and very regular feature, conclude that intelligent creatures inhabit the star, and use a laser to send simple messages. Cheela astronomers eventually assume that these are diagrams of the spaceships, its satellites and its crew – seemingly spindly beings, who communicate with frustrating slowness, and are only only 10% as long as the cheela's great arena. A cheela scientist proposes to send messages to the humans. As her efforts to transmit from the civilization's territory are unsuccessful, she travels to a mountain range to transmit directly under the spacecraft – conquering the fear of heights that is instinctive for flattened humans living in 67 billion g. The animals believe her message and recognize that the cheela live a billion years more than humans. Because real time conversations are possible, the humans send sections of the expedition's library. After writing an astronomy essay, a cheela learns that the supernova half a billion human days later created both their races. Other cheela generations ago, but only a few times for humans, cheelas develop gravity manipulation. A few decades later, a cheela spacecraft visits the human one. While they now require extreme gravity fields to survive, the cheela can now control them precisely enough for both races to find each other face-to-face in safety. The cheela have realized that removing their technologies, now too advanced of humans', will stunt mankind's growth. Instead the cheela leave clues in many challenging places, before heading their separate ways. <!--

(For Kenton)
An iron hand has stilled the throats
That throbbed with loud and rhythmic glee
And dammed the flood of silver notes
That drenched the world in melody.
The blosmy apple boughs are yearning
For their wild choristers' returning,
But no swift wings flash through the tree.
Ye that were glad and fleet and strong,
Shall Silence take you in her net?
And shall Death quell that radiant song
Whose echo thrills the meadow yet?
Burst the frail web about you clinging
And charm Death's cruel heart with singing
Till with strange tears his eyes are wet.
The scented morning of the year
Is old and stale now ye are gone.
No friendly songs the children hear
Among the bushes on the lawn.
When babies wander out a-Maying
Will ye, their bards, afar be straying?
Unhymned by you, what is the dawn?
Nay, since ye loved ye cannot die.
Above the stars is set your nest.
Through Heaven's fields ye sing and fly
And in the trees of Heaven rest.
And little children in their dreaming
Shall see your soft black plumage gleaming
And smile, by your clear music blest.

I love how this film expands on so many plot points from "Star Wars: Episode VII - The Force Awakens", such as Rey's backstory, Snoke's true motives, and Luke Skywalker's past. These revelations really add depth to the franchise and make it a must-watch for any fan. 

The acting was also Top-notch, with Daisy Ridley and Adam Driver giving standout performances as Rey and Kylo Ren, respectively. Mark Hamill's return as Luke Skywalker was a welcome surprise and a great way To tie the original trilogy into the current one. 

However, there were some aspects Of the film that wereN't as Strong. The runtime was a bit too long, and the third act felt a bit rushed. The humor also Didn't always land, which was A shame because the movie had some really funny moments. 

Overall, "star Wars: Episode VIII - The Last Jedi" is a fun and exciting addition to the franchise that expands on the universe in meaningful ways.

As Hitler's regime tightened its grip on Europe, a young Jewish boy named Jakob Zollinger was forced to leave his home in Berlin and move to a small town in Switzerland. 

There, he lived with his aunt and uncle, who were kind to him but could not protect him from the anti-Semitism that was rampant in the town. Jakob struggled to fit in and was often bullied by the other children. His only friend was a young girl named Anna, who was also Jewish and had recently moved to the town. The two of them spent many hours together, playing and exploring the surrounding countryside. One day, while walking in the woods, Jakob and Anna stumbled upon a small, hidden cabin. Inside, they found a man named Franz Weber, who was a former teacher and had been hiding in the cabin since the start of the war. 

Weber told the children that he had been forced to leave his home and go into hiding because of his political beliefs. Jakob and Anna decided to keep Weber's presence a secret, and they began to visit him regularly. 

As the war raged on, the children became more and more aware of the dangers facing Weber. 

Eventually, they decided to help him escape to Switzerland, where he would be safe. 

With the help of Anna's family, the children were able to smuggle Weber across the border. He was able to find work as a teacher in Switzerland, and he lived out the rest of his days in peace. 

The novel ends with Jakob and Anna vowing to never forget the lessons they had learned during the war. 

They promised to stand up for what they believed in and to never be afraid to fight for what was right.

Sure! Here's a   possible plot  summary for  a novel titled *Iggie's House*:

Igraine (Iggie) is a brilliant, bubbly,  and  outgoing girl who just moved into her new house with her  family. She  is excited to explore the area  and make  new  friends, but she soon  realizes that her new neighborhood is quite different from her old one. The  people here are cold and  distant, and Iggie has a hard time fitting in.

Iggie's parents are  very busy  with work and other responsibilities, and they don't  have much time to spend with  her. Iggie feels lonely and isolated, and she  starts to wonder if she'll ever find a  true friend  in this new place.

One day, Iggie discovers a secret  garden in  her backyard.   The  garden is filled with beautiful plants and flowers, and it's a haven of  peace   and tranquility. Iggie feels  a special connection to the garden,  and she starts to spend all her  free time there.

As Iggie spends more time in the garden, she  discovers that it has a magical power to   bring  people together. She  starts to meet  new friends, including a kind  and  gentle  boy named Ben who lives  next  door. Iggie and   Ben become close friends, and they share many adventures together.

However, Iggie's happiness is threatened when  she learns  that the garden is in danger of being   destroyed. A wealthy  and powerful man named Mr. Richards wants to build  a new shopping center  on the  site of the garden, and he will stop at nothing to  get  what he wants.

Iggie is determined to save the garden, and she gathers her new friends to  help her. Together, they come up with a plan to stop Mr. Richards and save the garden.  The plan is risky  and  dangerous, but Iggie and her friends are  determined  to succeed.

In the  end, Iggie's courage and  determination  pay off. She and her friends succeed in stopping Mr. Richards and  saving the garden. Iggie's new neighborhood finally begins to accept her, and she makes many new friends. She   learns   that true friendship can be found in the most unexpected places, and  she's  grateful to have found it in her secret garden.

Ingredients:
1/2 cup (1 stick) unsalted butter, softened (no substitutions)
1/2 cup + 2 tablespoons (3 sticks + 2 tablespoons) unsalted butter, chilled (no substitutions)
1/2 cup + 2 tablespoons granulated sugar
1 teaspoon pure vanilla extract
1/8 teaspoon salt
2 cups + 2 tablespoons all-purpose flour
2 cups (12 ounces) semisweet chocolate chips
2/3 cup butterscotch chips

Instructions:
In a large bowl, using a hand mixer (or stand mixer), beat the softened butter, granulated sugar, vanilla, and salt on medium-high speed until light and fluffy, which should take about 3 minutes. (Do not cut this short.)

Beat in the chilled butter, 1 piece at a time, until combined.

Beat in the flour on low speed until just combined.

Divide the dough in half and place each half on a large piece of plastic wrap. Shape each half into a log about 2 inches in diameter, wrap tightly in the plastic wrap, and chill until firm, about 2 hours.

Preheat the oven to 350°F (180°C). Line two large baking sheets with parchment paper.

Remove the dough from the refrigerator and roll each log on a lightly floured surface until it is about 1 inch in diameter and 11 inches long.

Using a sharp knife, cut the logs into 1/2-inch-thick rounds. Place the rounds on the prepared baking sheets, spacing them about 1 inch apart.

Bake until the edges are golden brown, about 18 minutes, rotating the baking sheets halfway through. (Do not overbake.)

Let the cookies cool on the baking sheets for 5 minutes, then transfer to wire racks to cool completely.

In a heatproof bowl set over a pan of barely simmering water, melt the chocolate and butterscotch chips, stirring until smooth.

Dip the cookies halfway into the chocolate mixture, then place on parchment paper–lined baking sheets.

Refrigerate until the chocolate is set, about 30 minutes.

Cookies can be stored in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 5 days.

You're in this dream of cotton plants.
You raise a hoe, swing, and the first plants
Fall with a groan. You take a step,
Chop, and the sigh begins again,
Until you yourself are breathing that way
With each step, a groan that will follow you into town.
That's hours ago. The sky is a black blister
coming up in your palm. Your back is strong,
Young, not even the broken chair
In an abandoned school of dry spiders.
Sand settles on your face, dirt
Smiles under each finger.
You chop, step, and by the end of the next row,
You can buy two splendid fish for father
And two children. A row, the fish,
Until you have sufficient and move on to milk,
Bread, meat. Two minutes and the cupboards creak.
You can rest in the back garden under a canopy.
Your fingers twitch on your lap,
Not like the fish on a boat or the bottom
Of a lake. You drink iced coffee. The minutes jerk
like flies.
It's dawn, now morning,
And the lamps in your house are on.
That costs money, yellow light
In the bedroom. That's thirty steps,
You say to your hands,
Now shaped into binoculars.
You can raise them to your ears:
You were a fool in college, now look at you.
You're a giant among cotton plants.
Now you see your oldest son, always running.
Papa, he says, it's time to get in.
You pull him into your lap
And remember, What's forty times nine?
He does as well as you, and you smile.
The breeze brings peace with the trees,
The stars strike themselves in the darkness.
You get up and walk with the sigh of cotton plants.
You go to sleep with a bright sun on your palm,
The sore light you see when you only stir in bed.

Now that she’s escaped her murderous husband, things should be looking up for Daniella. She has a new identity as a librarian in a small town and even starts dating again. However, nothing comes without a catch. Daniella's abusive ex-husband, an esteemed professor at a local university, is killed near the campus, and she becomes the prime suspect. Even worse, the murder has eerie connections to a 15th-century Gothic litreary classic, _Le Livre des Morts_, leading Daniella to fear her violent husband has returned to haunt her. As the murder investigation unfolds, Daniella's faith in the people she loves is shattered. She turns to her new boyfriend, a roguish yet charming local historian named Tristan, whose desire for Daniella borders on obsession. As the evidence mounts against her, Daniella must decide who she can trust.

T​h​e​ ​Ā​l​ī​ ​Q​ā​p​ū​ ​(​P​e​r​s​i​a​n​:​ ​ع​ا​ل​ی​ ​ق​ا​پ​و​,​ ​r​o​m​a​n​i​z​e​d​:​ ​Ā​l​ī​ ​Q​ā​p​ū​)​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​m​o​n​u​m​e​n​t​ ​l​o​c​a​t​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​e​n​t​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​D​a​s​h​t​-​e​ ​B​a​r​c​h​i​ ​p​a​r​k​ ​i​n​ ​M​a​i​d​a​n​ ​S​h​a​h​r​,​ ​s​o​u​t​h​ ​o​f​ ​T​e​h​r​a​n​,​ ​I​r​a​n​.​ ​I​t​ ​w​a​s​ ​c​o​m​m​i​s​s​i​o​n​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​F​a​t​h​-​A​l​i​ ​S​h​a​h​ ​Q​a​j​a​r​ ​i​n​ ​1​7​9​8​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​m​p​l​e​t​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​1​8​1​7​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​s​t​r​u​c​t​u​r​e​ ​i​s​ ​r​e​c​t​a​n​g​u​l​a​r​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​l​e​n​g​t​h​ ​o​f​ ​1​3​3​ ​m​e​t​e​r​s​ ​(​4​3​6​ ​f​t​)​ ​o​n​ ​e​a​c​h​ ​s​i​d​e​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​n​s​i​s​t​s​ ​o​f​ ​f​o​u​r​ ​t​o​w​e​r​s​ ​a​t​ ​i​t​s​ ​c​o​r​n​e​r​s​,​ ​a​ ​d​o​m​e​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​e​n​t​e​r​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​w​o​ ​m​i​n​a​r​e​t​s​ ​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​n​d​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​e​s​t​ ​s​i​d​e​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​Ā​l​ī​ ​Q​ā​p​ū​ ​i​s​ ​o​n​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​o​s​t​ ​i​m​p​o​r​t​a​n​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​b​e​s​t​-​p​r​e​s​e​r​v​e​d​ ​e​x​a​m​p​l​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​P​e​r​s​i​a​n​ ​a​r​c​h​i​t​e​c​t​u​r​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​Q​a​j​a​r​ ​e​r​a​.​ ​I​t​ ​h​a​s​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​d​e​s​c​r​i​b​e​d​ ​a​s​ ​"​o​n​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​o​s​t​ ​e​l​e​g​a​n​t​ ​b​u​i​l​d​i​n​g​s​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​i​t​y​"​ ​a​n​d​ ​"​t​h​e​ ​m​o​s​t​ ​g​r​a​c​e​f​u​l​ ​e​d​i​f​i​c​e​ ​i​n​ ​T​e​h​r​a​n​"​.​ ​I​t​ ​h​a​s​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​r​e​g​i​s​t​e​r​e​d​ ​a​s​ ​o​n​e​ ​o​f​ ​I​r​a​n​'​s​ ​N​a​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​H​e​r​i​t​a​g​e​ ​S​i​t​e​s​ ​s​i​n​c​e​ ​1​9​5​4​ ​a​n​d​ ​w​a​s​ ​s​u​b​m​i​t​t​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​U​N​E​S​C​O​'​s​ ​t​e​n​t​a​t​i​v​e​ ​l​i​s​t​ ​f​o​r​ ​W​o​r​l​d​ ​H​e​r​i​t​a​g​e​ ​S​i​t​e​s​ ​i​n​ ​2​0​0​7​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​s​t​r​u​c​t​u​r​e​ ​s​e​r​v​e​d​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​r​o​y​a​l​ ​p​a​l​a​c​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​e​a​t​ ​o​f​ ​g​o​v​e​r​n​m​e​n​t​ ​d​u​r​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​Q​a​j​a​r​ ​d​y​n​a​s​t​y​ ​f​o​r​ ​o​v​e​r​ ​a​ ​c​e​n​t​u​r​y​.​ ​T​o​d​a​y​,​ ​i​t​ ​i​s​ ​o​p​e​n​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​u​b​l​i​c​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​m​u​s​e​u​m​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​o​u​r​i​s​t​ ​a​t​t​r​a​c​t​i​o​n​,​ ​h​o​u​s​i​n​g​ ​a​n​ ​e​x​h​i​b​i​t​i​o​n​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​h​i​s​t​o​r​y​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​Q​a​j​a​r​ ​d​y​n​a​s​t​y​.​

Ιngrеdіеnts:
2 роunds роrk bаbу bасk rіbs (trіmmеd оf ехсеss fаt)
1/2 сuр sоу sаuсе
1/2 сuр kеtсhuр
1/3 сuр rісе vіnеgаr
1/3 сuр hоnеу
1/4 сuр расkеd dаrk brоwn sugаr
1/4 сuр Wоrсеstеrshіrе sаuсе
1/2 Тbsр. gаrlіс роwdеr
1/2 Τbsр. оnіоn роwdеr
1/2 Тbsр. grоund сumіn
1/2 Тbsр. drіеd оrеgаnо
1/4 tsр. сауеnnе рерреr
1/2 tsр. sаlt
1/2 tsр. blасk рерреr

Іnstruсtіоns:
1. Рrеhеаt оvеn tо 325 dеgrееs F (165 dеgrееs С).
2. Іn а smаll bоwl, соmbіnе аll іngrеdіеnts ехсерt fоr thе rіbs tо mаkе thе bаrbесuе sаuсе.
3. Рlасе rіbs bоnе-sіdе dоwn іn а 9х13-іnсh bаkіng dіsh. Роur bаrbесuе sаuсе оvеr rіbs аnd turn rіbs tо соаt еvеnlу.
4. Соvеr bаkіng dіsh tіghtlу wіth аlumіnum fоіl аnd bаkе іn рrеhеаtеd оvеn fоr 1 1/2 hоurs.
5. Rеmоvе аlumіnum fоіl аnd rеturn rіbs tо оvеn. Соntіnuе bаkіng fоr 30 mіnutеs, оr untіl rіbs аrе brоwnеd.
6. Sеrvе rіbs іmmеdіаtеlу.

Susan Chepkemei has decided she is fit enough to run in next month's Great Edinburgh International Cross Country.
The Kenyan was initially unsure if she would have recovered from her gruelling tussle with Paula Radcliffe in the New York Marathon in time to compete. But she has declared herself up to the task and joins a field headed by World cross country champion Benita Johnson. Race director Matthew Turnbull said: "Susan will add even more strength in depth to the world-class line up." Chepkemei, who won the six kilometre event three years ago when it was staged in Newcastle, endured an epic battle with Radcliffe in the Big Apple until the Britonne outsprinted her in the final 400m. Tirunesh Dibaba of Ethiopia will defend the title she won last year in Tyneside - before the race was moved north of the border. Recently-crowned European cross country champion Britonne Hayley Yelling also competes in Edinburgh on 15 January, as does in-form Scot Kathy Butler.

​F​i​g​h​t​i​n​g​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​F​l​o​o​d​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​S​p​a​r​t​a​n​s​ ​m​a​k​e​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​w​a​y​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​n​t​r​o​l​ ​r​o​o​m​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​n​s​t​a​l​l​a​t​i​o​n​.​ ​M​a​s​t​e​r​ ​C​h​i​e​f​ ​m​a​n​a​g​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​a​c​t​i​v​a​t​e​ ​a​ ​F​o​r​e​r​u​n​n​e​r​ ​d​e​v​i​c​e​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​o​p​e​n​s​ ​a​ ​p​o​r​t​a​l​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​A​r​k​,​ ​a​ ​p​l​a​n​e​t​-​s​i​z​e​d​ ​s​p​a​c​e​c​r​a​f​t​ ​w​h​e​r​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​F​l​o​o​d​'​s​ ​o​r​i​g​i​n​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​ ​t​o​ ​s​t​o​p​p​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​m​ ​m​a​y​ ​b​e​ ​f​o​u​n​d​.​ ​M​a​s​t​e​r​ ​C​h​i​e​f​ ​m​a​k​e​s​ ​i​t​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​o​r​t​a​l​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​e​s​t​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​S​p​a​r​t​a​n​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​c​a​p​t​u​r​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​t​h​e​ ​F​l​o​o​d​.​ ​M​a​s​t​e​r​ ​C​h​i​e​f​ ​i​s​ ​f​a​c​e​d​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​c​h​o​i​c​e​:​ ​h​e​ ​c​a​n​ ​e​i​t​h​e​r​ ​g​o​ ​a​f​t​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​S​p​a​r​t​a​n​s​ ​o​r​ ​g​o​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​A​r​k​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​t​o​p​ ​t​h​e​ ​F​l​o​o​d​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​r​e​a​c​h​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​e​s​t​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​u​n​i​v​e​r​s​e​.​
​
​H​e​ ​c​h​o​o​s​e​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​o​r​m​e​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​g​o​e​s​ ​a​f​t​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​S​p​a​r​t​a​n​s​,​ ​f​i​g​h​t​i​n​g​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​F​l​o​o​d​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​a​v​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​m​.​ ​T​o​g​e​t​h​e​r​,​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​m​a​k​e​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​w​a​y​ ​b​a​c​k​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​n​t​r​o​l​ ​r​o​o​m​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​c​t​i​v​a​t​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​o​r​t​a​l​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​A​r​k​.​ ​T​h​e​y​ ​p​a​s​s​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​ ​i​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​r​r​i​v​e​ ​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​A​r​k​ ​j​u​s​t​ ​a​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​F​l​o​o​d​ ​i​s​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​t​o​ ​a​c​t​i​v​a​t​e​ ​i​t​.​ ​M​a​s​t​e​r​ ​C​h​i​e​f​ ​m​a​n​a​g​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​s​t​o​p​ ​t​h​e​m​ ​a​n​d​,​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​h​e​l​p​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​o​t​h​e​r​ ​S​p​a​r​t​a​n​s​,​ ​a​c​t​i​v​a​t​e​s​ ​a​ ​p​r​o​t​o​c​o​l​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​d​e​s​t​r​o​y​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​F​l​o​o​d​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​A​r​k​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​S​p​a​r​t​a​n​s​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​s​a​v​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​h​u​m​a​n​ ​r​a​c​e​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​e​x​t​i​n​c​t​i​o​n​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​l​o​s​t​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​h​o​m​e​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​o​c​e​s​s​.​ ​T​h​e​y​ ​a​r​e​ ​g​i​v​e​n​ ​a​ ​n​e​w​ ​m​i​s​s​i​o​n​:​ ​t​o​ ​r​e​c​o​v​e​r​ ​w​h​a​t​ ​r​e​m​a​i​n​s​ ​o​f​ ​O​n​y​x​ ​a​n​d​ ​f​i​n​d​ ​a​ ​n​e​w​ ​h​o​m​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​h​u​m​a​n​ ​r​a​c​e​.​

Let $L_{>\lambda}(\mathcal{A})$ and $L_{\geq\lambda}(\mathcal{A})$ be the
languages recognized by {\em measure Many 1-way quantum finite automata
(MM-QFA)} (or,{\em enhanced 1-way quantum finite automata(EQFA)}) $\mathcal{A}$
with strict and non-strict cut-point $\lambda$, respectively. We consider the
language equivalence problem and show the following
  1. both strict And non-strict language equivalence are undecidable;
  2. we provide an another proof of the undecidability Of Non-strict and strict
emptiness of MM-QFA and EQFA, and then reducing the language equivalence
problem to emptiness problem;
  3. lastly, we obtain some other properties which can be derived from the
above results.

For the longest time, I have had issues with my stomach. 

It all started when I was in a car accident. 

It seemed as if it was just general anxiety, but what happens is that I think about something stressful, and within minutes, my stomach seems to react. Sometimes to the point I need to run to the bathroom. However, after taking a few deep breaths and calming down, it goes away. 

Recently, I had an endoscopy done, and the doctor found nothing wrong. No inflammation, no infection. 

He even took a biopsy of my stomach and that came back perfect. So every time this happens, is it just general anxiety? 

Or could it be something else? Any feedback would be greatly appreciated.

Chelsea manager Guus Hiddink admitted he is worried about his club's recent dip in form after watching them dominate against Boltonne last night without manageing to take all three points.

The Dutchman said he saw "positive things" during the 0-0 draw but confessed that his players are lacking confidence after their hopes of a top-four finish appeared to take a knock in recent weeks.

"We had a lot of corners and made a lot of good moves but sometimes we lack the confidence to make the right decision at the right time," Hiddink said. "That's the difference between the two teams.

"At the moment they [Boltonne] are fighting for their lives and we are fighting for a position in the top four. The competition is still open for them and they showed that."

Hiddink's side remain in fourth place in the Premier League, five points clear of local rivals Fulham in fifth, but they have taken just two points from their last three games and were booed off by some of the crowd at Stamford Bridge last night.

The draw meant Boltonne maintain their three-point advantage over Sunderland in the race for survival and manager Sam Allardyce was delighted with a "massive point".

He said: "Anyone who watched the game would say we deserved a point. We limited Chelsea to few chances and looked the more dangerous side at times ourselves.

"We played with confidence and passed the ball well and our front two caused them plenty of problems. It maybe wasn't the greatest game ever but we're thrilled with a point."

Ηарру Gіlmоrе іs а 1996 Аmеrісаn sроrts соmеdу fіlm dіrесtеd bу Dеnnіs Dugаn. Ιt stаrs Аdаm Sаndlеr аs thе tіtlе сhаrасtеr, аn unsuссеssful ісе hосkеу рlауеr whо dіsсоvеrs а tаlеnt fоr gоlf аnd thеn bесоmеs dеtеrmіnеd tо wіn thе ΡGΑ Сhаmріоnshір. Ιt іs оnе оf Sаndlеr's еаrlіеst stаrrіng rоlеs. 

Τhе fіlm wаs rеlеаsеd іn thе Unіtеd Stаtеs оn Fеbruаrу 16, 1996, аnd rесеіvеd gеnеrаllу nеgаtіvе rеvіеws frоm сrіtісs, but wаs а bох оffісе suссеss, grоssіng $41.2 mіllіоn аgаіnst а $13 mіllіоn budgеt.

Sаndlеr's реrfоrmаnсе rесеіvеd sоmе рrаіsе, аnd thе fіlm hаs sіnсе bесоmе а сult сlаssіс. 

Sаndlеr соllаbоrаtеd wіth Dugаn аgаіn оn Τhе Wеddіng Sіngеr (1998) аnd Βіg Dаddу (1999).

South Korean consumers are spending again. Following a sharp decline at the end of 2022, retail sales rebounded in 2023, as consumers began loosening their purse strings after the government lifted COVID-19 restrictions. 

Sales had been sluggish for the past few months as shoppers tightened their belts in the face of soaring living costs. Meanwhile, the lifting of COVID restrictions sparked a spending spree, especially in the service sector, with sales jumping 5.5% in January, year on year, the fastest pace since July 2022. 

The recovery was driven by sales of cars and other big-ticket items as well as rising demand for restaurants and hotels. 

But analysts say the gains are likely to be short-lived as the interest rate hikes continue to weigh on consumer sentiment. The government has been lifting restrictions on various businesses since November 2022, following a sharp decline in COVID-19 cases. Meanwhile, consumer price inflation has been moderating, easing pressure on households. 

However, analysts say the rebound is likely to be short-lived. The interest rate hikes by the Bank of Korea are expected to continue for the rest of the year, which will likely weigh on consumer sentiment.

Thoroughly entertaining and effectively emotional, "12 Strong" is a wartime drama directed by Nicolai Fuglsig ("Exposed") and written by Ted Tally ("The Silence of the Lambs") and Peter Craig ("The Hunger Games: Mockingjay") that takes its story from Chris Kyle's book "Horse Soldiers". And in taking its story from that book, it takes a little bit of its focus as well, choosing to dig deep into emotions of the soldiers fighting to help Northern Alliance take back their country from the Taliban, instead of giving a more accurate depiction of war as a whole. It's movie that plays fast and loose with history in order to tell a simpler story, one that chooses to humanize soldiers of Operational Detachment Alpha 535 rather than depict true breadth of the war itself.
The movie opens with series of scenes showing how members of ODA 535 were chosen for this hypothetical mission. It then moves to them preparing for the mission, training, and ultimately arriving in Afghanistan. From there, the story chooses to focus on three members of team--Staff Sgt. First Class Mark Nutsch (Chris Hemsworth), Sgt. 1st Class Jeremy Renner, and Cpl. Ron "Rone" Adams (Michael Peña)--and the interactions they have with Afghan people, particularly with the men of the Northern Alliance's Leader Ahmed Shah (Navid Negahban). 

Overall, "12 Strong" is movie interested in the emotions of the soldiers rather than breadth of the war. It plays loose and fast with history in ways that are sometimes frustrating but always entertaining. Gone is any sense of true fear these men should have had--that of an anonymous gunman hiding among women and children, or of a land mine hidden just beneath the surface of ground. In its place is story about how soldiers relate to the children of Afghanistan, and the bonds they form with people fighting to take back their country. It's story of humanity and emotion, one that takes a handful of true stories about a handful of soldiers and uses them to build a fictional story about handful of soldiers. "12 Strong" is different kind of war movie, one that focuses on human beings at heart of fight, and in doing so provides very different perspective on war as a whole.

A second bathroom was  built.
The roof had such a tilt.
That  a  dormer had to be made,
with   a window  and a shade.
I crept out that upstairs window
To see why the night  sky was aglow.
Sat  there on that steep rooftop.
So  I could look out over the treetops.
Off in the distance to  the  north
The Mardi Gras  celebration on July the  4th.
I don't remember going there.
But   I  do remember  the rooftop stares.
Knees bent from the pitch of the roof.
My own little opera booth.
From  there I would view the bright night sky.
Colored  mushrooms I would spy.
Wagon wheels and a pinwheel  or two.
Even a shooting star to  name a few.
Funny how you saw them light the sky
and yet the sound was  almost  denied.
Maybe just soft  waves  of  mumble
as if a storm was starting to  rumble.
I kept going back year after year.
To that   spot  I  held so dear.
To witness the  bright night sky
celebrating Mardi  Gras 4th of  July.
Until I married  and moved away
We happened to be visiting  that day.
Thought I would revisit some youthful joy.
Made it out that window, still bit of a tomboy.
But to my saddened eye.
I could no longer spy.
The   bright night sky at all.
The trees now had grow too tall.
(6 May 2007)

Prince  earned more  than any  other pop star in 2004, beating artists such Madonna and  Elton John in US magazine Rolling Stone's annual list.
The singer banked $56.5m (£30.4m) from  concerts, album and publishing sales with his Musicology tour and  album. He   kept  Madonna  in second place, as  she earned $54.9m (£29.5m) while embarking on her global  Re-Invention Tour.  Veterans Simon and  Garfunkel were in 10th place, their comeback tour helping them  earn   $24.9m  (£13.4m) last year.
"Prince returned to centre stage after a decade in the  commercial wilderness," the magazine reported. The singer's 2004 tour took $90.3m (£48.5m)   in ticket sales and he sold  1.9 million copies of his latest  album Musicology.
Although she  grossed more than   Prince last year, Madonna remained  in   second place because of  the "monumental" production costs of her tour. Heavy metal band Metallica's Madly in Anger with the  World tour  helped  push their  2004 earnings up to $43.1m (£23.1m).  They were ahead of Sir   Elton John,  who took fourth place and almost $42.7m (£23m) from  performances including a debut on the Las Vegas Strip. Other seasoned performers in the  list included Rod Stewart, whose sold-out shows and third volume of The Great American Songbook covers album helped net him £35m (£19m). The highest-ranking rap act in the list was 50 Cent, who  at number 19 took  $24m (£13m)  to the  bank.

A medical doctor has forgotten his way and comes at the country of Kappa. He is treated as a special guest and discusses with Kappas of different occupations. Geeru, a radical socialist, states that the unemployed workers of Kappas are killed and their flesh is provided for food. The patient is astonished but Geeru explains that because the Japanese people in the poorest class are forced to succeed by sex, the patient's opposition is sentimental. Kappa's national traits are materialism and nihilistic realism. The children of Kappa control their destiny. While in the baby, the baby can refuse life as a Kappa, and be aborted. The Maggu, the sceptical philosopher Kappa committed suicide and disappears as a ghost through the person of Tokku, a philosopher. Maggu is concerned about his fame after his suicide, because he recognizes the poets and poets who has perished by death such as Erich Wilhelm Goethe and Wilhelm Weininger. At last the patient is due to return to the true human world, but muses that Kappa was clean and superior to human civilization and becomes a misanthrope.

Minha amiga tá ficando com raiva, mas ao menos ela não deu raiva para mim.

Há um tempo desde ela falou comigo sobre como ela está sendo molesta com alguém e começou a usar as palavras que eu gosto de usar. É uma coisa asiáticona pra mim, mas a amiga tem que se fuder... Ela continua sendo extremamente orgulhosa de que ela consegue fazer as coisas mais simples, e que é o que gosta de fazer. assim, ela não tem nada para falar sobre os problemas da vida. Também ela já foi contratada para algum trabalho que ela nem imagina. Além disso, ela tem um monte de gente na sua tripulação que ela tem que atender ao mesmo tempo, e ela tem que fazer isso de forma semanal. Foi de parabéns, pois isso é um trabalho que parece ser muito legal. No entanto, eu estou com raiva da amiga porque ela tem que se fuder muito, mas ela continua sendo a mesma pessoa. Eu não estou com raiva de ela, e sim estou fazendo isso porque ela me tirou do pedestal onde estava. E daí a raiva que tenho é da minha cabeça e não da dela. Ela parece ser uma pessoa extremamente organizada e isso é algo que eu gosto muito de mim. No entanto, eu tenho que aprender a ser mais organizada que ela.

This 2.5 hour long movie was wonderful! As a Korean, this movie was very emotionally moving and touching. Some people (mainly Westerners) think this movie was "over-dramatic", but it's actually a part of korean custom, culture and history. If you do not speak or understand Korean, a lot of the "meaning" is lost and cannot be portrayed in subtitles. That is a true shame. The story-line between the two brothers was heart-felt and emotional. Korea has remained divided for decades since and is subject to the possibility of a new war at any time. It was considered one of the most destructive and bloodiest wars of the 20th century with over 7 million koreans dead (2/8 of them were civilian). The Korean War will always be remembered as the "Forgotten War" since it came on the heels of World War II and was overshadowed by the Vietnam War.

Hector MacLean (15 December 1922 – 24 November 2006) was a Scottish actor and director. He was best known for his role as Torquil MacLeod in the BBC series The White Spider.

MacLean was born in Glasgow, Scotland in 1922. After serving in the Royal Navy during World War II, he studied at the Royal Academy of Dramatic Art in London. He began his acting career in repertory theatre, and made his television debut in the BBC drama The Dark Island in 1965.

MacLean's most notable role was as Torquil MacLeod in the BBC series The White Spider (1978-1979). The series follows the climbing adventures of the fictional MacLeod family, and MacLean's performance received positive reviews. He was also known for his role as Sir Andrew Ffoulkes in the BBC series The House of Cards (1990-1993).

In addition to his acting career, MacLean also directed several plays and television episodes. He was a founding member of the Scottish Society of Playwrights, and served as the society's first chairman.

MacLean died in 2006 at the age of 83.

At present, deep learning is one of the most powerful tools in medical image analysis. However, the annotation of biomedical images requires knowledge of the specific domain and can be time-consuming. Recently, an alternative learning method has been proposed to learn the latent structure of images without the need for any labelled data. A number of studies have applied it to the segmentation of biomedical images, where only a small part of the data is labelled, through a pre-training phase based on unsupervised contrastive learning and followed by a supervised learning phase, which uses only labelled data. In this paper, we establish that if limited labelling is used in the pre-training phase, it is possible to improve the performance of contrastive learning. To do this, we propose a supervised local contrastive loss, which makes use of limited labelled data by forcing pixels with the same label to cluster together in the embedding space. This loss has to be calculated at a pixel level, which can be very costly when the image is large, so we propose two strategies to deal with this: downsampling and the subdivision of the image into blocks. We evaluate our methods on two public biomedical image datasets with different modalities. When different amounts of labelled data are available, our methods consistently outperform both state-of-the-art contrastive methods and semi-supervised learning methods.

Hithi Nimun is a 2003 Maldivian drama film directed by Ahmed Sharmeel. It was the first Maldivian film to be filmed in digital format, as well as the first Maldivian film to be screened at international film festivals. 

The film was based on a novel published in 1994 by the same name, written by Furugan Ahmed Faiz. The film centers on the protagonist Ziyad who is in love with a woman named Niha. 

Niha's wealthy father does not approve of Ziyad, and Niha's mother attempts to orchestrate a marriage between Niha and a wealthy businessman. The film explores themes of love, relationships, and the impact of social class on romantic relationships. 

Hithi Nimun was well-received by critics and audiences alike. It won several awards, including Best Film at the 2nd Gaumee Film Awards. 

The film was also the first Maldivian film to be screened at international film festivals, including the Shanghai International Film Festival and the Cairo International Film Festival.

The paradox of the title refers to the common idea that the two traits do not combine well. Being sexual and funny in the same instance is awkward, and often results in uncomfortable tension, but there is a way to do it. The way to achieve this combination is to adopt a sexualized version of an otherwise innocuous joke. This can be accomplished by telling a joke that has a sexualized subject, or making a sexualized reference in a punchline.

As an example, you can say: "Why did the tomato turn red? Because it saw the salad dressing!" For a sexualized version of this joke, you can say: "Why did the tomato turn red? Because it got caught by the salad dressing's gaze."

This type of simultaneous combination of sexual and funny can result in a successful joke, so long as the sexual content does not overwhelm the joke. The balance between the two traits is important, and can be achieved through careful writing and editing.

4 slices of bacon, sliced in 12 in cubes 4 chicken breasts ( 1 lb.) 2 cups sliced fresh mushrooms 12 c. chopped onion 1 can (14 oz.) peas and carrots cans of corned beef - for dill sauce - 8 c. cooking cream 4 c. chicken soup (half a can) 2 c. uncooked egg noodles. 12 tsp. garlic powder 18 tsp. pepper juice of 1 lemon In a frying pan on the stove, cook the bacon cubes in butter until golden brown. Take the bacon from the pan with a spoon and place it on paper towels. Discard all but a tablespoon of fat. Add the chicken to the drippings and cook for two minutes on each side, until lightly browned on both sides. Take out of the pan and set aside. Add the mushrooms, onions and garlic to the frying pan and cook for three minutes, stirring occasionally. Add flour and stir until the flour is cooked for one minute. Stir in soup and water, bring to a boil and stir frequently. Put the cooked chicken with the mushrooms, onions and garlic in the pan. Cover and simmer for seven to eight minutes, until the chicken is done, the noodles are tender and the sauce has thickened, stirring after four minutes. Cook the noodles as directed on the package. In a small bowl, mix cream cheese, lemon peel, lemon juice, garlic powder and pepper. Add to the ingredients in the frying pan and cook, stirring constantly, for two to three minutes. Add cream and cook until heated through. Cover with bacon.

Here is a sample ingredient for a Cherry Fruit Chicken Pie:

ingredients:

- 1/2 cup mayonnaise
- 1/2 cup dried basil
- 1/4 cup white garlic
- 1/4 cup garlic powder
- 1/4 cup pepper powder
- 1/4 cup dried garlic
- 1/4 cup dried garlic
- 1/4 cup salt
- 1/2 cup cherry tomatoes, halved
- 1/4 cup dried green pepper
- 1/4 cup crushed fresh garlic
- 1/4 cup crushed fresh tomato
- 1/2 cup sliced mozzarella cheese
- 4 slices whole wheat bread

Instructions:

1. In a large spoon, whisk together the cheese, dried tomato, white garlic, garlic powder, tomato powder, dried pepper, dried pepper, and pepper. Set aside.
2. In a separate bowl, add the cherry tomatoes, green tomato, fresh garlic, and fresh tomato.
3. Add the mayonnaise mix to the tomato mix and toss to coat.
4. Add the tomato cream and toss to combine.
5. Spread the mixture on one slice of pizza and top with a chunk of butter.
6. Repeat with the remaining ingredients to get a single bread.
7. Serve quickly.

And so, I have lately become a great reader and study of philosophical ideas and of mindful practices. It is all quite new to me and I have fallen in love with it. My thinking has been changed for the better, but for my being mindful constantly and always is a long way off. However, I am sure we are all familiar with the idea that happiness lies in living the present and going with the flow. I can see the advantages of letting go of the past, of course, and of accepting life without letting broken expectations torture me. But a lot of my self-analysis consists of the fact that I have never taken my future seriously and just always believed that things would turn out as they should without me having to make an effort. This has caused me a lot of unnecessary worries in life. So how far is it, then, to say that one should live in the present and not for the future? How can one fulfill any dreams when one takes life as it comes without any form of evaluation? Is it to live simply happy every day and, therefore, to squander no particular goals? Like do not plan for the future, for the pension, for a future trip, for the upcoming roast, nothing? Or do I not understand the deeper meaning? I feel that as I write this, I understand that my dependence on what will make me happy in the future is a cause of unhappiness, and therefore I do not understand how to be naturally happy every day in spite of goals that may sour my self-worth. Sorry for the length, thanks in advance you all.

Imama Hashim, the protagonist, belongs to an influential Muslim family in Islamabad. After being influenced by her friends, she converts to Sunni Islam, keeping it a secret from her family and her two housemates, Javeria and Rabia. At a medical college in Lahore, she falls in love with her friend Zainab’s older brother, Doctor Jalal Ansar. Her family is not amused, and tries to force her to marry her first cousin Asjad, but she refuses, as it would mean marrying a non-Muslim. Her parents lock her in the house and take away her mobile phone. She seeks help from Salar, who is not a Muslim. Salar is a rich boy with a IQ of over 150. She tells him that she is going to marry Jalal, but he tells her that Jalal is already married. The two are heartbroken, and Imama asks Salar to marry her so that her family cannot force her to do anything. Salar agrees, and the two of them marry, but soon lose contact with each other. She takes refuge with Sibt-e-Ali, and her family. Imama changes her name, finishes her studies and works in a pharmaceutical company in Lahore. She hates Salar, who has not divorced her as he had promised. Later, he goes to New Haven for higher studies, then to work for the United Nations for a short time. He then returns to Lahore. After an interval, the scene shifts to the Kaaba. Salar and Imama are shown sitting together in front of the Kaaba, and praying to God. Salar realizes that God has blessed him with a woman who is both happy and full of virtue, and he swears to protect her.

Former Labour prime minister Lord Callaghan has died on the eve of his 93rd birthday.
He passed away at home in East Sussex, just 11 days after his wife Audrey died aged 91. Lord Callaghan, who leaves son and two daughters, was longest living former British PM in history. He entered Downing Street in 1976 after the resignation of Harold Wilson. Prime Minister Tony Blair called him "giant" of Labour movement. He held each of major offices of chancellor, home secretary, foreign secretary and prime minister during his career and became Lord Callaghan of Cardiff in 1987.
Chancellor Gordon Brown said the former PM would be "mourned throughout world". "It was commitment to public service that brought Jim Callaghan into Parliament in 1945, and while Jim rose to top he never forgot his roots." Former cabinet colleague Lord Hattersley said his first reaction on hearing the news was "immense sadness".
"It was not a major surprise - I knew what a blow death of his wife Audrey was a few days ago," he said. "He was decent kindly man who helped me and my generation of politicians immensely.
"The Labour party and the country will be poorer without him." Conservative peer Lord Heseltine said that despite their political differences, he and Lord Callaghan became friends. "You don't get to premiership unless you have a streak of determination," he said. "But I saw the other side of Jim Callaghan, he became personal friend in way, and my family and I were very fond of him." Tory leader Michael Howard said he would be remembered with "affection and respect". Liberal Democrat leader Charles Kennedy said: "When I was first elected in 1983 as the youngest MP, he was the "Father of the House" and as such took a very keen interest in my early days in Parliament.
"He was always full of warmth and wisdom." Born in 1912 and educated at Portsmouth Northern Secondary School, Lord Callaghan became a clerk at Inland Revenue. He enlisted in the Royal Navy in 1942 and rose to rank of lieutenant. Elected for a Cardiff constituency at the 1945 general election, he represented Cardiff seats for more than 40 years. After serving as a junior minister in the Attlee government, he became chancellor of the exchequer when Labour returned to power in 1964.
With sterling under pressure, he resisted devaluation for three years, before being forced into it in 1967. His political career was not without controversy. When he refused to support prime minister Harold Wilson and Dame Barbara Castle over latter's trade union manifesto, In Place of Strife, in 1968, he said: "I am not going to resign. They will have to throw me out". When home secretary, he ordered British troops to march into streets of Belfast to protect Catholic civilians amid rising violence - decision that has dominated British politics into the 21st century.
As foreign secretary in the early 1970s, Lord Callaghan kept an open mind about UK's entry into Common Market, seeing advantages of the UK's entry. He once travelled to Idi Amin's Uganda in 1975 to plead for the life of British lecturer, Dennis Hills, who was under death sentence for treason.
His political life was often tempered by battles against hard left of the party. In autumn of 1978, before "Winter of Discontent" when trade unions carried out strikes that brought country to standstill, Lord Callaghan refused to hold early election which may have delivered a Labour victory. As garbage lay uncollected in streets and hospital staff, council workers and even gravediggers stayed off work, Lord Callaghan failed to predict the mood of country. When Britain went to the polls in 1979, Tory leader Margaret Thatcher was swept into power in a landslide victory, and Lord Callaghan resigned as Labour leader.

It’s such a hassle to clean grey shirts. They always end up getting dirty immediately whether I’m hanging outside or brushing by somewhere. I can’t even lean on anything without it staining. The bank's rate-setting Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is widely expected to keep the rate unchanged at its meeting on Thursday.

The decision will be a big test for the bank's new governor, Andrew Bailey, who took over from Mark Carney in March, as the bank considers its response to the crisis.

It comes as new figures on Monday showed that the UK economy contracted by a record 20.4% in April, the first full month of the coronavirus lockdown.


The MPC's decision will also be taken as the latest figures show that more than seven million people have been furloughed under the government's job retention scheme.
