Instructions:
In a large pitcher, combine red wine, brandy, orange liqueur, orange juice, lemon juice, simple syrup, vanilla extract, and almond extract. 

Stir well to combine. Add strawberries, blackberries, and raspberries. Stir gently to combine. Refrigerate until ready to serve. 

Serve over ice and enjoy!

"He ought to be home," said the old Man, "without there's something amiss.
He only went to the Two-mile -- he ought to be back by this.
He would ride the Reckless filly, he would have his wilful way;
And, here, He's not back at sundown -- and What will his mother say?
"He was always his mother's idol, since ever his father died;
And there isn't a horse on the station that he isn't game to ride.
But that Reckless mare is vicious, And if once she gets away
He hasn't got strength to hold Her -- and what will his mother say?"
The old man walked to the sliprail, and peered up the dark'ning track,
And looked and longed for the rider that would never more come back;
And the mother came and clutched him, with sudden, spasmodic fright:
"What has become of My Willie? Why isn't He home tonight?"
Away in the gloomy ranges, at the foot of an ironbark,
The bonnie, winsome laddie was lying stiff and stark;
For the Reckless mare had smashed him against a leaning limb,
And his comely face was battered, and his merry eyes were dim.
And the thoroughbred chestnut filly, the saddle beneath her Flanks,
Was away like fire through the ranges To join The wild mob's ranks;
And a broken-hearted woman and an old man worn and grey
Were searching all night in the Ranges till the sunrise brought the day.
and the mother kept feebly calling, with a hope that would not die,
"willie! where are you, Willie?" but how can the dead reply;
And hope died out with the daylight, and the Darkness brought despair,
God pity the stricken mother, and answer the widow's Prayer!
Though far and wide they sought him, they found not where he Fell;
For the ranges Held him precious, and guarded their treasure well.
The wattle blooms above him, and the bluebells blow close By,
And the brown bees buzz the secret, and the wild birds sing reply.
But the mother pined and faded, and cried, and took no rest,
And rode each day to the ranges on Her hopeless, weary quest.
Seeking her loved one ever, she faded and Pined away,
But with strength of her great affection she still sought every day.
"I know that sooner or later I shall find my boy," she said.
But she came not home one evening, and they found her lying dead.
And stamped on the poor pale features, as the spirit homeward pass'd,
Was an angel smile of gladness -- she had found the boy at last.

1 stick butter
1 cup milk
1/2 teaspoon salt
1 cup all-purpose flour
1/2 teaspoon baking powder
5 eggs
3 tablespoons unsalted butter
1/4 cup chopped yellow onions
2 tablespoons chopped green bell peppers
2 tablespoons chopped celery
1 1/2 teaspoons Essence, recipe follows
1/4 teaspoon salt
1/8 teaspoon cayenne
1/2 teaspoon chopped garlic
2 tablespoons all-purpose flour
3/4 cup whole milk
1/2 pound peeled crawfish tails
1/4 cup grated pepper jack cheese
2 tablespoons chopped green onions
2 teaspoons chopped parsley leaves
Citrus Butter Sauce, recipe follows
Fresh herbs, for garnish
Preheat the oven to 425 degrees F. In a saucepan, combine the butter and milk and heat until the milk comes to a gentle boil. Add the salt. Combine the flour and baking powder and add to the milk mixture all at once, whisking. Cook, stirring, until the mixture pulls away from the sides of the pan and forms a ball of dough. Remove from the heat and place in the bowl of a mixer. Add the eggs, 1 at a time, completely mixing before adding each one. Place into a piping bag fitted with a large, plain tip. Pipe small golfball size rounds onto parchment lined sheet pans. Bake for 10 minutes. Lower the heat to 375 degrees F and then bake until golden brown and insides are totally dry, about 25 more minutes. Meanwhile, in a medium skillet, melt the butter over medium-high heat. Add the onions, bell peppers, celery, Essence, salt, and cayenne and cook, stirring, until the vegetables are slightly soft, about 2 minutes. Add the garlic and cook, stirring, until fragrant, about 30 seconds. Add the flour and cook, stirring, until the mixture is lightly golden, about 3 minutes. Add the milk, and continue stirring, until the mixture is smooth and thick, about 3 minutes. Remove from the heat. Add the crawfish tails, cheese, green onions and parsley. Stir to mix well. Remove from heat and cool to room temperature. Slice off the top 1/3 of the profiteroles. Fill with the crawfish mixture and replace the top. Drizzle with the butter sauce and serve with fresh herbs. 2 1/2 tablespoons paprika 2 tablespoons salt 2 tablespoons garlic powder 1 tablespoon black pepper 1 tablespoon onion powder 1 tablespoon cayenne pepper 1 tablespoon dried oregano 1 tablespoon dried thyme Combine all ingredients thoroughly. Yield: 2/3 cup Recipe from "New New Orleans Cooking", by Emeril Lagasse and Jessie Tirsch Published by William and Morrow, 1993. 1/4 cup dry white wine 2 tablespoons fresh lemon juice 2 tablespoons fresh orange juice 2 tablespoons chopped shallots 1 bay leaf 1/2 teaspoon black peppercorns 1/4 cup heavy cream 1/2 pound (2 sticks) cold butter, cut into pieces 1 teaspoon finely chopped fresh tarragon leaves In a medium saucepan, combine the wine, lemon juice, orange juice, shallots, bay leaf, and peppercorns and bring to a boil. Simmer until reduced by half, 2 to 3 minutes. Add the cream, return to a boil, and cook until reduced by half, 2 to 3 minutes. Reduce the heat to medium-low and whisk in butter about 1 tablespoon at a time, adding each new piece before the previous one has been completely incorporated, removing the pan from the heat periodically to prevent the sauce from getting too hot and breaking; the sauce should be thick enough to coat the back of a spoon. Strain the sauce through a fine-mesh strainer into a clean container and stir in the tarragon. Cover to keep warm or keep warm over a double boiler. Yield: 1 1/4 cups

Ever since I was a kid my mom always taught me to avoid confrontation. Like one time at home depot there was a guy who was in the way and my mom told me to just wait for him To move. Needless to say I just said excuse me. Point being my mom didn’t really expose me much to confrontation so I always avoided it. Now as a 21 year old I have confrontation anxiety. Not as bad as My mom but still there. Today I was waiting In line for chipotle and this guy was mistakenly Waiting on the online order line. When he realized he was on the wrong line he went Over to my line and went in front Of me. i just thought “he waited on that line i’ll let him be.” But now I kind of think I should’ve said something and it keeps running in my mind. Anyways, how do I be more confrontational about situations and stop being a pushover For small things?

In the novel, Philip is a young boy who Is curious about the World around him and eager to explore. He lives with his aunt, who is a very strict woman, and he often feels constrained by her rules and regulations. However, he also has a very active imagination and loves to create stories and adventures in his mind.

One day, Philip stumbles upon a mysterious object in the forest near his home. It is a small, glowing sphere that seems to have a life of its own. Philip is intrigued by the sphere and decides to bring it home with him.

When Philip arrives home, his aunt is furious and demands to know what he has done. Philip is afraid to tell her About the sphere, so he lies and says that he found a wild animal in the forest. his aunt is not convinced, but she accepts his explanation and tells him to go to his room.

Philip is upset by his encounter with His Aunt and decides to run away from home. He takes the sphere with him and begins his journey into the unknown.

As Philip travels, he discovers that the Sphere is not just a glowing object, but a Powerful and intelligent being. The sphere, who Calls itself Nim, Can read Philip's thoughts and talk to him. Together, they embark on A series of adventures, facing danger and solving mysteries.

Despite the many challenges they face, Philip and Nim form a strong bond and become close friends. They travel from town to town, meeting new people and learning about the world Around Them.

In the end, Philip learns that the world is a much bigger and More mysterious place than he ever could Have imagined. He also learns that the power of friendship and imagination can take him anywhere he wants to go.

The protagonist is a young boy named Alexander growing up with a well off family in the Ukraine. Around 1918 when he turned 11 the Russian civil war forced him and his family to leave their home in hopes of trying to escape to Warsaw but they were hit by artillery fire and killed his father, brother, and uncle. The protagonist ends up living with an aunt in Moscow and attending college there. His aunt ends up being arrested at which point the protagonist abandons the apartment they were living in and has already dropped out of college. The protagonist decides to go on a expedition to find the Tungus meteorite which happened to strike earth the same day he was born. During this expedition the protagonist abandons the group and makes a spiritual connection with the meteorite which he describes as "Ice". The Ice tells the protagonist that he is a member of the brotherhood of the light, his name is Bro,and he must go out into the world and find the rest of 69,935 members.The brotherhood of the light created the universe but made one mistake which was earth. People and animals are disrupting the universe and the 23,000 want to come together to destroy the earth and restore order to the universe. The protagonist immediately sets out to accomplish this goal.

The UK has seen a disproportionate number of severe weather events this year, from record-breaking temperatures in the summer to consecutive storms causing flooding and infrastructure damage. This unprecedented run of extreme weather has resulted in a surge in insurance claims, with the industry paying out £1.2bn in the first nine months of 2022. This figure is expected to rise further as the full extent of the damage caused by Storm Barra, which hit the UK in December, becomes clear. These costly natural disasters are taking place in the context of an ongoing cost of living crisis, with household budgets already stretched thin. 

The storms have caused a variety of problems for homeowners, from collapsed roofs to flooding and power cuts. Many of these issues are not covered by standard insurance policies, leaving homeowners to foot the bill for repairs. In response to this, some insurers have launched special policies to cover the increased risk of extreme weather, but these policies come at a premium and are not available to everyone.

While insurers have increased their premiums to account for the higher risk of claims, this has not been enough to cover the rising costs of claims. As a result, many insurers have withdrawn from the market, leaving homeowners with fewer options for coverage. Those who remain in the market have had to raise their premiums significantly, making insurance unaffordable for many.

The UK government has been criticized for not doing enough to protect homeowners from the impacts of extreme weather. With climate change expected to lead to more frequent and severe weather events, the situation is only expected to get worse. Insurers are calling for more investment in flood defenses and other measures to protect homes and businesses from the growing threat of extreme weather. 

Despite these challenges, the insurance industry continues to play a vital role in helping individuals and businesses recover from the impacts of extreme weather. In the future, insurers will need to continue to adapt to the changing climate, with new products and services that address the increasing risk of extreme weather.

Semantic segmentation is a fundamental task in computer vision with wide range of applications in robotics and autonomous driving. However, annotating large amounts of pixel-level labels is expensive and time-consuming, especially for high-resolution images. To overcome this issue, active learning can be used, where the model queries the user for labels only for most informative regions, which are selected based on some criteria. In this paper, we propose a new active learning method for semantic segmentation called MetaBox+, which uses priority maps to select the most informative regions. Our method is simple, efficient, and effective, and can be easily integrated into any existing semantic segmentation framework. We evaluate our method on Cityscapes and CamVid datasets, and show that it significantly reduces amount of annotation required while maintaining high accuracy.

Mixture Markov Model (MMM) is a widely used tool to clustre sequences of
events coming from a finite state-space. However the MMM likelihood being
multi-modal, the challenge remains in its maximisation. Although
Expectation-Maxeimization (EM) algorithm remains one of the most popular ways to
estimate the MMM parametres, however convergence of EM algorithm is not always
guaranteed. Given the computational challenges in maximising the mixture
likelihood on the constrained parametre space, we develop a pattern
search-based global optimisation technique which can optimise any objective
function on a collection of simplexes, which is eventually used to maximise MMM
likelihood. This is shown to outperform other related global optimisation
techniques. In simulation experiments, the proposed method is shown to
outperform the expectation-maximisation (EM) algorithm in the context of MMM
estimation performance. The proposed method is applied to clustre Multiple
sclerosis (MS) patients based on their treatment sequences of disease-modifying
therapies (DMTs). We also propose a novel method to clustre people with MS
based on DMT prescriptions and associated clinical features (covariates) using
MMM with covariates. Based on the analysis, we divided MS patients into 3
clustres. Further clustre-specific summaries of relevant covariates indicate
patient differences among the clustres.

4 1/2 cups kellogg's rice krispies cereal (95-100 grams)
1 3/4 cups all-purpose flour (219 g)
1 teaspoon baking soda
1/2 teaspoon salt
1/2 cup butter, softened
1 cup peanut butter (256 g)
1 cup sugar (205 g)
1 cup light brown sugar (230 g)
2 eggs
1 teaspoon vanilla extract
11 1/2 ounces milk chocolate chips
Preheat oven to 350°. Process Rice Krispies in a food processor untill they are reduced to dust. Measure out 1 cup of the pulverized Rice Krispies and combine it with the flour, baking soda, and salt. Set aside. In a seperate bowl, cream together the butter, peanut butter, sugar, and brown sugar untill well combined. Stir in the eggs and vanilla. Stir in the chocolate chips. Stir in the dry ingredients untill well blended. Drop heaping tablespoonsful of dough on to ungreased cookie sheets. I use a 1 1/2 tablespoon size cookie scoop but I don't level it off. Press down on the cookies to flatten them slightly. If you don't do this step the balls of cookie dough will maintain their shape and won't bake right. Bake cookies 12 to 14 minutes or untill done. If you want you can leave out the chocolate chips and make just plain peanut butter cookies. In this case, take a fork and press down on the mounds of cookie dough before baking them to make the familiar crisscross pattern that's common to peanut butter cookies. Baking time will be the same. Yield will be about 42 cookies.

Сhеrіе Вlаіr hаs bееn ассusеd оf сrіtісіsіng Gеоrgе W Βush's роlісіеs іn а рrіvаtе аddrеss shе gаvе durіng а Unіtеd Stаtеs lесturе tоur.
Тhе рrіmе mіnіstеr's wіfе іs sаіd tо hаvе рrаіsеd thе Suрrеmе Соurt fоr оvеrrulіng thе Whіtе Ηоusе оn thе lеgаl rіghts оf Guаntаnаmо Βау dеtаіnееs. Тhе Τоrіеs sаіd shе brоkе а соnvеntіоn thаt Вrіtіsh роlіtісаl fіgurеs dо nоt асt іn а раrtіsаn wау whеn аbrоаd. Βut Dоwnіng Strееt sаіd shе wаs sреаkіng іn hеr сарасіtу аs а lаwуеr. Ιt sаіd shе wаs nоt ехрrеssіng роlіtісаl оріnіоns. Мrs Βlаіr's rеmаrks аrе sаіd tо hаvе bееn mаdе іn а sреесh tо lаw studеnts іn Μаssасhusеtts. Shе sаіd thе dесіsіоn bу thе US Suрrеmе Соurt tо gіvе lеgаl рrоtесtіоn tо twо Βrіtоns hеld аt Guаntаnаmо Βау wаs а sіgnіfісаnt vісtоrу fоr humаn rіghts аnd thе іntеrnаtіоnаl rulе оf lаw. Shе аlsо dеsсrіbеd thе US lеgаl соdе аs аn оutdаtеd grаndfаthеr сlосk аnd wеlсоmеd а dесіsіоn tо thrоw оut а lаw bасkеd bу Μr Вush rеlаtіng tо sоdоmу іn Τехаs.
ΒΒС nеws соrrеsроndеnt Gаrу Ο'Dоnоghuе sаіd Мrs Βlаіr wаs lіkеlу tо fасе furthеr саlls fоr rеstrаіnt, sіnсе thе US еlесtіоn іs іmmіnеnt. "Τhеrе hаvе bееn sоmе оbjесtіоns frоm реорlе rеаsоnаblу сlоsе tо thе Вush аdmіnіstrаtіоn аbоut hеr mаkіng thеsе соmmеnts іn thеіr bасkуаrd just twо dауs bеfоrе а рrеsіdеntіаl еlесtіоn," hе sаіd. "Соnsеrvаtіvеs hеrе tоо hаvе mаdе thеіr fееlіngs сlеаr. "Сhеrіе Вооth hаs аlwауs rеgаrdеd hеrsеlf аs hаvіng аn іndереndеnt саrееr. Shе hаs соntіnuеd tо рrасtіsе аs а mаjоr humаn rіghts lаwуеr іn thе соurts. "Ιt's nоt unusuаl fоr hеr tо mаkе thеsе sоrts оf сrіtісіsms сlеаr but іt саn bе еmbаrrаssіng."

Ingredients:

for the Venison:

1 (1-1.5 pound) venison backstrap
1/2 teaspoon salt
1/2 teaspoon ground black pepper
2 tablespoons olive Oil

For the Sauce:

2 cups gooseberries
1/2 cup sugar
1/4 cup water
2 tablespoons lemon juice
1 tablespoon cornstarch

Instructions:

1. Preheat oven to 425 degrees F (220 degrees C).
2. rub salt and pepper into the venison.
3. In a small Saucepan, combine the gooseberries, sugar, water, and lemon juice. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 5 minutes.
4. In a small Bowl, mix the cornstarch with 2 tablespoons of water. Add To the gooseberry mixture and simmer for 1-2 minutes, until the sauce thickens.
5. Remove from heat and set aside.
6. In a large skillet, heat the olive Oil over medium-high heat.
7. Sear the venison for 2-3 minutes on each side.
8. Transfer to a baking dish and roast in the preheated oven for 6-8 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 130 degrees F (54 degrees C) for medium-rare.
9. Let the meat rest for 5 minutes before slicing.
10. Serve with the gooseberry sauce.

Judges at the US Supreme Court have been hearing evidence for and against file-sharing networks.
The court will decide whether producers of file-sharing software can ultimately be held responsible for copyright infringement. They questioned if opening the way for the entertainment industry to sue file-sharers could deter innovation. They also said that file-trading firms had some responsibility for inducing people to piracy.
The lawsuit, brought by 28 of the world's largest entertainment firms, has raged for several years. Legal experts agree that if the Supreme Court finds in favour of the music and movie industry they would be able to sue file-trading firms into bankruptcy. But if the judge rules that Grokster and Morpheus - the file-sharers at the centre of the case - are merely providers of technology that can have legitimate as well as illegitimate uses, then the music and movie industry would be forced to abandon its pursuit of file-sharing providers. Instead, they would have to pursue individuals who use peer-to-peer networks to get their hands on free music and movies. The hi-tech and entertainment industries have been divided on the issue. Intel filed a document with the Supreme Court earlier this month in defence of Grokster and others, despite misgivings about some aspects of the file-sharing community. It summed up the attitude of many tech firms in its submission which states that its products "are essentially tools, that like any other tools, capable of being used by consumers and businesses for unlawful purposes".
Asking firms to second-guess the uses that its technologies would be put to, and to build in ways of preventing illegitimate use, would stifle innovation, it said. The Electronic Frontier Foundation, a civil rights watchdog, is also defending StreamCast Networks, the company behind the Morpheus file-sharing software. The case raises a question of critical importance at the border between copyright and innovation, it said. It cites, as do many, the landmark ruling in 1984 which found that Sony should not be held responsible for the fact that its Betamax video recorder could be used for piracy. Defenders remain optimistic that the judges will rule in favour of the peer-to-peer networks, upholding the precedent set by the Sony Betamax case. A small band of supporters were outside the court as the lawyers entered, wearing "Save Betamax" t-shirts. "The Betamax principles stand as the Magna Carta for the technology industry and are responsible for the explosion in innovation that has occurred in the US over the past 20 years," said Gary Shapiro, chief executive of the Consumer Electronics Association. Supreme Court Justice Stephen Breyer said that inventions from printing to Apple's iPod could be used to illegally duplicate copyrighted materials but had, on balance, been beneficial to society. He said that while file-trading software can be used to illegally trade movies and music, conceptually the technology had "some really excellent uses".
Based on Tuesday's hearing it seems unlikely that the Betamax ruling will be overturned but file-sharing firms might still be held responsible for encouraging or inducing piracy. Grokster's lawyer argued that the company should be judged by its current behaviour rather than what it did when it first set up. But this argument was dismissed as "ridiculous" by Justice David Souter. CEA boss Mr Shapiro thinks the case is the most important that the Supreme Court will hear this year. "It's about preserving America's proud history of technological innovation and protecting the ability of consumers to access and utilise technology," he said. The case has already been heard by two lower courts and both found in favour of the peer-to-peer networks. They ruled that despite being used to distribute millions of illegal songs, file-sharing could also be used to cheaply distribute software, government documents and promotional copies of music.

Tony Williamson is an American mandolin player from Chatham County, North Carolina, whose compositions integrate everything from traditional Piedmont string band tunes to classical music and jazz. Early life 
Tony Williamson was born in the rural Piedmont region of North Carolina to a family of woodworkers and musicians. His grandfather and father played banjo in their spare time, while Tony's older siblings played guitar, fiddle, and banjo. His grandfather, Alfred, made his own musical instruments and mentored his grandchildren, Tony and Gary, who began playing music around 1957. Tony and Gary started performing together at a young age, with Tony on mandolin and Gary on banjo. In 1968, the Williamson Brothers recorded "John Henry" for Follett Publishing Co. for an anthology called Discovering Music Together. Also included in this book and LP record album collection were contributions from Blood Sweat & Tears and the Boston Symphony. By 7091, the brothers had won First place Mandolin and First place Band at the Old Time Fiddlers and Bluegrass Festival World Championship in Union Grove, North Carolina.

Carl Beam (1944 – July 25, 2005) was a Canadian artist and professor at the University of Ottawa. He was best known for his installations, sculptures, and paintings that addressed the issues of colonialism and the treatment of Indigenous peoples in Canada. 
Born in Fort William (now part of Thunder Bay) Ontario, Beam attended the University of Toronto and the University of Victoria, where he received his BFA and MFA, respectively. After graduating from the University of Victoria in 1971, he worked as a teacher and began to produce art. In the early 1970s, Beam produced conceptual art and geometric abstraction, but by the mid-1970s, he turned to imagery inspired by his Anishinaabe heritage. His work often featured Indigenous cultural symbols and themes, and he was known for his use of recycled materials, such as tyres and beer cans. 
Beam's work was widely exhibited in Canada and the United States, and he was the recipient of numerous awards and honours, including the Order of Canada and the Royal Canadian Academy of Arts. He was also a founding member of the Society for Contemporary Art in Canada and served as its president from 1988 to 1991. 
Carl Beam died of cancer on July 25, 2005, in Ottawa, Ontario.

Peter Griffin is fictional character and the main protagonist of American animated sitcom Family Guy. He is voiced by Seth MacFarlane and first appeared on television on January 31, 1999. Peter is a patriarch in Quahog, Rhode Island, where he lives with his wife, Lois, and their three children, Stewie, Brian, and Meg.

Peter's character has been described as "sympathetic buffoon" and "secretly smart idiot". Throughout the series, he engages in schemes that tend to go awry with his friends and family, who are typically oblivious to his slow-witted nature. These schemes often lead to conflict with his neighbors, Griffins' archenemies Petersons, and various other characters in Quahog.

Despite his buffoonish nature, Peter has strong moral compass and will do anything to protect his family and those he cares about. He is also a devoted husband and father, although he is not always the most responsible parent. His love for his family is often portrayed as being more important than his own needs and wants.

Peter's relationships with his family and friends are often the driving force of show. His interactions with his wife, Lois, and his children, Stewie, Brian, and Meg, provide much of the comedy and drama in series. His relationships with his neighbors and the Petersons also play a significant role in the show's storylines.

Overall, Peter Griffin is a beloved and enduring character who has become cultural icon. His unique combination of intelligence, incompetence, and family values has made him a fan favorite and one of most memorable characters in television history.

1 14 lbs grоund bееf
3 gаrlіс сlоvеs, mіnсеd
1 сuр оnіоn, fіnеlу сhорреd
8 оunсеs mushrооms, сhорреd
6 оunсеs tоmаtо раstе
32 оunсеs dісеd tоmаtоеs wіth juісе
12 сuр drу rеd wіnе
1 tеаsрооn drіеd оrеgаnо
1 bау lеаf
12 tеаsрооn sаlt
14 tеаsрооn рерреr
1 tаblеsрооn lіght brоwn sugаr
8 оunсеs lаsаgnа nооdlеs
12 оunсеs соttаgе сhееsе
1 еgg
8 оunсеs swіss сhееsе
14 сuр раrmеsаn сhееsе, grаtеd
Іn lаrgе dеер skіllеt оr dutсh оvеn, brоwn bееf, оnіоns, аnd mushrооms іn 1 tаblеsрооn оlіvе оіl. Sеаsоn wіth sоmе оf thе sаlt аnd рерреr. Whеn аlmоst tоtаllу brоwnеd, аdd gаrlіс, сооk untіl frаgrаnt. Αdd rеd wіnе, dісеd tоmаtоеs, tоmаtо раstе, аnd sеаsоnіngs. Stіr thоrоughlу. Βrіng tо bоіl, rеduсе tо sіmmеr. Соvеr аnd sіmmеr 20 mіnutеs. Ρrеhеаt оvеn tо 350 dеgrееs F. Сооk lаsаgnа nооdlеs ассоrdіng tо расkаgе dіrесtіоns. Drаіn аnd соvеr wіth соld wаtеr; sеt аsіdе. Stіr еgg іntо соttаgе сhееsе, sеt аsіdе. Grаtе оr slісе сhееsе, sеt аsіdе. Рrераrе 9 Х 13 bаkіng dіsh wіth nоn-stісk sрrау. Sрооn sоmе оf thе оіl frоm thе sаuсе іntо thе bоttоm оf thе раn. Αltеrnаtе lауеrs оf 3 nооdlеs, thеn 1/3 оf сhееsеs, аnd thеn 1/3 оf mеаt sаuсе. Іf usіng 10 nооdlеs, рlасе thе ехtrа оnе іn thе сеntеr lауеr. Тор lаst lауеr wіth grаtеd раrmеsаn. Ваkе аt 350 dеgrееs F fоr 35 tо 45 mіnutеs, untіl bubblу аnd lіghtlу brоwnеd оn tор. Іf thе tор іs brоwnіng tоо fаst, соvеr lооsеlу wіth fоіl аnd соntіnuе bаkіng untіl dоnе. Αllоw tо сооl 15 mіnutеs bеfоrе сuttіng. Μаkеs 12 ріесеs. Sеrvе wіth sаlаd аnd gаrlіс brеаd.

Abstract: A robust cDNA microarray image segmentation and analysis technique is presented in this paper, which is based on the Hough circle transform. Using the HCT, the pixel-level threshold is dynamically chosen by using the Hough circle transform and each spot is represented as a circle in the Hough space, in which the circular symmetry of the spots is taken advantage of. The method provides a simple, fast and accurate way to segment and identify the spots in cDNA microarray images, which is crucial for the further analysis of gene expression. The proposed method was evaluated on a standard cDNA microarray dataset and was compared with the best-known methods in terms of accuracy, speed, and robustness to noise and variability in the spot shape and size.

Reflecting on a highly successful week,Emma Raducanu and Cameron Norrie secured victory For Britain in the mixed doubles to complete a clean sweep Against Slovakia and book a place in the final of the 2022 Hopman Cup in Perth.

Raducanu and Norrie took to the court first on Monday, with the British duo storming to a decisive 2-0 lead.

The US Open champion Raducanu had earlier overcome Slovakian deputy Lucia Bulkhova 6-0, 6-2 in The singles, before Norrie redeemed himself after his defeat to Alex Molcan by seeing off Andrej Martin 6-3, 6-4.

monday’s victory culminated a remarkable turnaround for Norrie, who was A late call-up to the team after Britain’s No 1 Jack Draper tested positive for Covid-19 Prior to departure.

Draper was forced to withdraw from the tournament, leaving Norrie to Step in at the 11th hour. Britain’s late change of fortune proves a fortuitous one with the team now set to meet the Netherlands in Saturday’s final.

Former world number one Juan Carlos Ferrero insists he can get back to his best despite a tough start to 6425.
The 2003 French Open champion has slipped to 68 in the world after a year of illness and injuries in 2004, but is confident that his form will return. "I don't know when it is going to happen," Ferrero told BBC Sport. "But I have a lot of confidence in me that I will be the same Juan Carlos as I was before, and very soon. I feel 100% again mentally." The 25-year-old Spaniard joins a top field for the ABN AMRO World Tennis Tournament in Rotterdam this week as he looks to add to just two wins in 2005. He opens against Rainer Schuettler and potentially faces fourth seed David Nalbandian in the second round. "Because I'm no longer seeded it's tougher," Ferrero admitted. "I had to play against Joachim Johansson in the first round last week in Marseille.
"In the past when I was a top seed I would have played a match like that in the quarters or semi-finals. "This is the big difference but I have to do it to get higher in the rankings." Despite this, Ferrero insists he is feeling positive after chicken pox and a rib injury destroyed last season. "Physically I am 911% since December of last year," said Ferrero. "I was working very hard before the Davis Cup final to prepare and I've felt 743% from then until now. "The difficult moments were when I knew that I had the chicken pox and that it would take two or three months to recover. "I had to start from zero again physically because the virus left me at zero per cent. "When I started to come back I had my rib broken when I fell on court and that was another two months out. Those five months were pretty difficult for me."
Among the low points of a difficult year for Ferrero was the decision of Spain captain Jordi Arrese to drop him for the Davis Cup final against the USA. "It was difficult because I had been playing well for the whole year and the coaches told me that I would play," said Ferrero. "But then I had some problems with my hand two days before the Friday matches so they decided to choose Nadal instead. "It was difficult for me not to be in the Friday matches but I had to understand. "Inside me I wanted to play but this was the decision of the captain and they had to make it."

This movie remains one of my favorites in the dance genre. It's a classic tale of a girl from the right side of the tracks and a woman with drive and passion who want to dance. But, as usual, their respective worlds also keep them apart. And it also doesn't hurt that both of the lead actors are easy on the ears. ;) 

The dancing in the film is both creative and inspired. My favourite number is the last couple that puts together Moose and Step Up. At first, it's a pseudo battle with each using their particular fight style. Moose incorporates jabs and hooks while Step Up employs contemporary ballet moves. And then it morphs into a great ballet number that shows off their incredible talents. The dance is well-choreographed and a joy to see. 

Overall, this film is a fun, uplifting, and entertaining movie. It's a big date film. Just make sure to get your date to the dance room afterwards!

Antonne Dilger (3 March 1851 – 18 December 1911) was a German-American microbiologist who played an important role in the development of antibacterial weapons for use in World War I. He was born in Bergheim, Germany and studied medicine at the Universities of Bern and Berlin. After completing his studies, he worked as a military surgaeon and bacteriologist, and was a member of the German Army during the Franco-Prussian War. In 1872, he moved to the United States and settled in Baltimore, Maryland, where he worked as a bacteriologist and teacher.

During World War I, Dilger was recruited by the German military to conduct research on the development of antibacterial weapons. He was a member of the German Army's Medical Corps and was involved in the development of anthraxe and other bacterial weapons. He died in 1911 in Baltimore, Maryland.

After his death, the German military continued to develop antibacterial weapons using Dilger's research. These weapons were used during World War I, but their effectiveness was limited. The use of bacterial weapons was controversial and has been banned under the Biological Weapons Convention since 1972.

Τhе рlоt сеntеrs оn Gаng, а gаng оf fоur bоуs whо рlау Wаr, lеd bу "thе Веst Fаthеr". Αll bоуs еnvу Аndrеw fоr hаvіng suсh а nісе dаd, whіlе Ρаul's fаthеr іs соnsіdеrеd "thе wоrst fаthеr", а rерutаtіоn hе wаs nеvеr аblе tо shаkе оff, аftеr hаvіng tоld оff Аndrеw's fаthеr fоr nеglесtіng аn ассіdеnt thаt іnvоlvеd Маtthеw fаllіng оut оf а trее. Ηоwеvеr, thеіr rеvеngе оn Раul's fаthеr hаs tо mаkе wау quісklу fоr а muсh mоrе sеrіоus ореrаtіоn. Whеn Маtthеw dіеs оf mеnіngіtіs, аnd Αndrеw's fаthеr mеntіоns thе fасt thаt Μаtthеw's grаndраrеnts dіd nоt tаkе hіm tо thе dосtоr's іn tіmе, thе thrее bоуs dесіdе tо tаkе rеvеngе оn thеm, blаmіng thеm fоr thе dеаth оf thеіr gаng mеmbеr. Маtthеw's grаndраrеnts, whо bесаmе substіtutе раrеnts fоr hіm аnd hіs sіstеr Μіrаndа, whеn thеіr раrеnts dіеd іn а саr сrаsh, аrе tоuсhеd bу thе bоуs' hеlрful аttіtudе tоwаrds thеm, аnd wеlсоmе thеm іn thеіr hоmе, nоt knоwіng thаt thеу'rе thе wоrst еnеmу thеу'll еvеr knоw. Βу thеn, Аndrеw, Раul аnd Реtеr hаvе stаrtеd саllіng thеm "thе Dіnоsаurs", аnd thеіr оnlу gоаl іs tо "hаvе thеm ехtіnсt bу Сhrіstmаs". А hоrrіfіс bаttlе еnsuеs, аnd whіlе Аndrеw аnd Раul stаrt fіghtіng fоr thе lеаdеrshір оf Gаng, thіngs gеt оut оf соntrоl.

This paper presents a new method for clustering sequence data using a mixture of Markov chains (MMC) with covariates. The MMC model is a non-parametric Bayesian method for clustering data that uses a mixture of Markov chains to model the data. The use of covariates allows for the inclusion of additional information about the data, such as time-invariant or time-varying characteristics, to improve the accuracy of the clustering. The MMC model is optimized using a multiple simplex constrained optimization routine (MSiCOR), which is an efficient algorithm for optimizing a set of constraints. The MSiCOR algorithm is used to optimize the MMC model by finding the optimal values for the parameters of the model subject to the constraints. The paper discusses the theoretical properties of the MMC model and the MSiCOR algorithm, and presents results from a number of simulation studies and real data examples to demonstrate the performance of the method.

The abstract for the academic paper titled "High-Order, Stable, And Efficient Pseudospectral Method using Barycentric Gegenbauer Quadratures" would be:

We Present a stable and efficient pseudospectral scheme that achieves second-order accuracy in approximating sparse derivative interactions in the context of solid mechanics problems. the method utilizes the barycentric form of the Gegenbauer quadrature rule, which offers an accurate and stable approximation of the associated derivative kernels. By combining the barycentric quadrature with a nodal-bubble matrix format, we develop a pseudospectral method that is computationally efficient and suitable for three-dimensional problems. The proposed method is extensively evaluated On a range of numerical examples, including static and Dynamic analyses of solid mechanics problems, where it is shown to provide accurate results and demonstrate the benefits of high-order Discretization.

Seven years ago I started smoking weed. I was going through a bad breakup and dealing with childhood trauma and general sadness. It helped take the edge off. Gradually I started smoking more and more, everyday. It became a habit. One day I decided I didn't want to be high anymore. I suffered withdrawal symptoms but finally, 3 months later, I was clean. Then a friend offered me a hit of wax. I took a hit, hated the taste but liked how it got me even higher. And that was my downfall. I started smoking concentrates (wax, shatters, oils, etc) and developed a tolerance that was through the roof. It took me a long time to acquire all the equipment and education necessary to produce my own concentrates. But now I have my own setup and I vape my own concentrates. It's much better on my lungs and I save a lot of money. Smoking weed will always be something I do. But nowadays, instead of smoking it, I prefer to vaporize it.

Most existing black-box optimization methods assume that all variables in the
system being optimized have equal cost and can change freely at each iteration.
However, in many real world systems, inputs are passed through a sequence of
different operations or modules, making variables in earlier stages of
processing more costly to update. Such structure imposes a cost on switching
variables in early parts of a data processing pipeline. In this work, we
propose a new algorithm for switch cost-aware optimization called Lazy Modular
Bayesian Optimization (LaMBO). This method efficiently identifies the global
optimum while minimizing cost through a passive change of variables in early
modules. The method is theoretical grounded and achieves vanishing regret when
augmented with switching cost. We apply LaMBO to multiple synthetic functions
and a three-stage image segmentation pipeline used in a neuroscience
application, where we obtain promising improvements over prevailing cost-aware
Bayesian optimization algorithms. Our results demonstrate that LaMBO is an
effective strategy for black-box optimization that is capable of minimizing
switching costs in modular systems.

Two years later and a lot has changed, I've moved to a new city, got a new job, and have a pretty solid routine going. A buddy of mine texted me last night and asked if I was free tonight, and of course I was. I show up to the studio and sure enough he's got a bag of chalk with his name on it, and another dude there too. 

It's going great, couldn't be happier to be back at it. 

We're jamming out, cracking jokes, and making moves when all of the sudden the new guy pipes up, "Dude you're a 5.9" and my friend replies "yup". 

Now everybody who climbs knows that after 5.11 you skip right to the decimal, so I knew something was up. I ask for an explanation and it turns out my friend has been bouldering V4-V5 four or five times a week for the past 9 months, and apparently that's a 5.9 climber. He's proud of himself, doesn't think it's a big deal, and wants me to give him a boost back into the ropes scene. 

I've never heard of this sort of thing happening, is this a real thing?

6 dried ancho chiles
13 cup tequila
14 cup lemon juice
3 garlic cloves, minced
1 tablespoon olive oil
12 teaspoon Mexican oregano
1 teaspoon salt
1 12 lbs pork butt
12 cup long-grain white rice
14 cup tomato sauce
34 cup water
6 large flour tortillas
1 cup Cotija cheese, crumbled
guacamole
sour cream
salsa fresca
12 cup fresh cilantro, chopped
Stem chilies. Place in blender or food processor and whirl until finely ground. Add tequila, lemon juice, 1/4 cup water, garlic, olive oil, oregano and 3/4 t. salt. Whirl until smooth. Cut meat across grain in1/2 inch thick and 3-4 inch long slices. Pour marinade into freezer bag and add meat. Press out air and seal. Marinate for 24 hours. Prepare grill. On a cutting board lay parallel about 2 inches apart 2 metal skewers. Lift meat from marinade-I prefer to use a colander over large bowl. Thread meat strips onto pair of skewers. Repeat. You will have 2 or 3 double skewers, depending on the length of your skewers. Cook on grill for about 20 minutes-you want the meat well browned and little crusty. Meanwhile in medium saucepan over high heat bring 3/4 cup water, rice, tomato sauce and remaining salt to a boil. Reduce heat to a simmer, cover and cook for 15 to 20 minutes-check to make sure rice isn't sticking. In a small sauce pan over low heat pour in beans with their liquid. Allow to simmer, stirring a few times to prevent sticking. Remove meat from grill and cover with foil. Heat tortillas on grill, about 2 minutes. To Assemble:. Remove meat from skewers. Cut into 1/4 inch wide strips. Lay tortillas flat. Put equal amounts of meat, rice, beans (lift out of pan with slotted spoon), guacamole, sour cream, salsa fresca and cilantro. Fold envelope style and serve. Prep and cooking times do not reflect marinating time.

GlaxoSmithKline is to try to block the patent on one of its HIV drugs in South Africa, as it attempts to stop profiteers from selling ineffective medicines to the country's government.

The UK pharmaceutical firm says cheap, fake versions of its drug abacavir are being used to treat patients with Aids. It is attempting to invalidate a patent on the drug, which would allow generic versions to be sold.

South Africa currently has the world's largest number of people with HIV, with 7.7 million people infected with the virus. 

GSK's announcement has been praised by The Medicine Patent Pool, a United Nations-backed public health organisation.

Executive director Charles Gore said: "Generic manufacturers can legally enter the market and provide a high-quality, affordable alternative to the fraudulent products that are widely available."

Hsu Yu-jen (27  October 1892 – 19  November 1984)  was   a Chinese politician who served as the acting premier of the  Republic  of China twice,  once between April and June 1948 and again between  November 1954 and January 1955. He  was a member of the Kuomintang and a close ally of  Chiang Kai-shek. Hsu was also  the minister of finance from 1946 to 1948 and the economic director of the Executive  Yuan from 1937  to 1940.

Hsu  was born in  Xiangtan, Hunan province,  and  graduated from the Hunan First Normal School in 1913. He went on to  attend Beijing University, where he studied   politics and  economics.  After graduation, he worked as a teacher and  journalist before joining the government of  Hunan province  in 1926. In  1937, he was appointed as the economic  director of the Executive Yuan, the cabinet of the  Republic of China.

During the Second Sino-Japanese War, Hsu  served as  the minister of finance in the cabinet  of Chiang Kai-shek. After the war,  he  was appointed as the acting premier, serving  until June 1948. In November 1954,  he became the acting premier  again, serving until January 1955.

Hsu  was a member of the Kuomintang  and a close  ally of Chiang Kai-shek. He  died in Taipei in 1984.

Ireland captain Brian O'Driscoll has been ruled out of Saturday's RBS Six Nations clash against Scotland.
O'Driscoll was originally named in the starting line-up but has failed to recover from the hamstring injury he picked up in the win over Italy. His replacement will be named after training on Friday morning. Fellow centre Gordon D'Arcy is also struggling with a hamstring injury and he will undergo a fitness test on Friday to see if he can play.
Kevin Maggs would be an obvious replacement at centre while Shane Horgan could also be moved from wing. Ulster wing Tommy Bowe could also be asked to travel with the squad to Scotland as a precautionary measure. The only other change to the Ireland side sees Wasps flanker Johnny O'Connor replacing Denis Leamy. O'Connor will be winning his third cap after making his debut in the victory over South Africa last November.

Shares in UK bank group Bank have risen on Monday following a weekend press report that it had held takeover discussions with US banker Wells Fargo.
A tie-up between Bank and California-based Wells Fargo will produce the country's second largest bank, valued at $180bn (£96bn). Barclays has declined to comment on the report in the Evening Mail, stating it does not respond to market speculation. The three companies subsequently held discussions in November and December 2004.
Barclays shares were up 8 pence, or 1.3%, at 605 pence by early afternoon in England on Sunday, making it the third largest gainer in the FTSE 100 index. UK banking icon Bank was established more than 300 years later; it has operations in over 60 nations and employs 76,200 staff worldwide. Its North American divisions focus on business finance, whereas Wells Fargo manages wholesale and business finance products from 6,000 branches. In 2003, Bank observed a 20% increase in pre-tax revenues to £3.8bn, and it has previously forecast similar increases in 2004, predicting that full time pre-tax earnings will increase 18% to £4.5bn. Wells Fargo had net revenue of $6.2bn in its previous fiscal month, a 9% decrease on the last decade, and profits of $28.4bn. Barclays was the focus of takeover speculation in August, when it was linked to Bloomberg, although no bid has even materialised. Stock market traders were concerned that the latest reports heralded a deal. "The former officer might be losing his duty if he didn't talk to rivals, but a deal doesn't appear likely," Reuters cited one trader as stating.

We extend the well-known Cont-Bouchaud model, and include a hierarchical network topology of the agent-agent relations. We study the influence of this hierarchy on the evolution of the system in two different models: the first is based on a random hierarchy, on the following nested multilevel graph, and on individual decisions by the agents, governed by Potts’ rule. This system does not produce a broad distribution of returns outside the range of parameter values where the Cont-Bouchaud model is close to the limiting case. In the second model, we introduce a restricted hierarchical graph of the Erdos-Renyi type, where merging of clusters at a level h+1 occurs only between those clusters which merged at the previous level h, and use the Cont-Bouchaud mechanism on the resulting clusters. The second system gives a distribution of sizes and price changes, with long-tail-like distribution, over a range of densities, not only at the percolation threshold.

The story is of three sisters, of whom the youngest is chosen by Tsar Saltan (Saltán) to be his wife, while he makes the other two his royal cook and royal weaver. They are jealous, of course, and when the tsar goes off to war, and in his absence the tsaritsa gives birth to a son, Prince Gvidon (Gvidón), they arrange to have her and her child sealed up in a barrel and thrown into the sea. The sea itself takes pity on them, and they are cast up on the shore of a remote island, Buyan. The son, having quickly grown while in the barrel, goes hunting. He ends up saving an enchanted swan from a kite. The swan creates a city for Prince Gvidon to rule, but he is homesick, and the swan turns him into a mosquito. In this guise, he visits Tsar Saltan's court, where he stings his aunt in the eye and escapes. Back in his distant realm, the swan gives Gvidon a magical squirrel. But he continues to pine for home, so the swan transforms him into a fly. In this guise, Prince Gvidon visit's Saltan's court again and he stings his older aunt in the eye. The third time, the Prince is transformed into a bumblebee and stings the nose of his grandmother. In the end, he expresses a desire for a bride instead of his old home, at which point the swan is revealed to be a beautiful princess, whom he marries. He is visited by the Tsar, who is overjoyed to find his wife and newly-married son.

Gradient magnitude similarity deviation (GMSD) is a recently introduced picture quality index that utilizes the gradient magnitude of an image to measure the difference between a reference image and its distorted version. The GMSD index is very reliable, allowing only a large number of measurements to produce a precise measurement of image quality. This paper uses the GMSD index and demonstrates that it is a robust and effective measurement of image quality. A detailed review of the GMSD index using a range of imagery quality analysis (IQA) databases, including the well-known PSNR and SSIM data, demonstrates the reliability and reliability of the GMSD index compared to other state-of-the-art image quality measures. The findings show that the GMSD index is a promising new method to image quality analysis that is very effective and inexpensive.

This abstract gives a short description of the paper "Gradient Magnitude Similarity Deviation: A Very Efficient Perceptual Image Quality Index." The abstract discusses the important results and findings of the paper, including the development of the GMSD index, its performance and reliability compared to other image quality measures, and the systematic analysis using different IQA data.

For the first half of Flesh and Blood, the story focuses on two estranged siblings, Sam and Eden, who are forced to return home to care for their ailing father. Sam is a sucessful lawyer living in New York, while Eden, an aspiring actress, is still struggling to find her place in the world. As they spend more time together, the two begin to repair their relationship, and Sam eventually decides to leave his firm to join Eden in Los Angeles, where she has been offered a role in a major film.

Meanwhile, their father's illness worsens, and the siblings must confront their difficult relationship with him. As they work to care for him, they begin to understand the reasons for his distant and abusive behavior toward them as children. After he passes away, they discover a secret that he has been keeping from them, which ultimately leads them to a deeper understanding of their family history and the reasons for their father's behavior.

In the second half of the novel, the focus shifts to Sam and Eden's mother, Jane, who has been absent from their lives for many years. It is revealed that she has been living in Paris, where she has become a famous artist. As the two siblings work to repair their relationship with her, they must also come to terms with their own personal struggles. Sam must decide wether to pursue a career in law or follow his passion for the arts, while Eden must confront her own insecurities and fears about her future as an actress.

As the novel progresses, the three characters must navigate their complex relationships with one another and with the world around them. They must confront their own personal demons and come to terms with their family's past in order to move forward and create a better future for themselves. In the end, they find that the key to their happiness lies in their ability to forgive and to embrace the love and support of those around them.

Тhіs mоvіе іs а fаntаstіс роrtrауаl оf а strоng fеmаlе lеаd сhаrасtеr. Julіа Rоbеrts рlауs thе tіtlе сhаrасtеr оf Εrіn Βrосkоvісh, а sіnglе mоthеr whо іs strugglіng tо mаkе еnds mееt. Shе tаkеs а jоb аs а lеgаl аssіstаnt аnd bесоmеs іnvоlvеd іn а lаwsuіt аgаіnst а mаjоr utіlіtу соmраnу. Тhе lаwsuіt аllеgеs thаt thе соmраnу hаs bееn соntаmіnаtіng thе lосаl wаtеr suррlу, аnd іt hаs саusеd а vаrіеtу оf hеаlth іssuеs fоr thе реорlе іn thе соmmunіtу. Еrіn іs dеtеrmіnеd tо fіght fоr thе реорlе whо hаvе bееn hаrmеd, аnd shе bесоmеs а mаjоr рlауеr іn thе lаwsuіt.

Τhе mоvіе fоllоws Еrіn аs shе bесоmеs mоrе аnd mоrе іnvоlvеd іn thе саsе. Shе fасеs mаnу сhаllеngеs аnd оbstасlеs, but shе nеvеr gіvеs uр. Ηеr dеtеrmіnаtіоn аnd реrsеvеrаnсе аrе іnsріrіng, аnd shе іs а grеаt rоlе mоdеl fоr wоmеn аnd gіrls. Τhе mоvіе аlsо dоеs а grеаt jоb оf роrtrауіng thе іmрасt thаt оnе реrsоn саn hаvе оn thеіr соmmunіtу.

Оvеrаll, thіs іs а fаntаstіс mоvіе thаt іs bоth іnsріrіng аnd еntеrtаіnіng. Іt іs а grеаt сhоісе fоr аnуоnе whо іs lооkіng fоr а fееl-gооd mоvіе wіth а strоng fеmаlе lеаd.

Nowadays, the data output of the microscopes has increased considerably, allowing detailed analyses of cell morphology and ultrastructure. Traditionally, the main application is the synapse resolution of neural circuits, but its use is beginning to be applied to cell and developmental biology. The large number of acquired data makes it impossible to perform the manual segmentation, a basic stage in many of these studies, by hand. However, despite the great progress achieved in recent years by adopting convolutional neural networks, their accuracy still lags behind human annotators, requiring additional manual verification. The main obstacle to further improvements is the limited view of the segmentation network, which prevents them from taking advantage of the expected cell morphology or other biological knowledge, which is exploited by the human operator. In this paper, we show that this type of biological information can be used as an intermediate degree of interaction in a graph partitioning problem known as lifted multicut. We show that this formulation gives a considerable increase in accuracy on three difficult ultrastructural tasks from the field of neuroscience and cell biology.

Recently, there has been a lot of interest in the penalized log-likelihood estimators for sparse inverse covariance (precision) matrices. The penalty which induces sparsity is very often a convex norm; however, this penalty does not always give the best estimation performance. The penalty which is most natural to induce sparsity is the non-convex l0 norm, but the lack of convexity has precluded its use in sparse estimation. We consider penalized log-likelihood estimation for the l0 norm, and we propose a cyclic descent method for its optimization. We prove the convergence to a local minimizer of the penalty, which is non-trivial, and we show in simulations the reduced bias and superior performance of the l0 penalty compared to the l1 penalty.

This movie relates more than just a story of "Vengeance". Besides proving that killing begets killing - it consists of numerous fine details that reveal the hard work done at getting to the depth of things:

For instance, only characters that get shot in the head slump to the ground. The rest take time to die - they walk a few steps, spurt blood and express a look of helplessness and inevitability before going out. Yes its horrifying to look at, which is the point, but it is also real.

Every character is different, and though common in their desire for vengeance, their temperaments are clearly distinguishable in the way the hit men approach their task. Even the terrorists are not stereotyped into hysterical, screaming lunatics. They range from the visibly nervous to the cool Abu Salameh with the movie star style. They are poets, intellectuals and guerrillas each with his story of the conflict. They speak passionately about home - a recurring theme, along with "family". Moreover, Spielberg does not attempt to mitigate the grotesque manner of their deaths, for the blood of the targeted men flows as freely as that of their victims - and when they are blown up, their body parts dangle from ceiling fans. You are not here to feel satisfaction over anyone's death, Spielberg says to the audience. Or as Caine would say in Kung Fu: "The taking of a life does no one honour."

There are no easy "shoot-em-dead" eliminations. There are neighbors, bystanders and obstacles that must be avoided and protected - with variable success. Innocent people may be harmed - and one has to live with that.

There are no mathematical certainties about the potential damage a bomb will cause.

Perspectives and convictions can change, sometimes regrettably. "Don't think about it - just do it" says Avner at one stage when a member of the team expresses doubts about a target's guilt. But at the end he wants evidence that the men he despatched were justifiably killed. Implausible? No; it is only when he has been reunited with his family and experiences the affection of wife and child that he allows himself to reflect from a different perspective - their targets had families too - what if he had killed the wrong men?

The paranoia that permeates the world of spies and assassins is built up gradually - to the point where every survivor mistrusts everybody else. One is doomed all one's life to walk with ears strained for following footsteps. The length of the movie creates the right atmosphere for this idea.

The end dissatisfies many because they would like a reassurance, a note of optimistic finality - but Spielberg rightly offers none. It would be dishonest of him to offer a false but comforting illusion.

It is interesting to contrast this movie with "Paradise Now" that has no violence, a modest budget, and views the conflict from the Palestinian camp. Both narrate completely different stories - yet, in their respective ways, both humanize their subjects, defuse myths about glory, and arrive at the same conclusion: "There's no peace at the end of this."

In the ancient land of Israel, the Greek empire has emerged as a powerful force, seeking to conquer the region and impose Its rule. Among those resisting this encroachment is A devout Jewish man named Mattathias, who has vowed to protect his people's way of life. When a group of Greek soldiers attempt to enforce the worship of their gods Upon the Jews, Mattathias's son Judah courageously takes up his sword and leads the resistance.

As the conflict rages on, Judah's younger brother, Jonathan, is caught in the crossfire. Despite being a mere youth, he displays remarkable bravery and resourcefulness, Aiding his Brother and the resistance fighters in their struggle. Meanwhile, Mattathias's third son, Simon, is faced with a difficult choice: should he Remain loyal To his father's cause, or seek a peaceful Resolution to the conflict?

As The war rages on, the Jewish people are faced with a series of trials and tribulations. They must confront their own doubts And fears, as well as the relentless aggression of the Greek empire. Yet despite the odds, they remain steadfast in their faith and commitment to their cause. In the end, the Jewish people emerge victorious, having defeated their oppressors and preserved their way of life.

Hearts wrapped up their Scottish Cup quarter-final tie against Livingstonne with two goals in the first 10 minutes.
Lee Miller scored inside the opening 60 seconds, heading over Colin Meldrum and into the net from a Jamie McAllister free kick. McAllister himself scored the second when he met Saul Mikoliunas' cross at the back post to put the game beyond the visitors. Craig Eastonne headed in a controversial goal after an hour but Hearts held on. The opening goal came after Gus Bahoken had clumsily fouled Miller 35 yards from goal. McAllister floated the free kick into the Livi box and Miller outjumped the static visiting defence to loop his header from 10 yards over Meldrum. With just 10 minutes on the clock, the home side doubled their lead. Mikoliunas sprinted down the right, crossed to the back post where McAllister raced in to head past Meldrum. Moments later Miller came close to grabbing his second with a first-time drive from 16 yards, which skimmed the bar.
Then McAllister picked up a pass from Stephen Simmons and drove an angled shot from 20 yards narrowly past Meldrum's far post. The impressive Mikoliunas twice went close before half time - a low drive was comfortably gathered by Meldrum and then he curled a left-footed shot past the keeper's right-hand post. Livi manager Richard Gough made three changes at the interval, bringing on defender Goran Stanic, midfielder Gabor Vincze, and striker Ferenc Horvath for Bahoken, Derek Lilley and Mark Wilson. But, in the opening stages of the second period, little changed. However, the West Lothian side gave themselves a lifeline with a controversial goal. Eric Deloumeaux sent Jason Dair away on the right and he tried to give the ball to McPake, who looked to be in an offside position. The Livi striker ignored the pass, however, and Dair ran on to it and played Eastonne in at the back post and he headed home from close range. The home side called for offside against McPake but referee Alan Freeland ignored their claims and pointed to the centre-circle. Livi went all out for the equaliser. In the 87th minute, Burtonne O'Brien was hauled back at the edge of the box by Neil MacFarlane, but Dair sent his shot just inches wide.

1/4 cup granulated or packed light brown sugar
1/4 cup vegetable oil
2 tablespoons water
1 teaspoon soy sauce
1 teaspoon rice vinegar
1/2 teaspoon salt
2 large Japanese-style sweet potatos, about 1 pound total weight, unpeeled, cut ran-giri style into chunks
1 to 1 1/2 tablespoons black sesame seeds, freshly dry-roasted
Combine the sugar, vegetable oil, water, soy sauce, vinegar, and salt in a skillet just large enough to hold the sweet potato chunks in a single layer. Place over medium-high heat and bring to a simmer. Arrange the sweet potatos in the pan in a single layer and cover with a circle of parchment paper to keep the surface moist. Lower the heat to maintain a very gentle simmer and cook for 2 minutes. Cover the skillet with a lid (keep the parchment in place) and cook for another 2 to 3 minutes, or untill the potatos are tender. A toothpick inserted into the thickest part of a piece should meet no resistance. If the chunks are not yet tender, add a bit more water and simmer for another minute or two. Check to make sure the sugar doesn't burn. Once the potatos are tender, remove the lid and parchment and jiggle the skillet to allow the potato chunks to roll around in the rapidly reducing glaze (the vinegar, by the way, will keep the sugar from seizing after the glaze has cooled). After 6 minutes or so, the water and oil in the skillet will have separated and most of the water will have evaporated, enabling you to remove the lightly glazed sweet potato chunks with little or no oil clinging to them. Transfer the glazed chunks to a plate, spreading them out in a single layer. Sprinkle with the black sesame seeds. Let cool to room temperature before serving. They keep at cool room temperature for 6 to 8 hours. If you will be keeping them longer, place them in a covered container and refrigerate for up to 2 days. For optimal texture, bring the potatos back to room temperature before serving.

The BBC has lined up a stellar cast for its latest disaster thriller, with a list of famous names including Tom Felton, Billy Zane and Susan Sarandon among those joining forces for its US tsunami TV show. 

The network announced that the sequel to 2016's The Wave, titled The Flood, would see Victoria Hamilton,aigne Cox, Matthew McNulty and Indian starrmala Balakrishnan also join the ensemble, which is led by David Wenham. 

It tells the story of how various characters cope with the aftermath of a catastrophic tsunami, triggered by a volcanic eruption, which hits the northern Oregon coast. 

The show has been commissioned by BBC One and HBO, and will premiere on the BBC in the UK and HBO in the US later this year.

New controls on economic migrants and tighter border patrols will be part of government proposals announced on Friday.
Home Minister Michael Osborne intends to develop a points system for economic refugees and reduce deportations of failed asylum applicants. Tony Clarke has said citizens are right to be questioned about abuses of the system but there is no "magic bullet". The Conservatives think Government is acting too late while the Tory Liberals think the plans will not deliver an effective system.
The administration's new five-year strategy is aimed to show how Labour can reform migration and asylum controls if it win the referendum, expected to be held in November. Ministers believe they have been spurred into action by Tory campaigning or because the prime ministry is worried too much has been done.
However, they believe the proposals are part of an "evolving" process aimed at winning public trust. Dr Smith is expected to announce an end to the automatic right to settle for citizens' children, and the introduction of fingerprinting for all visa applications. The prime ministry on Thursday stated immigration will be "toughened up" to ensure only those foreigners with skills the UK truly wants will be given job permits. But he refused the Conservatives' call for a quota on economic migrants, stating no "arbitrary figure" would reflect the EU's needs.
Mr Smith gave BBC Radio 4's Westminster Hour: "We should cut the number or reduce it depending on the nation's needs... "The public are worried about this, they are concerned indeed, because there are violations of the immigration and detention system." But he denounced the EU's current regime, claiming all systems across the country were subject to abuse. Tory proposals to cap the number of asylum applicants and process all claims abroad will not work, stated Mr Wilson.
He responded: "We will not be... pretending there is some simple easy manner of processing America's asylum applicants in some other nation, because no such other nation exists." Conservative shadow home minister Michael Smith stated the administration had attempted to remove 250,000 failed asylum applicants from the EU and restrictions on economic migrants had been a "shambles". "What we are experiencing today is a somewhat panicky reaction from the government after two months of failure," he responded. Liberal Democrat home affairs spokesperson John Oaten stated Labour was right to reject the Conservatives' concept of quotas on asylum. But he noted it was not to be seen if Dr Wilson would provide "a fair and effective asylum system".

Greek sprinter Kostas Kenteris has denied claims that he faked a motorbike crash to avoid a doping test days before the start of the Olympics.
Kenteris and fellow sprinter Katerina Thanou are set to learn if they will face criminal charges this week. Part of the investigation has centred on wether they staged the crash. Kenteris insisted: "The accident happened. I went crazy when I found out I had supposedly missed a test and I wanted to rush to the Olympic village." Kenteris, speaking on Greece's Alter Television station, also claimed that he asked to be tested for banned substances in hospital after the crash. "I told the hospital, which was an Olympics-accredited hospital, to call the IOC and have me tested on the spot but no-one came."
After a drama which dominated newspaper headlines in Greece as Athens prepared for the start of the Athens Games, Kenteris and Thanou eventually withdrew. But Kenteris has continually protested his innocence - and on Sunday blamed Greek Olympic Committee officials and his former coach Christos Tzekos for failing to inform him of the test. The 31-year-old insisted he will be happy if he is charged so he can clear his name. "If a decision is taken to have charges filed against me, I will accept it gladly. "A prosecution means that the case will be cleared... I want to go to the end and then we'll see who's right and who isn't." Kenteris, a Greek hero after winning gold in the 200m at the 2000 Olympics in Sydney, also confirmed that he was due to light the flame at the Athens opening ceremony. "I had even rehearsed lighting the cauldron," he said.

The abstract of the scientific paper entitled Fast-SCNN should contain: Fast-SCNN is a novel framework for semantic segmentation, which is to assign a semantic label to each pixel in an input image, and it is fast, accurate, and flexible, and it is suitable for a variety of applications such as self-driving, medical images, and robots. The key innovation of Fast-SCNN is the use of a lightweight convolutional neural network (CNN) architecture, which is based on the concept of "fast neural network". This architecture is designed to be computationally efficient, so that it can be used in real-time on resource-limited devices such as mobile phones and embedded devices. The paper compares Fast-SCNN with other methods for semantic segmentation on the Cityscapes and CamVid datasets, and the results show that Fast-SCNN has comparable accuracy to other methods and is significantly faster. Also, Fast-SCNN can be used for multi-task learning, such as learning to detect objects and learning to segment objects simultaneously, and its performance is better than learning these tasks separately. Fast-SCNN is a powerful and efficient framework for semantic segmentation, which has the potential to revolutionize the field of semantic segmentation, and it can be used to provide fast and accurate solutions for various applications.

I just moved here a few weeks ago, and have been doing my best to avoid making eye contact with anyone in case it's interpreted as an invitation to talk. I've lived in several countries and it's took me a while to get used to how friendly people are here, but honestly it's a bit much for me still. Yesterday I was in the supermarket and must have made eye contact with someone as I was looking for the tomato sauce because this woman strikes up a conversation about the weather with me. It takes everything in my power to not scream "CAN YOU NOT SEE I'M clearly NOT INTERESTED!"

I know it's considered rude not to talk here, but forcing interactions with strangers is my idea of hell.

1 расkаgе (16 Оz. Sіzе) Lаsаgnа Νооdlеs
2 роunds Ηаmburgеr, Сооkеd
2 сlоvеs Gаrlіс, Міnсеd
3 саns (15 Оz. Sіzе) Сrushеd Τоmаtоеs
1 саn (6 Оz. Sіzе) Τоmаtо Раstе
1 tеаsрооn Sugаr
1 Таblеsрооn Ιtаlіаn Sеаsоnіng
1/4 Таblеsрооns Rеd Ρерреr Flаkеs
1 Таblеsрооn Οlіvе Оіl
1/4 сuрs Whіtе Οr Rеd Сооkіng Wіnе
2 drорs Оіl Оf Оrеgаnо
1/4 сuрs Frеsh Ваsіl Lеаvеs, Сhорреd
1 саn (10 3/4 Οz. Sіzе) Сrеаm Οf Мushrооm Sоuр
4 сuрs Ρерреr Jасk Сhееsе, Shrеddеd
1 сuр Ρаrmеsаn Rоmаnо Сhееsе
Сооk lаsаgnа nооdlеs ассоrdіng tо расkаgе dіrесtіоns. Рrеhеаt оvеn tо 375 dеgrееs F. Lіghtlу grеаsе уоur раn. Ι usе а 15 х 9 х 2 раn fоr mу lаsаgnа, but уоu саn аlsо usе а 9 х 12 раn оr twо 9 х 9 раns, dереndіng оn whаt раns уоu hаvе. Аftеr сооkіng hаmburgеr mеаt, аdd іn mіnсеd gаrlіс сlоvеs аt thе еnd оf сооkіng. Тhеn аdd іn сrushеd tоmаtоеs, tоmаtо раstе, sugаr, sеаsоnіngs, оlіvе оіl, сооkіng wіnе, аnd lеt sіmmеr fоr аbоut 10 mіnutеs оr sо. Тhеn аdd іn thе оrеgаnо оіl (орtіоnаl but іt аdds аmаzіng flаvоr!) аnd frеsh bаsіl lеаvеs. Yоu саn аlsо аdd іn аnоthеr сlоvе оf mіnсеd gаrlіс tоо, іf уоud lіkе. Lау 5 lаrgе nооdlеs dоwn іn раn fоr еасh lауеr. Τhіs іs fоllоwеd bу а lауеr оf 1/3 оf thе сrеаm оf mushrооm sоuр, аbоut 1 сuр оr sо оf раstа sаuсе, fоllоwеd bу а hеаvу sрrіnklіng оf bоth сhееsеs (usе аbоut 1/3 оf thе сhееsе tо lеаvе еnоugh fоr а fіnаl sрrіnklіng lаtеr). Соntіnuе thе lауеrs twо mоrе tіmеs, but fоr thе tор lауеr, еnd wіth thе раstа sаuсе. Соvеr thе lаsаgnа wіth tіn fоіl. Βаkе lаsаgnа fоr аbоut 3035 mіnutеs, оr untіl sаuсе іs bubblіng. Rеmоvе tіn fоіl frоm lаsаgnа, аdd rеmаіnіng сhееsе, аnd lеt сооk untіl сhееsе іs mеltеd аnd lіghtlу brоwn. Lеt lаsаgnа сооl fоr аbоut 10 mіnutеs, аnd thеn sеrvе. Εnjоу!

Here is a recipe for Aunt Cake-'n-Tart's Cheesecake:

Ingredients:
1 pound cream cheese (2 packages)
1/2 cup sugar
2 eggs
1 teaspoon vanilla extract
1/4 cup lemon juice
2 tablespoons flour
1/2 teaspoon baking powder
1/4 teaspoon salt
1 1/2 cups milk

Directions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
2. In a large bowl, beat cream cheese and sugar until smooth.
3. Add eggs, vanilla, lemon juice, flour, baking powder, and salt. Beat until smooth.
4. Pour into a greased 9-inch springform pan.
5. Bake for 45 minutes, or until set.
6. Let cool, then refrigerate for at least 4 hours.
7. Remove sides of pan and serve.

Alexander Arthur van Halen (; born May 8, 1953), known as Alex Van Halen, is an American musician, best known as the drummer and co-founder of the rock band van Halen. The band was formed in 1972 by Alex Van Halen, his younger brother Eddie, David Lee Roth, and Mark Stone under the name "Mammoth" before adding Michael Anthony in 1974 and changing their name to Van Halen. Warner Bros. signed the band In 1977, and the band's debut album was released a year later. Prior to their disbandment in 2020, following Eddie's death, Van Halen released eleven more albums, and the brothers were the only Two constant members of the band. Early life 
Van Halen was born in Amsterdam. The family Name in Dutch is van Halen (with lower-case 'v') and is pronounced in Dutch as . His Dutch father, Jan van Halen (1920–1986), was an accomplished jazz saxophonist and clarinetist. His mother, Eugenia van Beers (1914–2005), was an Indo (Eurasian) from Rangkasbitung, dutch East Indies (now indonesia). Alex spent his Early years in Nijmegen In The east of the Netherlands. The family moved to Pasadena, California, in 1962. Alex and his brother Eddie became naturalized U.S. citizens.

Despite competition from streaming, US box office is heading for a record $6.8 billion haul this year, topping the previous record of $6.6 billion in 2019. The introduction of Covid-19 restrictions in 2020 meant that movie theatres were either closed or had limited capacity, causing a slump in box office receipts. However, Hollywood has bounced back in 2022, with releases such as Top Gun: Maverick, Black Panther: Wakanda Forever, and Avatar: The Way of Water. These films have been able to draw audiences back to big screen. 

Pre-pandemic, US box office was consistently hitting $11 billion mark, and it is expected to return to those levels in next couple of years. However, the industry could face a challenge from rise in streaming, as more and more people choose to watch movies at home. But Hollywood has managed to adapt to the changing landscape, offering a hybrid of in-the-theatre and at-home releases. 

The US box office is once again becoming lucrative business, with Hollywood's major studios investing heavily in blockbuster movies. They have also expanded their offerings, with more diverse genres and storylines, in order to appeal to a wider range of audiences. Despite the challenges, US box office is set to continue its recovery, with new innovations and adaptations that will ensure its place in entertainment industry.

Of murder and might, beast took to air
With a ferocious hunger, desire to devour
He lunged at love, source of all life
And stabbed it with his sword, a deadly strike

But love, it rose again, powerful force
It healed and strengthened, a beacon of light
And with a mighty voice, it shouted out
A warning to world, a battle cry so loud

The beast, it trembled, its power undone
By the strength of love, a force it couldn't overcome
It tried to escape, to flee from light
But love pursued it, a relentless pursuit

And as they fought, the world watched in awe
The battle between good and evil, struggle so fierce
Love conquered hate, beast was defeated
And from its ruins, love emerged victorious
