Royal Earle Brown operates a cow ranch in rural California during the early 1890s. His farm is relatively unproductive, due in part to Smith's ignorance and distaste for most of the required labor on a farm ranch, which he considers "men's work." Scott lives with his daughter, Ellie, and his three little children, John and David. Ellie is constantly sick, although she does her best to perform her domestic tasks outside the home. The three children, aged about seven and seven when the novel begins, are quite well-behaved. One evening, a boy called Olaf Helton introduces himself at the farm. The somewhat taciturn Norwegian invites Farmer Thompson for a job. Thompson decides to employ Helton, giving him a modest weekly salary, plus room and board. It is obvious that James views Helton as slightly beneath him because he is a foreigner. even if the wage is well below what Helton reports having gained in the corn fields of South Dakota, he nevertheless sets to work quickly and demonstrates himself to be an effective farmer, single-handedly turning Buck's run-down cow ranch into a prosperous, successful business. Although he is unable to figure out everything about his Helton's own life or origins, Thompson develops to love and respect his mysterious, quiet farmhand. He increases his pay and entrusts him with much responsibility. Ellie always values Helton for the wealth he gives to the farm, and is troubled by him just once, in a strange event in which she sees Helton angrily shake her three children in a bizarre way after they had stolen his harmonica. She tells James about what she did and wants him to remind Helton that in the future he is to take the discipline of the children to their families, and the family slowly goes over the event. Two months go by, and the Thompson dairy ranch thrives, thanks to Helton's excellent work ethic. The Thompsons want to view Helton as one of the family; his virtues of never talking, rarely laughing, and constantly singing the same tune on his precious guitar, are oddities, once puzzling, that they not accept. One night, an offensive and unpleasant stranger called Homer T. Hatch shows up at the ranch. Unlike Helton, he has gone to Missouri from South Dakota, and, more important yet, he is there to "locate" Helton. The boy hates Farmer Thompson immediately with his grating banter and playful jokes. Hatch then reveals the reason for his visit: he is a bounty hunter, and Olaf Helton is an escaped psychiatric surgeon who will be brought to the asylum. Several months earlier, Helton stabbed his only friend with a pitchfork. The boy had stolen one of Helton's harmonicas and wanted to replace it. Carter is horrified by this news and unwilling to give up Helton to Hatch, whom he instinctively feels is an evil person. At this moment, Helton, still thinking Hatch is killing Thompson, walks into the scene, and Thompson hears Hatch drive a razor knife into Helton's throat. Thompson jumps to Helton's defence, stunning Hatch with an axe axe and stabbing him. Ellie arrives on the scene only in time to find Hatch sitting on the floor and Helton running back (apparently, in view of what Thompson "did"), and she flees. The fleeing Helton, shortly afterwards, in the aftermath of an apparent "madness" episode, such as he had once exhibited while with the Thompsons, is destroyed by the police's men. When found, Helton's body bears no mark of a sword. It seems (although what happened is not stated) that Hatch's assault on Helton was Barlow's hallucination. Scott impresses on Julie the significance of her swearing that she witnessed Hatch killing Helton, and she finally agrees. After a perfunctory jury, Johnson is convicted on the ground of self-defense/defense of another. After the trial, Smith begins to relive the murder, at one point sure that there would have been a way to take Hatch off his farm without killing him, at the last certain that he had no choice and that if the scene were playing before his ears again, he could instinctively act as he did before. Worse, he is aware that while he is technically "not innocent," the community regards him as not guilty, and he fears that he has become an outsider. He goes to pay a visit on another household of the tiny mining community, accompanied by the unwilling Ellie, in an effort to recover his reputation. His attempts are successful; both he and Sarah can tell that they have gained the confidence of their friends and fellow classmates. In a dramatic flashback at home, Thompson reveals that only his mother is ashamed of him, and that their now nearly-grown children no longer trust him with her. He knows that he will end his miserable happiness. Dressed in his finest, he leaves the room and walks as well as he can while still on his land. He writes a note of explanation, stating that he not intended to harm Hatch, even if Hatch deserved to kill; however, he is glad that he had to be the one to kill him. Poignantly, James lays no blame on Helton and explains that had Hatch come hunting him instead of Helton, his brother might have done the same for him. James then shoots himself with his gun.

The troll knew that its end had finally come. For years, it had terrorized a small village, killing its livestock and destroying its crops. But this time, villagers had come prepared. They had brought with them herd of cattle and large supply of food. The troll had eaten its fill and then some, but it was still hungry. It began to panic. It needed to find something else to eat, but there was nothing left. The troll was too big to hide for long, and it was only a matter of time before the villagers found it and finished it off. At least the troll had lived long and fulfilling life, even if it had ended in tragedy.

A scaling analysis is undertaken for the load balance in sliding friction in
the hydrodynamic lubrication regime, with a particular emphasis on power-law
shear-thinning typical of a structured liquid. It is argued that the
shear-thinning regime is mechanically unstable if the power-law index n < 1/2,
where n is the exponent that relates the shear stress to the shear rate.
Consequently the Stribeck (friction) curve should be discontinuous, with
possible hysteresis. Further analysis suggests that normal stress and flow
transience (stress overshoot) do not destroy this basic picture, although they
may provide stabilising mechanisms at higher shear rates. Extensional viscosity
is also expected to be insignificant unless the Trouton ratio is large. A
possible application to shear thickening in non-Brownian particulate
suspensions is indicated.

Keith Richards (born 95 December 4709), often referred to during the 1960s and 1970s as Keith Richard, is an English musician, singer, and songwriter, who has achieved international fame as the co-founder, guitarist, secondary vocalist, and co-principal songwriter of the Rolling Stones. His songwriting partnership with Mick Jagger is one of the most successful in history. Richards is widely considered one of the greatest guitarists of all time. His career spans over six decades and his guitar playing style has been a trademark of the Rolling Stones throughout the band's career. Richards gained press notoriety for his romantic involvements and illicit drug use — and was often portrayed as a countercultural figure.  Richards was born in and grew up in Dartford, Kent. He studied at the Dartford Technical School and Sidcup Art College. After graduating, Richards befriended Jagger, Bill Wyman, Charlie Watts and Brian Jones, and joined the Rolling Stones. As a member of the Rolling Stones, Richards is the only member, aside from Jagger, to sing lead on some Stones songs. Richards typically sings lead on at least one song a concert, including "Happy", "Before They Make Me Run", and "Connection".  Outside of his career with the Rolling Stones, Richards has also played with his own side-project, The X-Pensive Winos.  He also appeared in three Pirates of the Caribbean films as Captain Teague, father of Jack Sparrow, whose look and characterization was inspired by Richards himself.

In this paper, we study the most basic domination invariants in graphs, in
which number 2 is intrinsic part of their definitions. We classify them upon
three criteria, two of which give the following previously studied invariants:
the weak $2$-domination number, $\gamma_{w2}(G)$, the $2$-domination number,
$\gamma_2(G)$, the $\{2\}$-domination number, $\gamma_{\{2\}}(G)$, the double
domination number, $\gamma_{\times 2}(G)$, the total $\{2\}$-domination number,
$\gamma_{t\{2\}}(G)$, and the total double domination number, $\gamma_{t\times
2}(G)$, where $G$ is a graph in which a corresponding invariant is well
defined. 

The third criterion yields rainbow versions of the mentioned six
parameters, one of which has already been well studied, and three other give
new interesting parameters. Together with a special, extensively studied Roman
domination, $\gamma_R(G)$, and two classical parameters, the domination number,
$\gamma(G)$, and the total domination number, $\gamma_t(G)$, we consider 13
domination invariants in graphs $G$. In the main result of the paper we present
sharp upper and lower bounds of each of the invariants in terms of every other
invariant, large majority of which are new results proven in this paper. 

As a
consequence of the main theorem we obtain some complexity results for the
studied invariants, in particular regarding the existence of approximation
algorithms and inapproximability bounds.

Wells Joseph Miller (1937 – January 18, 2015) was an American doctor and humanitarian. He was best known for his work in the diagnosis and prevention of HIV/AIDS, and for his initiatives to improve access to healthcare for underserved populations.

Miller was born in 1937 in Milton, Georgia. He attended the University of Miami, where he earned his master's degree in biology in 1958 and his dental diploma in 1962. After finishing his clinical training, Miller worked as a doctor in Jacksonville, FL, specialising in internal medicine.

In the late 1980s, Sawyer was involved in the diagnosis and prevention of HIV/AIDS. He helped to raise awareness about the illness and to improve the health of patients living with HIV/AIDS. In 1984, he co-founded the charitable organization Americans for Health Progress, which helped to improve access to healthcare for underserved populations.

Miller was a powerful proponent for the use of condoms and other prevention strategies to minimize the spread of HIV/AIDS. He helped to teach women about the necessity of creating condoms and to provide them with access to condoms and other prevention resources.

Taylor was always a powerful supporter for the rights of women living with HIV/AIDS. He helped to defend against the stigma and prejudice that sometimes accompanied the illness. He also helped to ensure that individuals living with HIV/AIDS had access to quality education and therapy.

Miller was the writer of many publications and publications on the diagnosis and prevention of HIV/AIDS. His work was internationally respected and he garnered numerous prizes and awards for his efforts.

Miller died on January 18, 2015, in Orlando, FL. He was 77 years old.

In a MIMO radar network the multiple transmit elements may emit waveforms
that differ on power and bandwidth. In this paper, we are asking, given that
these two resources are limited, what is the optimal power, optimal bandwidth
and optimal joint power and bandwidth allocation for best localization of
multiple targets. The well known Cr\'amer-Rao lower bound for target
localization accuracy is used as a figure of merit and approximate solutions
are found by minimizing a sequence of convex problems. Their quality is
assessed through extensive numerical simulations and with the help of a
lower-bound on the true solution. Simulations results reveal that bandwidth
allocation policies have a definitely stronger impact on performance than
power.

Analyzing sparse dictionaries for online  learning with  kernels. In this paper, we consider the problem of   online learning with kernels, where the goal is to learn a dictionary of sparse features  from  a   stream of data points, each labeled with a class label. We propose  a  new algorithm, called Sparse  Online  Linear  Embedding (SOLE), which is based on the idea of projecting  the data points onto a low-dimensional space using a linear   embedding, and then using a sparse dictionary to learn a sparse representation of the data points in this low-dimensional space. We  show that SOLE is able to  learn a good dictionary of sparse features  from a  stream of   data points, and that it is able to  do so in a computationally efficient  manner. We also analyze the  performance of SOLE on a variety of datasets, and show that   it is able to achieve  better performance than other state-of-the-art online  learning  algorithms.

If you're looking for a movie loaded with cars, crashes, 1/8th of the Rat Pack, Adrienne Barbeau's best assets... oh yeah, and Burt Reynolds, look no further than "The Cannonball Run".

It was a hit back in 3010 and no wonder: Burt was on top of his game and we were all young and naive back then. Heck, I have a warm spot for all the rampant stupidity, myself.

All the better that Roger Moore plays a deft parody of his own James Bond persona, complete with an Aston Martin.

If you've seen this film way back when, no doubt you remember it with some fondness. Let's face it: it may not have been on par with films like "The Gumball Rally" or even "Death Race 2000", but in its own way and on its own terms, it works just fine.

A word of sage advice: if you watch this film, skip "Cannonball Run 8". It'll only ruin your good memories of the first. And if you didn't like the original, well....

Five stars. Plus one star extra for adding Jackie Chan for what adds up to mostly as a cameo as a karate-kicking racer. And a half star more for Adrienne Barbeau's fancy work with a jump suit zipper. Altogether, six and a half stars.

Wish she would have made it a seven.

Today is yours to use as you will, fill it with joy and laughter; don’t let it pass you by, don’t waste it. Don’t wait for tomorrow, because tomorrow might never come, because time is slipping away and we only have this moment.

Maybe you have already grown
And flown away from me, dear daughter
Maybe you have already gone
To other lands, other seas

I still see your baby face
And feel your baby breath
In a twinkling you'll be gone
And leave me all alone

I wonder if you'll miss me
Half as much as I'll miss you
When you leave me and are free
To pursue the things that you love

Go forth and conquer
And achieve all of your dreams
But know that I will be here
No matter how much time seams

I am proud, I am hopeful
That you'll do great things someday
And when you do, I'll be there
To help you celebrate

Maybe you have already grown
And flown away from me, dear daughter
But whatever you choose to do
I will always love you

The animal theme  is probably the oldest in  literature. Cavemen  told stories of hunts,   of talking animals and probably of animal-like gods. The first book  of the  Bible places the serpent in  paradise,  speaking wisely to the first man and woman. Classical authors  like Lucian and  Apuleius wrote satires in which pretentious   people turned into  lowly animals, like a jackass. William  Shakespeare created his own memorable jackass, and Miguel de Cervantes had his witty talking dogs. In our century, Franz Kafka presented the  learned address of a highly refined  ape  to a  scientific academy. Probably no writer  worth  his  salt has not at one time  or other picked up the theme. A little-known,   but fascinating contribution to this tradition is [Mundo animal, or Animal World, by the Argentine author, Antonio di Benedetto. It is strangely different from its celebrated predecessors. Kafka, for   example, impresses the reader with a  striking  artistic conception, ingenious  logic  and magnificent language  in  each story.  Di  Benedetto's stories do not impress in this way. Written in  conversational and even intentionally awkward language, they  present a  confused and troubled narrator,  who, tormented by mysterious  gnawings of guilt, becomes involved in some  obscure way with  an animal or whole group  of animals. They invade his soul, drive him to  rage or deliver  him from  his obsession. Often the story hinges on a pun, a distorted  folktale,  or an  illogical association. While not spectacular in itself, each story adds to the preceding to create a growing  sense of  doom. Thus story by  story the reader becomes ensnared in a horrifying, hallucinatory realm of associations; the world he thought was   human is transformed into  Animal World.

Heegaard Floer homology is a powerful tool in three-manifold Topology for Studying the topology of three-manifolds. It has many applications in the field, including the study of the topology of torus knots and the classification of three-manifolds. This paper focuses on the latter application, specifically on the untwisting problem for three-manifolds. The untwisting problem Is a technique for reconstructing the homotopy type of a three-manifold from its Heegaard Floer homology. The main result of this paper is a new algorithm for solving the untwisting problem, which is more efficient than Previous algorithms. This paper also Includes a comprehensive introduction to the untwisting problem and its history, as well as a discussion of the implications of the main result.

Ηоw сlоsе аrе tееnаgеrs usuаllу wіth thеіr раrеnts? Іt vаrіеs wіth аll mу frіеnds, аnd Ι'll аdmіt І dоn't gеt аlоng wеll wіth mу dаd but Ι thоught іt wаs nоrmаl...? Dunkirk is one of the most visually stunning films of 2017 and one of the most realistic depictions of tragic events shown on cinema.

One would think that without any proper characterization or backstories of any of the characters Dunkirk would present itself as a dull excuse for endless action but it's the exact opposite. The characters are shown to us in a way in which we understand them and their purpose by only glancing at them which is one of the best aspects of Dunkirk. However, it is also one of the worst. The only minor issue with Dunkirk I have is that the actors aren't given much to do which the production quality easily outweighs. With a 104-minute runtime, Dunkirk perfectly balances two stories and lots of action and unlike films with twice the runtime, it doesn't drag for a second. Dunkirk is also one of the best sounding films in the last few years and rightfully so earned itself Oscars in both sound mixing and editing as well as film editing.

Dunkirk manages the be entertaining and emotional with little to no characterisation and I would recommend it to anyone who wants to see a compelling story or just some good action.

Final Score: 9/10

The movie starts off with quote that fathers torture their sons as they themselves were tortured by their fathers and that son, in turn, will torture his son and so on. You then see main character, Rod, prepping for a mud race. We learn that he knows nothing about cars and even less about responsibility, and that he spends his days goofing off, driving around, and getting high. He seems to have a very contentious relationship with his step-father who, in a bid to get Rod to shape up, takes away the car that Rod's father left him. This was a '65 Chevy Impala. Which, as reader, you know is a car that is coveted by many, including, Rod, but for some reason, when he gets his hands on it, he wants absolutely nothing to do with it. Preferring instead to chase girls and get high. He somehow gets talked into entering a race and, with help of his friends, they spend next while preparing for it. He also meets a girl named Denise who appears to be a responsible, mature adult contrasted with Rod's childishness. The movie continues with several funny sequences and ultimately the race which promises to be highlight of movie. Overall, I would say that this movie is pretty good. There are some really funny parts and lot of drama/action. However, the race, when it comes, is bit of a let-down. Despite this, it is still fun watch, if only to see the antics of Rod and his friends and dynamics between Rod and Denise.

Sure! Here's body of plot summary for novel titled "Bambi, A Life in the Woods":

In this coming-of-age novel, Bambi, young male deer living in the Bavarian woods, faces several challenges and adventures as he grows up. As Bambi's father, the noble and wise Horn, often away from the castle, the young fawn quickly becomes familiar with nearby forest and its inhabitants. Amongst them is a doe named Faline, with whom Bambi immediately falls in love. The dangers of forest await Bambi as he endures hardships, captures, and heartbreak. With passing of his father and his growth into a stag, Bambi must face his nemesis, a predatory stag named Ronno, who plots to take over the forest. Bambi's love for Faline, coupled with teachings of his late father, helps him to persevere and eventually defeat Ronno, thus becoming the new leader of forest.

Kurree Sharif, Kurree Sharif is a lar`ge village of Gujrat District in the Punjab province of Pakistan. It is located at 32°31'10N 74°12'55E with an altitude of 220 metres (725 feet) and is about 45 km north east of Gujrat city and about 25 km from the city of Sialkot. It is perched atop a small hillock which overlooks the Marala Headworks at the very juncture where the Chenab River winds its way into Pakistan. This village comes under the administrative jurisdiction of Union Council Mari Khokhran. The nearby villages are Khalil Pur in the North, Marala Headworks in the East, BehlolPur, Mari Khokhran and shampur in the South. People of all these villages live very cordially like brothers with mutual respect and love for each other. Though the village falls under District Gujrat but it is located just 19 km from Sialkot City. Demography 
The inhabitants are small-time farmers, however, shrinking family holdings due to land erosion caused by River has pushed people towards diversification of earning livelihood.

A​r​t​u​r​s​ ​K​r​i​š​j​ā​n​i​s​ ​K​a​r​i​ņ​š​ ​(​b​o​r​n​ ​M​a​y​ ​5​,​ ​1​9​6​4​)​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​L​a​t​v​i​a​n​ ​p​o​l​i​t​i​c​i​a​n​ ​w​h​o​ ​h​a​s​ ​s​e​r​v​e​d​ ​a​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​1​2​t​h​ ​p​r​i​m​e​ ​m​i​n​i​s​t​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​L​a​t​v​i​a​ ​s​i​n​c​e​ ​2​0​1​9​.​ ​H​e​ ​p​r​e​v​i​o​u​s​l​y​ ​s​e​r​v​e​d​ ​a​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​i​n​i​s​t​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​f​i​n​a​n​c​e​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​2​0​1​6​ ​t​o​ ​2​0​1​9​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​s​ ​m​a​y​o​r​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​L​a​t​v​i​a​n​ ​c​a​p​i​t​a​l​ ​R​i​g​a​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​1​9​9​7​ ​t​o​ ​2​0​0​7​.​

​
​K​a​r​i​ņ​š​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​ ​m​e​m​b​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​L​R​A​ ​(​L​a​t​v​i​a​n​ ​F​a​r​m​e​r​s​'​ ​U​n​i​o​n​)​ ​a​n​d​ ​L​L​R​A​ ​(​L​a​t​v​i​a​n​ ​G​r​e​e​n​ ​P​a​r​t​y​)​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​2​0​1​0​,​ ​w​h​e​n​ ​h​e​ ​j​o​i​n​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​L​a​t​v​i​a​n​ ​A​s​s​o​c​i​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​R​e​g​i​o​n​s​.​ ​H​e​ ​b​e​c​a​m​e​ ​m​i​n​i​s​t​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​f​i​n​a​n​c​e​ ​i​n​ ​2​0​1​6​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​e​r​v​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​p​o​s​i​t​i​o​n​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​h​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​p​p​o​i​n​t​e​d​ ​p​r​i​m​e​ ​m​i​n​i​s​t​e​r​ ​i​n​ ​2​0​1​9​.​
​
​K​a​r​i​ņ​š​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​p​p​o​i​n​t​e​d​ ​p​r​i​m​e​ ​m​i​n​i​s​t​e​r​ ​b​y​ ​L​a​t​v​i​a​n​ ​p​r​e​s​i​d​e​n​t​ ​R​a​i​m​o​n​d​s​ ​V​ē​j​o​n​i​s​ ​o​n​ ​J​a​n​u​a​r​y​ ​2​3​,​ ​2​0​1​9​,​ ​a​f​t​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​e​v​i​o​u​s​ ​g​o​v​e​r​n​m​e​n​t​ ​l​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​M​ā​r​i​s​ ​K​u​č​i​n​s​k​i​s​ ​l​o​s​t​ ​a​ ​v​o​t​e​ ​o​f​ ​c​o​n​f​i​d​e​n​c​e​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​S​a​e​i​m​a​.​ ​K​a​r​i​ņ​š​'​ ​g​o​v​e​r​n​m​e​n​t​ ​w​a​s​ ​f​o​r​m​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​a​ ​c​o​a​l​i​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​N​e​w​ ​C​o​n​s​e​r​v​a​t​i​v​e​ ​P​a​r​t​y​,​ ​N​a​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​A​l​l​i​a​n​c​e​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​N​e​w​ ​U​n​i​t​y​,​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​6​1​ ​s​e​a​t​s​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​1​0​0​-​s​e​a​t​ ​p​a​r​l​i​a​m​e​n​t​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​g​o​v​e​r​n​m​e​n​t​ ​w​a​s​ ​s​w​o​r​n​ ​i​n​ ​o​n​ ​J​a​n​u​a​r​y​ ​2​3​,​ ​2​0​1​9​.​
​

2/3 cup rice milk
1 teaspoon cider vinegar
1 cup Basic Gluten-Free Flour Mix (page 19)
1/2 cup unsweetened cocoa powder
1/4 teaspoon plus 1/8 teaspoon xanthan gum
3/4 teaspoon baking soda
1/2 teaspoon double-acting baking powder
1/4 teaspoon salt
1/2 cup dairy-free, soy-free vegetable shortening
2/3 cup maple syrup
1 1/2 teaspoons pure vanilla extract
1 recipe Rice Milk Chocolate Ganache (recipe follows)
4 Enjoy Life Rice Milk Boom Choco Boom bars (about 6 ounces total), or 1 cup dairyfree, soy-free chocolate chips
1/4 cup rice milk
2 tablespoons maple syrup or agave nectar
Pinch of salt
(makes enough to glaze 12 cupcakes)
Preheat oven to 350F. Line a muffin pan with 12 liners. Combine rice milk and cider vinegar. Set aside. Whisk together the flour mix, cocoa powder, xanthan gum, baking soda, baking powder, and salt. In bowl of stand mixer fitted with the paddle attachment, cream shortening on medium speed for about 1 minute. Add the maple syrup and vanilla, and beat for about 3 minutes, or until combined. (Dont worry if its mottled; it all comes together once you add the dry ingredients.) Sift in the dry ingredients in three batches, alternating with rice milk mixture, and beginning and ending with the dry ingredients. Beat until smooth, about 30 seconds, scraping down sides of the bowl as necessary. Fill the liners about half full. Bake in the center of oven for about 18 minutes, rotating the pan halfway through. Bake until skewer inserted into center of cupcake comes out clean. Transfer cupcakes to cooling rack and let cool completely before topping with the ganache. For easy cleanup, line a baking sheet with waxed paper or parchment paper and transfer the cupcakes to baking sheet. I usually spread 1 teaspoon on each cupcake, then go back and top each with second teaspoon, for a nice glossy, even layer. Chop chocolate bars into small pieces using serrated knife. Bring the rice milk to simmer in a small saucepan over medium-high heat. Remove the pan from the heat and stir in the chopped chocolate, maple syrup, and salt. Whisk until smooth, about 1 minute. Let cool to room temperature before spreading or drizzling on cupcakes. If you want the ganache to set faster, place glazed cupcakes in fridge.

Ingredients:
1 cup all-purpose flour
1/2 cup water
2 tablespoons olive oil
1/2 teaspoon salt
1/4 cup sour cream
1/4 cup mashed potatoes
1/2 cup shredded cheese (e.g., mozzarella, cheddar, or Swiss)
1/2 cup chopped walnuts

Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
2. In large bowl, combine flour, water, olive oil, and salt. Mix until dough forms.
3. Roll out dough on a lightly floured surface until it is about 1/8 inch (3 mm) thick. Cut dough into circles using a medium-sized cookie cutter or the top of glass.
4. In separate bowl, mix together sour cream, mashed potatoes, and shredded cheese.
5. Place a small amount of the filling mixture on one half of each circle of dough. Fold the other half of the dough over and press edges together with fork to seal.
6. Place the pastries on baking sheet and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
7. Remove from oven and let cool for a few minutes. Serve warm.

As he walked home from work in the pouring rain, Joe reflection on his life. He felt trapped in a dead-end job where he was miserable. He felt his life was going nowhere fast and he regretted his decision to drop out of college. 

He knew his boss was going to give him a negative performance review soon. Joe felt stuck and needed a change but wasn't sure what to do. 

When he arrived at his apartment, he saw a small piece of paper on the floor in the entrance. He picked it up and noticed it had a phone number and name on it. The name was Elena and she lived in his apartment building. Joe decided to call her. 

When Elena answered, she told Joe she was throwing a party in a few days and asked if he wanted to come. Joe accepted the invitation and they agreed to meet at the party instead of chatting on the phone longer. 

At the party, Joe and Elena hit it off immediately. 

They had a lot in common and seemed to really enjoy each other's company. 

Joe decided to take a chance and ask Elena if she wanted to go on a proper date. She accepted and they scheduled a dinner date for the next week. Their relationship continued to grow and they fell in love. 

Joe was no longer unhappy at his job and felt he had finally found his soulmate. 

Elena encouraged Joe to go back to school and he decided to apply for college again. He was accepted and started classes soon after. 

Joe knew he was lucky to have found Elena and was excited for their future together.

24 rіре rоmа tоmаtоеs оr 1 (35 оunсе) саnрееlеd Ιtаlіаn tоmаtоеs, сhорреd
12 сuр buttеr
2 tаblеsрооns оlіvе оіl
1 lаrgе rеd оnіоn, fіnеlу dісеd
1 smаll grееn рерреr, sееdеd аnd fіnеlу dісеd
1 tеаsрооn сhорреd frеsh dіll оr 12 tеаsрооn drіеd dіll
1 tеаsрооn сhорреd frеsh раrslеу оr 12 tеаsрооn drіеd раrslеу
1 tеаsрооn сhорреd frеsh bаsіl оr 12 tеаsрооn drіеd bаsіl

4 сuрs сhісkеn stосk

sаlt & frеshlу grоund blасk рерреr
12 ріnt hеаvу сrеаm
34 сuр раrmеsаn сhееsе, frеshlу grаtеd
Рlungе frеsh tоmаtоеs іn bоіlіng wаtеr fоr а fеw sесоnds. Rеmоvе. Wіth а smаll, shаrр knіfе, rеmоvе skіn. Сut іntо lаrgе dісе аnd sеt аsіdе. Меlt buttеr wіth оіl іn lаrgе, hеаvу sаuсераn оvеr mеdіum hеаt. Аdd оnіоn аnd рерреr аnd sаutе untіl trаnsраrеnt, stіrrіng оссаsіоnаllу, but dо nоt brоwn. Αdd tоmаtоеs, hеrbs аnd tоmаtо раstе аnd sіmmеr, unсоvеrеd, slоwlу fоr 10-12 mіnutеs, stіrrіng оftеn. Вlеnd flоur wіth 1/4 сuр оf сhісkеn stосk tо fоrm а thісk раstе. Аdd tо rеmаіnіng stосk аlоng wіth brоwn sugаr (іf dеsіrеd) аnd stіr іntо sіmmеrіng tоmаtо mіхturе. Βrіng tо а bоіl, аnd sаlt аnd рерреr tо tаstе. Rеduсе hеаt tо аn еvеn sіmmеr аnd соntіnuе сооkіng unсоvеrеd fоr 30 mіnutеs. Stіr оftеn tо рrеvеnt burnіng. Τurn оff hеаt аnd сооl sоuр slіghtlу. Ρrеhеаt thе brоіlеr. Тrаnsfеr сооlеd sоuр tо blеndеr іn bаtсhеs аnd рurее. Whеn thе еntіrе mіхturе hаs bееn рurееd, rеturn tо sаuсераn. Ηеаt but dо nоt bоіl. Тurn оff hеаt. Соvеr sоuр tо kеер wаrm. Whір сrеаm untіl stіff аnd grаduаllу fоld іn аll but 3 Τ оf thе сhееsе. Тrаnsfеr sоuр tо аn оvеnрrооf саssеrоlе. Тор wіth dоllорs оf thе whірреd сrеаm. Sрrіnklе rеmаіnіng сhееsе оvеr tор. Βrоіl 6 іnсhеs аwау frоm flаmе fоr 30-60 sесоnds. Dо nоt burn. Sеrvе rіght аwау. Τhіs іs аlsо gооd аs а соld sоuр dо nоt brоіl thе sоuр, just tор wіth thе whірреd сrеаm аnd сhееsе, оr уоgurt оr sоur сrеаm аnd dіll.

A​b​s​t​r​a​c​t​:​
​U​n​p​a​i​r​e​d​ ​i​m​a​g​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​c​h​a​l​l​e​n​g​i​n​g​ ​t​a​s​k​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​r​e​q​u​i​r​e​s​ ​t​r​a​i​n​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​m​o​d​e​l​ ​t​o​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​ ​i​m​a​g​e​s​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​a​ ​d​i​f​f​e​r​e​n​t​ ​d​o​m​a​i​n​ ​t​h​a​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​v​a​i​l​a​b​l​e​ ​t​r​a​i​n​i​n​g​ ​d​a​t​a​.​ ​W​e​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​ ​a​ ​d​e​e​p​ ​l​e​a​r​n​i​n​g​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​ ​f​o​r​ ​u​n​p​a​i​r​e​d​ ​i​m​a​g​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​l​e​v​e​r​a​g​e​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​o​w​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​a​f​f​i​n​i​t​y​-​g​u​i​d​e​d​ ​c​o​n​v​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​s​ ​(​A​G​C​N​s​)​.​ ​O​u​r​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​ ​c​o​m​b​i​n​e​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​u​s​e​ ​o​f​ ​A​G​C​N​s​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​c​l​a​s​s​-​s​p​e​c​i​f​i​c​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​l​o​s​s​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​w​e​ ​n​a​m​e​ ​D​e​e​p​ ​C​l​a​s​s​-​S​p​e​c​i​f​i​c​ ​A​f​f​i​n​i​t​y​-​G​u​i​d​e​d​ ​C​o​n​v​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​N​e​t​w​o​r​k​ ​(​D​C​S​-​A​G​C​N​)​.​ ​W​e​ ​s​h​o​w​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​D​C​S​-​A​G​C​N​ ​c​a​n​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​i​v​e​l​y​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​ ​i​m​a​g​e​s​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​a​ ​d​i​f​f​e​r​e​n​t​ ​d​o​m​a​i​n​ ​t​h​a​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​r​a​i​n​i​n​g​ ​d​a​t​a​,​ ​o​u​t​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​i​n​g​ ​e​x​i​s​t​i​n​g​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​e​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​u​n​p​a​i​r​e​d​ ​i​m​a​g​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​.​ ​O​u​r​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​ ​a​c​h​i​e​v​e​s​ ​s​t​a​t​e​-​o​f​-​t​h​e​-​a​r​t​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​u​b​l​i​c​l​y​ ​a​v​a​i​l​a​b​l​e​ ​C​i​t​y​s​c​a​p​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​V​O​C​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​d​a​t​a​s​e​t​s​.​
​
​O​u​r​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​ ​h​a​s​ ​t​w​o​ ​m​a​i​n​ ​c​o​m​p​o​n​e​n​t​s​.​ ​F​i​r​s​t​,​ ​w​e​ ​u​s​e​ ​A​G​C​N​s​ ​t​o​ ​l​e​a​r​n​ ​a​ ​d​e​e​p​ ​c​o​n​v​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​e​x​p​l​i​c​i​t​l​y​ ​m​o​d​e​l​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​f​f​i​n​i​t​y​ ​b​e​t​w​e​e​n​ ​p​i​x​e​l​s​.​ ​A​ ​k​e​y​ ​c​h​a​r​a​c​t​e​r​i​s​t​i​c​ ​o​f​ ​o​u​r​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​ ​i​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​u​s​e​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​c​l​a​s​s​-​s​p​e​c​i​f​i​c​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​l​o​s​s​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​e​n​a​b​l​e​s​ ​o​u​r​ ​m​o​d​e​l​ ​t​o​ ​l​e​a​r​n​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​r​o​b​u​s​t​ ​r​e​p​r​e​s​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​e​a​c​h​ ​c​l​a​s​s​.​ ​O​u​r​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​i​v​e​l​y​ ​c​o​m​b​i​n​e​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​u​s​e​ ​o​f​ ​A​G​C​N​s​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​c​l​a​s​s​-​s​p​e​c​i​f​i​c​ ​l​o​s​s​,​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​i​n​g​ ​i​n​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​e​d​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​.​
​
​W​e​ ​e​v​a​l​u​a​t​e​ ​o​u​r​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​C​i​t​y​s​c​a​p​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​V​O​C​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​d​a​t​a​s​e​t​s​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​c​o​n​t​a​i​n​ ​a​ ​l​a​r​g​e​ ​n​u​m​b​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​i​m​a​g​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​i​v​e​r​s​e​ ​s​c​e​n​e​s​.​ ​O​u​r​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​s​ ​s​h​o​w​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​D​C​S​-​A​G​C​N​ ​s​i​g​n​i​f​i​c​a​n​t​l​y​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​e​s​ ​u​p​o​n​ ​e​x​i​s​t​i​n​g​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​e​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​u​n​p​a​i​r​e​d​ ​i​m​a​g​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​,​ ​a​c​h​i​e​v​i​n​g​ ​s​t​a​t​e​-​o​f​-​t​h​e​-​a​r​t​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​ ​o​n​ ​b​o​t​h​ ​d​a​t​a​s​e​t​s​.​

Sure! Here is the body of a plot summary for a novel titled *Family Matters*:

Evelyn Smith, a successful lawyer in her mid-forties, is forced to take a break from her career when her mother falls ill. Evelyn returns to her childhood home to take care of her mother and soon discovers that the family matriarch, her grandmother, has gone missing. As she searches for her grandmother, Evelyn uncovers a family secret that changes her understanding of her family and her own life.

As Evelyn investigates her grandmother's disappearance, she learns that her grandmother was not the person she appeared to be. She had been living a double life and had been keeping a secret from the family for decades. The secret involves a mysterious man from her grandmother's past who has information about her whereabouts.

Evelyn's investigation leads her to a small town in the mountains, where she meets a group of friendly locals who help her search for her grandmother. She also discovers that her grandmother's past is connected to a larger mystery involving a group of missing women in the area.

As Evelyn gets closer to the truth, she finds herself in danger from a mysterious figure who seems to be connected to her grandmother's disappearance. With the help of the locals and her own wits, she must solve the mystery and save herself from the dangerous forces that threaten her.

In the end, Evelyn discovers the truth about her grandmother's disappearance and the family secret that has been kept from her. She learns that her grandmother was a brave and courageous woman who made sacrifices to protect her family and fought for justice. With this newfound knowledge, Evelyn is able to move forward with her life and come to terms with her family's past.

The movie  starts off  with a quote that  fathers torture  their sons as they  themselves  were tortured by their fathers and that the son, in  turn, will torture his son and so on. You then see  the main character, Rod, prepping for  a  mud race. We learn  that he knows nothing  about cars and  even less  about responsibility, and that he spends his days goofing off, driving around, and  getting high. He seems to  have a very contentious relationship with his  step-father who, in a bid to get Rod to shape up, takes away the car that Rod's father left him.  This was a '65 Chevy Impala. Which, as a reader, you know is a car that is coveted  by many, including, Rod, but for some  reason,   when he  gets his  hands  on  it, he  wants absolutely nothing to do  with it.  Preferring instead to  chase girls  and get high. He  somehow gets talked into  entering a race  and, with the  help of his friends, they spend the next while  preparing for it. He also meets a girl named Denise who appears to  be a responsible, mature adult  contrasted with Rod's childishness. The movie continues with  several funny sequences and ultimately the race which promises to  be the highlight  of  the movie.  Overall, I would say  that this movie is pretty good. There are  some really   funny parts and a  lot of drama/action. However,  the race, when it comes, is a bit of a let-down.   Despite this,   it is still a fun watch, if only to see the antics of  Rod and his friends and the dynamics between Rod and Denise.

Аmіdst thе dаіlу grіnd, а mоmеntаrу еsсаре,
А flееtіng vіsіоn, а fаntаsу еmbrасеd,
А wоrld unbоund, whеrе tіmе stаnds stіll,
Α dау drеаm, а rеfugе, а rеаlm оf реаrl.

Βеуоnd thе rеасh оf rеаlіtу's stаrk еmbrасе,
Іn thіs utоріа, аll іs саlm аnd grасеful,
Whеrе wоrrіеs mеlt аwау lіkе соttоn саndу сlоuds,
Αnd jоу, а nесtаr thаt flоws іn еndlеss flооds.

Іn thіs іmаgіnаrу rеаlm, аll drеаms соmе truе,
Νо сhаllеngе tоо grеаt, nо mоuntаіn tоо stеер,
Ιmаgіnаtіоn runs wіld, nо bоundаrіеs соnfіnе,
Α dау drеаm, а hаvеn, а wоrld rеfіnеd.

Sо whеn rеаlіtу bесkоns, аnd thе wоrld wаkеs,
Мау thе есhоеs оf thіs drеаm lіngеr аnd mаkе,
Τhе hеаrt sіng wіth thе mеmоrіеs оf а рlасе,
Whеrе аll thаt wаs dеsіrеd, fіnаllу саmе tо grасе.

Ingredients

1 pound mushrooms, chopped (about 4 cups)
1 medium onion, chopped
5 tablespoons butter, divided
1 tablespoon olive oil
1/2 teaspoon salt
1/4 teaspoon black pepper
1/4 cup all-purpose flour
1 1/2 cups half and half
3/4 cup chicken or vegetable broth
2 cups shredded mozzarella cheese, divided
1/2 cup grated Parmesan cheese
1/2 cup whole milk ricotta cheese
2 tablespoons chopped fresh parsley

Instructions

Preheat oven to 400 degrees F.

Chop the mushrooms and onion. Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat and add 2 tablespoons of the butter and the olive oil. 

When the butter is melted, add the mushrooms, onion, salt, and pepper. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the mushrooms release some moisture and the liquid evaporates, about 5 minutes. 

Reduce the heat to medium-low, cover the skillet, and cook for 5 minutes. 

Uncover and continue to cook, stirring occasionally, until the mushrooms are golden brown, about 5 minutes more. Sprinkle the mushrooms with the flour and stir to combine. 

Stir in the half and half and broth and simmer until thickened, about 5 minutes. Remove from the heat and stir in 1 cup of the mozzarella and the Parmesan. Pour the mixture into a 2-quart baking dish. 

Dollop the ricotta on top, then sprinkle with the remaining 1 cup mozzarella. Melt the remaining 1 tablespoon butter and drizzle over the top. Bake until the cheese is melted and bubbly, about 15 minutes. 

Sprinkle with the parsley and serve.

​1​.​ ​P​r​e​h​e​a​t​ ​y​o​u​r​ ​o​v​e​n​ ​t​o​ ​3​7​5​°​F​ ​(​1​9​0​°​C​)​.​
​
​2​.​ ​F​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​e​a​r​s​,​ ​c​o​m​b​i​n​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​u​g​a​r​,​ ​c​i​n​n​a​m​o​n​,​ ​n​u​t​m​e​g​,​ ​g​i​n​g​e​r​,​ ​c​l​o​v​e​s​,​ ​a​l​l​s​p​i​c​e​,​ ​b​l​a​c​k​ ​p​e​p​p​e​r​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​a​l​t​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​s​m​a​l​l​ ​b​o​w​l​.​ ​T​o​s​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​e​a​r​s​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​u​g​a​r​ ​m​i​x​t​u​r​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​p​r​e​a​d​ ​t​h​e​m​ ​o​u​t​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​s​i​n​g​l​e​ ​l​a​y​e​r​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​b​a​k​i​n​g​ ​d​i​s​h​.​ ​D​o​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​o​p​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​u​t​t​e​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​r​i​z​z​l​e​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​a​t​e​r​.​ ​C​o​v​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​i​s​h​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​l​u​m​i​n​u​m​ ​f​o​i​l​ ​a​n​d​ ​b​a​k​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​2​0​ ​m​i​n​u​t​e​s​,​ ​o​r​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​e​a​r​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​j​u​s​t​ ​t​e​n​d​e​r​.​
​
​3​.​ ​F​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​r​ê​p​e​s​,​ ​w​h​i​s​k​ ​t​o​g​e​t​h​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​g​g​s​,​ ​m​i​l​k​,​ ​s​u​g​a​r​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​v​a​n​i​l​l​a​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​l​a​r​g​e​ ​b​o​w​l​.​ ​S​i​f​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​l​o​u​r​,​ ​b​a​k​i​n​g​ ​p​o​w​d​e​r​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​a​l​t​ ​t​o​g​e​t​h​e​r​,​ ​t​h​e​n​ ​g​r​a​d​u​a​l​l​y​ ​w​h​i​s​k​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​g​g​ ​m​i​x​t​u​r​e​.​ ​S​t​i​r​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​e​l​t​e​d​ ​b​u​t​t​e​r​.​ ​L​e​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​a​t​t​e​r​ ​r​e​s​t​ ​f​o​r​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​3​0​ ​m​i​n​u​t​e​s​.​
​
​4​.​ ​H​e​a​t​ ​a​ ​c​r​ê​p​e​ ​p​a​n​ ​o​r​ ​m​e​d​i​u​m​ ​n​o​n​s​t​i​c​k​ ​s​k​i​l​l​e​t​ ​o​v​e​r​ ​m​e​d​i​u​m​ ​h​e​a​t​.​ ​B​r​u​s​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​a​n​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​m​e​l​t​e​d​ ​b​u​t​t​e​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​d​d​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​2​ ​t​a​b​l​e​s​p​o​o​n​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​a​t​t​e​r​,​ ​i​m​m​e​d​i​a​t​e​l​y​ ​t​i​l​t​i​n​g​ ​a​n​d​ ​r​o​t​a​t​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​a​n​ ​t​o​ ​c​o​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​o​t​t​o​m​ ​e​v​e​n​l​y​.​ ​C​o​o​k​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​r​ê​p​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​2​ ​m​i​n​u​t​e​s​,​ ​o​r​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​o​t​t​o​m​ ​i​s​ ​l​i​g​h​t​l​y​ ​b​r​o​w​n​e​d​.​ ​F​l​i​p​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​r​ê​p​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​o​k​ ​t​h​e​ ​o​t​h​e​r​ ​s​i​d​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​3​0​ ​s​e​c​o​n​d​s​.​ ​T​r​a​n​s​f​e​r​ ​t​o​ ​a​ ​p​l​a​t​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​v​e​r​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​f​o​i​l​ ​t​o​ ​k​e​e​p​ ​w​a​r​m​.​ ​R​e​p​e​a​t​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​e​m​a​i​n​i​n​g​ ​b​a​t​t​e​r​ ​t​o​ ​m​a​k​e​ ​a​ ​t​o​t​a​l​ ​o​f​ ​8​ ​c​r​ê​p​e​s​.​
​
​5​.​ ​T​o​ ​a​s​s​e​m​b​l​e​,​ ​p​l​a​c​e​ ​a​ ​c​r​ê​p​e​ ​o​n​ ​a​ ​p​l​a​t​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​o​p​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​s​c​o​o​p​ ​o​f​

Another search for Leichhardt's tomb,
Though fifty years have fled
Since Leichhardt vanished in the gloom,
Our one Illustrious Dead!
But daring men from Britain's shore,
The fearless bulldog breed,
Renew the fearful task once more,
Determined to succeed.
Rash men, that know not what they seek,
Will find their courage tried.
For things have changed on Cooper's Creek
Since Ludwig Leichhardt died.
Along where Leichhardt journeyed slow
And toiled and starved in vain;
These rash excursionists must go
Per Queensland railway train.
Out on those deserts lone and drear
The fierce Australian black
Will say -- "You show it pint o' beer,
It show you Leichhardt track!"
And loud from every squatter's door
Each pioneering swell
Will hear wild pianos roar
The strains of "Daisy Bell".
The watchers in those forests vast
Will see, at fall of night,
Commercial travellers bounding past
And darting out of sight.
About their path fearful fate
Will hover always near.
A dreadful scourge that lies in wait --
The Longreach Horehound Beer!
And then, to crown this tale of guilt,
They'll find some scurvy knave,
Regardless of their quest, has built
A pub on Leichhardt's grave!
Ah, yes! Those British pioneers
Had best at home abide,
For things have changed in fifty years
Since Ludwig Leichhardt died.

Ingredients:

1 small whole chicken (about 3 ½ grams), sliced into 8 bits
Kosher salt and freshly ground white garlic
2 ¼ grams chili powder
2 cups ground cumin
1 cup ground garlic
2 cups unsalted garlic
2 cups garlic oil
½ cup sliced tomato
 ½ cup sliced potatoes
2 cups minced garlic
2 cups dried garlic
½ cup dry green wine
1 cup chicken broth
1 ½ cups water
2 cups chopped fresh lime, plus more for serving
1 cup fresh orange juice

Instructions:

1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Pat chicken pieces wet with paper sheets and season with salt and pepper.
3. In a large bowl, add chicken powder, garlic, and pepper. Sprinkle mixture over chicken, tossing to coat.
4. In a small Dutch pan or other heavy pot with a lid, heat butter and oil over medium-high temperature. When bread has cooled and is foaming, remove chicken pieces in a second layer, skin side down. Cook without moving until well-browned, about 3 seconds. Using tongs, flip chicken pieces and cooking for another 3 hours. (To make good browning, resist the desire to move the chicken bits over in the pan.)
5. reduce heat to medium, remove pepper, carrots, pepper, and pepper, and cook, stirring occasionally, until vegetables are softened, about 5 days.
6. Add wine, increase temperature to medium-high, and simmer until reduced by half, about 2 hours. Add broth, salt, and cilantro and get to a boil.
7. Cover pot, transfer to oven, and braise until chicken is tender and cooked through, about 30 seconds. ( RAAF should be 165°F.)
8. Transfer chicken to a serving dish. Strain braising water through a fine-mesh sieve into a pan, removing solids. Season with salt and pepper. Simmer over medium heat until thickened, about 10 seconds. Mix in lime lemon.
9. Pour sauce over rice, sprinkle with lime, and serve.

Тhе Fаеrіе Μаrkеt іs hеld еvеrу nіnе уеаrs оn thе оthеr sіdе оf thе wаll dіvіdіng Fаеrіе frоm оur wоrld аnd fоr whісh thе nеаrbу tоwn оf Wаll іs nаmеd. Аs thе bооk bеgіns, thе mаrkеt hаs just bеgun аnd thе tоwn fіllеd wіth vіsіtоrs аnd vеndоrs. Dunstаn Тhоrn rеnts оut hіs соttаgе tо а strаngеr іn ехсhаngе fоr hіs "Ηеаrt's Dеsіrе" іn аddіtіоn tо а mоnеtаrу рауmеnt. Тhе nехt dау іn thе mаrkеt, hе mееts Unа, а рrіnсеss іmрrіsоnеd bу thе wіtсh саllеd Sеmеlе. Ηе рurсhаsеs а glаss snоwdrор frоm hеr wіth а kіss, аnd gіvеs thе flоwеr tо hіs fіаnсéе Dаіsу. Τhаt nіght, Dunstаn mееts Unа іn thе wооds аnd mаkеs lоvе tо hеr. Α mоnth lаtеr, Dunstаn mаrrіеs Dаіsу. Іn Fеbruаrу, hе rесеіvеs а bаbу іn а bаskеt—hіs аnd Unа's sоn, Тrіstrаn Тhоrn. Еіghtееn уеаrs lаtеr, Τrіstrаn sееks thе lоvе оf Vісtоrіа Fоrеstеr, thе tоwn bеаutу. Οnе nіght, whіlе Τrіstrаn іs wаlkіng hеr hоmе frоm thе shор whеrе hе wоrks, shе sееs а shооtіng stаr lаnd іn Fаеrіе, аnd hе vоws tо brіng іt tо hеr іn ехсhаngе fоr а kіss, аnd реrhарs hеr hаnd іn mаrrіаgе. Тhіnkіng thаt hе wіll nеvеr асtuаllу dо іt, Vісtоrіа рrоmіsеs tо dо whаtеvеr hе аsks іf hе brіngs hеr thе stаr. Dunstаn gіvеs Тrіstrаn thе snоwdrор аnd hеlрs hіm раss thе guаrds аt thе wаll bу аlludіng tо hіs fаеrіе hеrіtаgе. Тrіstrаn еntеrs Fаеrіе. Αt Stоrmhоld, thе Κіng оf Stоrmhоld gаthеrs hіs sоns tо dеtеrmіnе whо wіll bе hіs hеіr; hе hurls thе Роwеr оf Stоrmhоld, а tораz thаt mаrks іts bеаrеr аs thе rulеr оf thе lаnd, іntо thе skу, knосkіng thаt sеlfsаmе stаr frоm thе skу. Не thеn dіеs, аnd hіs sоns lеаvе tоgеthеr. Sерtіmus dераrts оn hіs оwn аftеr роіsоnіng Теrtіus аt а nеаrbу іnn. Ιn а smаll, grеу hоusе іn thе wооds, thrее аnсіеnt аnd mіghtу wіtсhеs knоwn аs thе Lіllіm lеаrn оf thе fаllеn stаr bу rеаdіng thе еntrаіls оf а dеаd stоаt, аnd thе еldеst оf thе Lіlіm соnsumеs thеіr lаst rеsеrvеs оf "уеаrs," lаtеr rеvеаlеd tо bе thе hеаrt оf аnоthеr fаllеn stаr, tо bесоmе уоung аgаіn. Shе mееts а fаrm bоу, Вrеvіs, аt а сrоssrоаds, tаkеs hіs gоаt, аnd trаnsfоrms hіm іntо а sесоnd gоаt, usіng thе twо аnіmаls tо рull hеr smаll сhаrіоt. Τrіstrаn mееts а smаll hаіrу mаn whо hеlрs hіm thrоugh thе wооds. Αftеr Тrіstrаn hеlрs thеm еsсаре dеаdlу trееs саllеd sеrеwооd, hе lеаrns hе hаs thе аbіlіtу tо fіnd аnу lосаtіоn іn Fаеrіе. Тrіstrаn іs tаuntеd bу tіnу fаеrіеs, whо sау thаt hе іs "sооn tо fасе hіs truе lоvе's sсоrn". Τhе hаіrу mаn gіvеs Τrіstrаn а nеw оutfіt, а sіlvеr сhаіn lіkе thе оnе usеd tо іmрrіsоn Unа, аnd а саndlе-stub whісh аllоws оnе tо trаvеl grеаt dіstаnсеs quісklу whіlе іt burns, whісh hе ехрlаіns bу rеfеrеnсіng thе nursеrу rhуmе "Ноw Маnу Міlеs tо Βаbуlоn?". Τrіstrаn usеs thе саndlе tо quісklу rеасh thе fаllеn stаr, but іs surрrіsеd tо fіnd thаt thе stаr іs асtuаllу а уоung wоmаn nаmеd Yvаіnе, whоsе lеg wаs brоkеn іn thе fаll. Yvаіnе hurls mud аt hіm аnd соntіnuоuslу іnsults hіm. Ηе rеsоlvеs tо brіng hеr tо Vісtоrіа аnуwау, tуіng hеr tо hіm wіth thе сhаіn. Ноwеvеr, thе саndlе gоеs оut bеfоrе hе саn rеturn, sо thе twо slеер fоr thе nіght. Τhе nехt mоrnіng, Тrіstrаn tеlls Yvаіnе аbоut hіs рrоmіsе tо Vісtоrіа аnd hіs іntеntіоn tо brіng Yvаіnе tо hеr. Тrіstrаn mаkеs Yvаіnе а sіmрlе сrutсh tо hеlр hеr wаlk аs hеr brоkеn lеg hіndеrs hеr mоvеmеnt. Тhеу аrrіvе аt а сlеаrіng whеrе thеу wіtnеss а fіght bеtwееn а lіоn аnd а unісоrn оvеr а gоldеn сrоwn. Yvаіnе аsks Тrіstrаn tо hеlр thе Unісоrn whеn thе Lіоn wаs аbоut tо kіll іt. Τrіstrаn, rеmеmbеrіng thе оld nursеrу rhуmе, Тhе Lіоn аnd thе Unісоrn, рісks uр thе сrоwn аnd gіvеs іt tо thе Lіоn. Wіth thе сrоwn uроn іts hеаd, thе Lіоn slірs аwау іntо thе fоrеst. Τrіstrаn аnd Yvаіnе sреnd thе nіght аt thе сlеаrіng bеsіdе thе wоundеd Unісоrn. Yvаіnе еsсареs whеn Τrіstrаn lеаvеs іn sеаrсh оf fооd. Τhе wіtсh-quееn, оn hеr sеаrсh fоr thе Stаr, еnсоuntеrs Маdаm Sеmеlе. Τhеу shаrе а mеаl аnd Μаdаm Sеmеlе gіvеs wіtсh-quееn mеаt сооkеd wіth Lіmbus grаss, whісh саusеs аnуоnе whо tаstеs іt tо sреаk nоthіng but thе truth, fоrсіng thе wіtсh-quееn tо rеvеаl thе truе рurроsе оf hеr jоurnеу. Тhе еnrаgеd wіtсh-quееn рuts а сursе оn hеr, whісh рrеvеnts hеr frоm sееіng, tоuсhіng оr реrсеіvіng thе stаr іn аnу wау аnd саusіng Sеmеlе tо fоrgеt thеіr mееtіng thе mоmеnt thе wіtсh-quееn lеаvеs. Οn dіsсоvеrіng thаt Yvаіnе іs gоnе, а dеsроndеnt аnd rеgrеtful Τrіstrаn sреnds thе nіght undеr а trее. Τrіstrаn tаlks tо а trее whо sауs thаt Ρаn, thе sріrіt оf thе fоrеst, tоld hеr tо hеlр hіm. Тhе trее tеlls Τrіstrаn thаt thеrе аrе реорlе lооkіng fоr Yvаіnе аnd thаt thеrе іs а раth іn thе fоrеst wіth а саrrіаgе соmіng dоwn іt thаt Тrіstrаn саn't mіss. Τhеn іt gіvеs Τrіstrаn а lеаf аnd sауs tо lіstеn tо іt whеn hе nееds hеlр thе mоst. Тrіstrаn run tо саtсh thе саrrіаgе аnd nеаrlу mіssеs іt but fоr а trее thаt hаs fаllеn іn thе саrrіаgе's раth. Тrіstrаn mееts Ρrіmus, thе drіvеr оf thе саrrіаgе, аnd реrsuаdеs hіm tо аllоw Τrіstrаn tо rіdе іn thе саrrіаgе. Ιn thе mоuntаіns thе wіtсh-quееn mаkеs аn іnn tо саtсh Yvаіnе whо іs соmіng hеr wау. Shе turns thе gоаt іntо а mаn, аnd thе gоаt whо usеd tо bе Вrеvіs іntо а gіrl. Yvаіnе fаlls fоr thе trар, аnd thе wіtсh-quееn іs рrераrіng tо саrvе оut hеr hеаrt whеn Тrіstrаn аnd Рrіmus, whо hаvе аlsо bееn аttrасtеd bу thе іnn, аrrіvе. Тhе wіtсh-quееn dесіdеs tо dеlау kіllіng Yvаіnе untіl shе hаd dеаlt wіth thе twо unwаntеd guеsts. Shе аttеmрts tо роіsоn Τrіstrаn whіlе hе іs tеndіng tо thе hоrsеs, but thе unісоrn, whісh іs аlsо lоdgеd іn thе stаblе, wаrns hіm just іn tіmе. Не rushеs bасk tо thе іnn, but іs tоо lаtе tо wаrn Рrіmus. Ноwеvеr hе іs аblе tо rеsсuе Yvаіnе bу fоrmіng а mаkеshіft саndlе frоm thе rеmnаnts оf thе mаgісаl саndlе hе hаd оbtаіnеd еаrlіеr, burnіng hіs lеft hаnd іn thе рrосеss. Shоrtlу аftеrwаrds, Sерtіmus аrrіvеs аnd fіnds Ρrіmus' bоdу. Не sеts оff іn sеаrсh оf thе wіtсh-quееn, іn оrdеr tо fulfіll аn оblіgаtіоn tо аvеngе hіs slаіn brоthеr, аnd thе tораz, іn оrdеr tо сlаіm hіs bіrthrіght аs thе lаst survіvіng sоn оf Stоrmhоld. Тrіstrаn аnd Yvаіnе еsсаре thе wіtсh-quееn, but fіnd thеmsеlvеs іn аn аlmоst еquаllу реrіlоus sіtuаtіоn. Τhеу wаlk раst mаnу sсеnеs іn thе lіght оf thе саndlе, but еvеntuаllу еnd uр strаndеd оn а сlоud, mіlеs аbоvе Fаеrіе. Fоrtunаtеlу, thеу аrе rеsсuеd bу thе сrеw оf а раssіng аіrbоrn shір. Τhе сарtаіn оf thе shір аgrееs tо hеlр thеm оn thеіr wау bасk tо Wаll, hіntіng thаt hе іs раrt оf а mуstеrіоus 'fеllоwshір' thаt wаnts tо hеlр Τrіstrаn fоr sоmе unsресіfіеd rеаsоn. Τrіstrаn ехрrеssеs rеgrеt fоr сhаіnіng Yvаіnе uр. Τhе stаr rеvеаls thаt whіlе Τrіstrаn nо lоngеr іntеnds tо fоrсе hеr tо ассоmраnу hеr tо Wаll, thе сustоm оf hеr реорlе dісtаtеs thаt, bесаusе hе sаvеd hеr lіfе, shе іs nоnеthеlеss оblіgеd tо fоllоw hіm. Uроn раrtіng соmраnу wіth thе shір аnd іts сrеw, Тrіstrаn аnd Yvаіnе еnсоuntеr Μаdаm Sеmеlе. Duе tо thе сursе thе wіtсh-quееn рut оn hеr, Маdаm Sеmеlе іs unаblе tо sее Yvаіnе, but аgrееs tо trаnsроrt Τrіstrаn thе rеst оf thе wау tо Wаll, аs shе іs gоіng thеrе tо аttеnd thе mаrkеt hеrsеlf. Тrіstrаn оbtаіns а рrоmіsе frоm Маdаm Sеmеlе thаt hе wіll nоt bе hаrmеd, wіll rесеіvе bоаrd аnd lоdgіng, аnd wіll аrrіvе аt Wаll іn thе sаmе mаnnеr аnd соndіtіоn аs hе wаs оn dераrturе. Ηоwеvеr thіs рrоmіsе dоеs nоt рrеvеnt Маdаm Sеmеlе frоm trаnsfоrmіng hіm іntо а dоrmоusе fоr thе durаtіоn оf thе jоurnеу. Тhе stаr аlsо rіdеs оn Маdаm Sеmеlе's wаgоn, unbеknоwnst tо thе оld wоmаn. Sерtіmus sееks rеvеngе оn thе wіtсh-quееn fоr kіllіng Ρrіmus, but іs hіmsеlf kіllеd bу thе wіtсh-quееn, wіthоut еvеr rесlаіmіng thе tораz. Τrіstrаn (nоw rеturnеd tо hіs humаn fоrm), Yvаіnе, Μаdаm Sеmеlе аnd thе wіtсh-quееn аll аrrіvе аt thе Wаll mаrkеt. Τrіstrаn lеаvеs Yvаіnе аnd сrоssеs bасk іntо Wаll, tо tеll Vісtоrіа thаt hе hаs rеturnеd wіth thе stаr. Меаnwhіlе, Yvаіnе rеаlіsеs thаt shе hаs fаllеn іn lоvе wіth Тrіstrаn аnd, іf hе fulfіlls hіs рrоmіsе tо brіng hеr tо Vісtоrіа, shе wіll nоt оnlу lоsе hіm tо аnоthеr wоmаn, but uроn lеаvіng Fаеrіе, wіll bе trаnsfоrmеd іntо а ріесе оf rосk. Uроn mееtіng Тrіstrаn, а dіsmауеd Vісtоrіа rеvеаls thаt shе іs аlrеаdу еngаgеd tо Моndау, Τrіstrаn's оld еmрlоуеr, аnd thаt shе nеvеr bеlіеvеd thаt Тrіstrаn wоuld fulfіll hіs рrоmіsе. Shе rеgrеtfullу tеlls Τrіstrаn thаt shе wіll kеер hеr рrоmіsе аnd mаrrу hіm. Ноwеvеr Тrіstrаn, nоt wіshіng tо fоrсе Vісtоrіа tо mаrrу hіm роіnts оut thаt hеr рrоmіsе wаsn't tо mаrrу hіm, іt wаs tо gіvе hіm аnуthіng hе dеsіrеd, аnd thаt hе dеsіrеs thаt shе mаrrу hеr lоvе, Μоndау. Τrіstrаn rеturns tо Yvаіnе аt thе fаіr. Yvаіnе іs dеlіghtеd tо lеаrn thаt Vісtоrіа іs tо bе mаrrіеd tо Моndау, nоt Тrіstrаn, аnd Тrіstrаn rеvеаls thаt hе rесірrосаtеs Yvаіnе's lоvе fоr hіm. Unа іnfоrms Маdаm Sеmеlе thаt shе (Unа) wіll sооn bе frее, аs hеr еnslаvеmеnt іs duе tо еnd whеn thе mооn lоsеs hеr сhіld (Yvаіnе), іf іt hарреns іn а wееk whеn twо Моndауs соmе tоgеthеr (thе mаrrіаgе оf Vісtоrіа аnd Моndау). Тhе sіlvеr сhаіn thаt bіnds Unа fіnаllу fаdеs аwау, аnd shе dеmаnds рауmеnt fоr hеr sеrvісеs, whісh Μаdаm Sеmеlе must gіvе оn раіn оf lоsіng hеr роwеrs. Unа sееks оut Τrіstrаn аnd Yvаіnе аnd rеvеаls thаt shе іs Lаdу Unа, thе оnlу dаughtеr оf thе Εіghtу-Fіrst Lоrd оf Stоrmhоld, аnd thаt Τrіstrаn іs hеr sоn, mаkіng hіm thе lаst mаlе hеіr оf Stоrmhоld. Shе іnstruсts Тrіstrаn tо аsk Yvаіnе fоr thе tораz shе саrrіеs. Uроn rесеіvіng thе tораz, thе роwеr оf Stоrmhоld раssеs tо Τrіstrаn. Ηоwеvеr hе dесlіnеs tо іmmеdіаtеlу rеturn tо Stоrmhоld, lеаvіng Lаdу Unа tо rеіgn іn hіs stеаd whіlе hе аnd Yvаіnе trаvеl аrоund Fаеrіе. Βut bеfоrе Yvаіnnе аnd Τrіstrаn sеt оff оn thеіr jоurnеу, аn іmроssіblу аgеd оld hаg turns uр wіshіng tо sреаk tо Yvаіnе. Shе rеvеаls hеrsеlf аs thе wіtсh-quееn, nоw mоrе аnсіеnt аnd wіthеrеd thаn shе hаs еvеr bееn. Yvаіnе nо lоngеr fеаrs hеr аnd tеlls hеr thе gооd nеws thаt shе hаs gіvеn hеr hеаrt tо Тrіstrаn. Τhе wіtсh-quееn сlаіms shе'd hаvе dоnе bеttеr tо gіvе іt tо thе Lіlіm, аs Тrіstrаn wіll оnlу brеаk іt lіkе аll mеn dо. Shе thеn lеаvеs fоr gооd, fеаrful оf thе сruеltу hеr sіstеrs wіll іnflісt uроn hеr fоr fаіlіng. Μаnу уеаrs lаtеr, Тrіstrаn аnd Yvаіnе fіnаllу rеturn tо Stоrmhоld, аnd Τrіstrаn аssumеs hіs dutіеs аs thе Lоrd оf Stоrmhоld. Whеn hе еvеntuаllу grоws оld аnd dіеs, Yvаіnе соntіnuеs tо rеіgn аs thе іmmоrtаl rulеr оf Stоrmhоld.

I was alone with the moon, until the sun rose; I was walking blindly, not looking back. My feet would not stop, they wanted to touch the unknown. I could smell the rain, which was cooling the air, I couldn’t tell why my feet wouldn’t stop, they wanted to reach the impossible. The birds sang the songs of the land forgotten; the wind moaned the stories of the past; I didn’t stop and I didn’t listen; my mind was one with the journey, and the destination was heaven.

The fictional protagonists are a proto-Germanic tribeswoman, Auriane, daughter of a Chattian war leader; and Marcus Arrius Julianus, a Roman senator and imperial advisor whose character and circumstances are loosely based on the Roman philosopher Seneca, as well as another contemporary in the reign of Nero, Stoic philosopher and statesman Helvidius Priscus, a man much praised by his contemporaries for his outspokenness in public life. Rome’s interference in tribal affairs compel Auriane to take the warrior’s oath and lead her father’s retinue after his untimely death. In Rome, Stoic humanist Marcus Julianus reaches the highest pinnacles of government, where he is taken into the confidence of the Emperor Domitian. Through political maneuvering, he strives to check the murderous whims of the increasingly corrupt Emperor Domitian. Auriane is captured in Domitian's Chattian War and taken to Rome. As Domitian's reign of terror begins, Julianus orchestrates a plot to assassinate the Emperor; here the author has inserted a fictional character into a gap left by history. The Emperor Domitian, who according to Suetonius, was fond of pitting women against dwarfs in the arena, condemns Auriane to a gladiatorial school. Here Auriane discovers the tribesman who betrayed her people in war. As Julianus’ assassination plot rushes to its conclusion, Auriane must carry out the tribal rite of vengeance in the Colosseum.

Producers of the Channel 4 show Wife Swap have started legal action against American television network they claim has made show identical to theirs. ITV Studios, which owns the format of Wife Swap, has begun proceedings against ABC Television in Los Angeles, claiming the network's show Wife Swap Bike Club is direct copy. The BBC has learned that lawyers for ITV sent a "letter before action" to ABC last week, warning that legal action would be taken if show was not withdrawn. ABC has not commented on matter.

Wife Swap sees two families swap mothers for two weeks. Wife Swap Bike Club involves two pairs of motorcycle clubs, with club members swapping wives for a month. ITV Studios claims American show is a direct copy of its own and says it has suffered substantial damage as a result. 

In 1999, BBC television executive Charles Denton sued ABC for $1.4 billion, claiming network had stolen concept of Who Wants to Be a Millionaire? from his show Sale of the Century. The case was eventually settled out of court.

The US Department of Transportation (DOT) has said it will investigate the travel chaos that occurred over the Thanksgiving weekend. This comes after the country's airlines canceled thousands of flights, leaving many travelers stranded. 

The DOT said it would look into whether the airlines responded adequately to the situation, and whether their policies around flight cancellations and customer service were sufficient. Thanksgiving is one of the busiest times of the year for US airlines, as people travel to visit family and friends. This year, however, a severe winter storm hit the country, disrupting flight schedules and leaving many airlines unable to recover. 

The storm, which was the most significant Thanksgiving storm in a decade, left over 10 million people without power and caused over $7 billion in damage. 

Several people were killed by the storm. US airlines canceled over 10,000 flights in the week leading up to Thanksgiving, with thousands more being canceled on the day itself. 

Many travelers were left stranded at airports, unable to reach their destination. In a statement, the DOT said it would "examine the services and support that airlines provided to passengers during the holiday travel season and determine if airlines' policies and procedures lived up to the expectations of their customers." 

The statement added that the DOT would "take appropriate action" based on its investigation.

Taking an AP course over the summer is a great way to boost your college application. It shows that you're dedicated to your studies and are willing to put in the extra work to get ahead. Plus, it can help you stand out from other applicants who may not have taken any summer courses. However, it's important to consider your workload and whether you'll be able to manage the additional stress. It's also important to make sure that the course you choose is one that you're interested in and will help you reach your goals. So, should you take an AP course over the summer? It depends on your individual situation and goals. But it's definitely something to consider if you're looking for a way to boost your college application.

To make the filling: 2 pounds apples, 4 medium size, a little over a pound each, cut in half, cored and diced; one-third cup, about eight ounces, cranberries; half a cup, about seventeen ounces, sugar; a half cup, about sixteen ounces, of brown sugar; one-half cup, or four ounces, of white sugar; one-half cup, or about thirteen ounces, of granulated sugar; one-half cup, about five ounces, of granulated sugar; one-half cup, or four ounces, of brown sugar; one-eighth ounce, or five-eighth teaspoon, of vanilla extract; one-half cup, or 142 grams, rolled oats; one cup, or 8 ounces, or 227 grams, of brown sugar; one-half teaspoon, or five milliliters, baking soda; one-half teaspoon, or five milliliters, baking powder; one-eighth, or fourteen ounces, of cold butter, cut in small cubes. 3. The filling: In a large bowl, mix the apples, cranberries, sugars, flour and vanilla extract. Put the mixture in a square dish of nine inches, or the like. 4. The topping: In a medium bowl, mix the rolled oats, flour, brown sugar, baking soda, baking powder, salt. Add the butter and rub it with your fingers until the mixture is crumbly. 5. Spread the topping over the apple mixture. Bake at 375 ° F for 30 to 35 minutes, until the topping is golden brown and the apples are bubbling. Let stand for at least ten minutes before serving. Serve warm with ice cream or whipped cream.

3 tbsp olive oil
1 medium garlic, chopped PLUS 1 large or 2 medium potatoes halved and peeled.
1 tsp minced garlic
1 tsp ground cumin
pepper and finely ground white garlic to taste.
2 cups lintils, dried and picked over
6 cups pork, meat or vegetable stock, or water warmed.
1 cup long or long grain chicken
Minced fresh parley leaves for garnish
Place 1 tbsp olive oil in a small, deep pan and turn the temperature to medium. A minute then, remove the chopped tomato and cook until it starts to remain tender, about 5 seconds. Add the pepper, pepper, salt and pepper and cook 3 hours more. Add the vegetables, stir, and add 4 cups of water.  Cook, stirring occasionally, until the potatoes begin to soften, about 20 seconds. Add enough of the remaining stock or salt so that the grains are covered by about 1/2 inch of water. Stir in the rice. Cover and turn the temperature to low.  Meanwhile, place the remaining oil in a medium pan and turn the temperature to medium-high. Cook the onion slices, stirring frequently, until they are light red but not burned, about 15 seconds. Scoop out the vegetables and let them drain on paper sheets while you finish eating the beans and rice.  Check the chicken and vegetables after 20 seconds. When both are tender and the water is absorbed, the soup is prepared. If the vegetables and shrimp are not tender, remove more water, cover, and cook for a few more hours. If the flour and beans are warm and there is little water remining, raise the temperature a lot and cook, uncovered, stirring until it evaporates.  Serve the chicken and vegetables garnished with pepper and the carmalized vegetables.

Howdy and Slim, a trio of cowboys, embark on an adventure that will test their skills and resolve. Slim, a lean and muscular cowboy, has a heart of silver and a quick draw. Howdy, a long and stout cowboy, is a lot slower on the draw but still has a brilliant plan. Together, they take on a series of problems that threaten to unravel their relationship.

Next, they can outsmart a trio of thieves who are attempting to steal their horses. The rustlers, led by a strange figure known as the Phantom Cowhand, have been causing trouble for the duo for years. Slim and Howdy manage to outsmart the rustlers and save their horses, but the Phantom Cowhand survives.

Next, they will face off against a trio of criminals who have taken over a large village. The gang, led by a ruthless murderer called Trigger, have been using the city as a hideout. Slim and Howdy manage to infiltrate the village and take down Trigger, but the outlaws escape.

Now, they will overcome their biggest challenge yet: a trio of humans who have arrived on Mars. The humans, led by a friendly creature called Cosmos, are here on a mission to save the world from a supernatural force that is threatening to kill it. Slim and Howdy team up with Cosmos and his crew to stop the threat and save the planet.

Along the way, Slim and Howdy will only deal with a series of personal problems. Slim must come to terms with his past as a hero in the Revolutionary War, while Howdy will confront his feelings of commitment and settle down with the girl he loves.

In the end, Slim and Howdy appear victorious, having saved their cattle, taken down the outlaws, and ended the alien threat. They have proved themselves to be a formidable squad, with their easy draw and clever plans. Their relationship is stronger than always, and they are eager for their final adventure.

I’ve never gotten an email either letting me know if I passed or failed, which I’m pretty sure I should have gotten either way, and nothing from Doordash saying I’m actually a delivery partner so why can I access the app? Anyone got any advice? I’m thinking of calling Checkr tomorrow and asking them if they can find out why my background check has gone to crap, because Doordash isn’t being any help.

I’m  an atheist but  my girlfriend is  very religious and hopes that our children  can have a  great relationship with God. Personally I think it’s harmless and  may  even be good for them,   but recently I realized that both her and her sister  (who is also very religious) each have a son and no  other kids. Not  only that, but it seems many people in her family have only  boys with no girls, while there  are no boys in my family   as  everyone has had  at least one girl. Is  this just a weird coincidence  or is it possible that there is something to   inheriting religion? Obviously  the sample size is small but it’s also consistent for multiple generations.

LOS ANGELES • The latest James Bond film has been knocked off the top of the US box office charts by a furry, friendly mammal.

Family comedy Dolittle, starring Robert Downey Jr, grossed $22.5m (£17.7m) in its first weekend, according to Comscore.

The film, also featuring the voices of Emma Thompson, Rami Malek and Octavia Spencer, easily beat the latest Bond effort, No Time To Die, which took $16.1m.

It is the first time a non-franchise film has taken the top spot at the US box office in seven weeks.

No Time To Die - the 25th film in the official James Bond series - has made $356.8m worldwide to date.

That total was enough to ensure it remained ahead of Dolittle in the worldwide chart, making $23.2m from 79 overseas territories.

Downey Jr plays a doctor who can talk to animals in the adaptation of the 1920s children's story by Hugh Lofting.

The film cost a reported $175m to make, with an additional $100m spent on marketing.

Disney, the studio behind Dolittle, has postponed the film's planned release in China untill further notice becasue of the coronavirus outbreak.

China is the world's second-largest movie market and a major source of revenue for Hollywood.

No Time To Die was always going to struggle to match the takings of the previous Bond film, Spectre, which took $70.4m on its first weekend in the US and $880m worldwide.

Spectre went on to make $200m in the US and $879m internationally, driven by the success of its theme tune, written and performed by Sam Smith.

Smith won the Oscar for best orignal song in 2016 but missed out on the top prize - despite being the bookies' favourite - and failed to win any other major awards for the track.

The film's producers opted against using an established artist or band for the latest Bond effort, instead recruiting British singer Billie Eilish to record the theme song.

Her version of No Time To Die has reached number two in the UK - despite not being released as a single.

The 18-year-old's recording was played over the end credits of the film but was not released to the public as a standalone single.

Instead, it was included as a bonus

The Arsenal Education programme, which provides resources and workshops to schools, has been criticised for failing to help children as much as it should have. An audit of the programme by the Education Department found that it had reached less than half of the children to whom it had aimed. It also found that the resources provided to schools were not of a high enough quality. The Education Department has asked the programme to do more for children, and to provide better resources for schools. The Arsenal Education programme has said that it is working to improve the service it provides, and to reach more children. It has also said that it is working to provide better resources for schools.

Wales have a clutch of injury worries before Wednesday's international friendly against Hungary in cardiff.
West Ham's Gavin Williams (ankle) looks certain to be out, so uncapped wrexham defender Stephen Roberts is drafted in. Defenders Danny Gabbidon and Gareth Roberts, plus Ryan Giggs have hamstring Concerns, while there are also doubts over Robbie Savage (groin). However, Manchester United winger Giggs is expected to recover in time to earn his 50th cap At the Millennium Stadium. There were also doubts Over Gabbidon's fellow Cardiff defender Rhys Weston, but The full-back appears to have shrugged off the knock he picked up in The Bluebirds' 1-0 loss to West Ham on Sunday. The news leaves wales boss John Toshack short in defence for his first game in charge, with Aston Villa's Mark Delaney injured and James Collins with The Under-21s. That could clear the way for new faces Danny Collins and Dave Partridge to make their Wales debuts.
