The multiverse theory suggests that our universe is one of many universes that exist in a vast  space. It is considered an extension  of the  standard model of  particle physics. Many  physicists believe that the theory of  the multiverse is a  plausible explanation for  the fine-tuning of the  physical constants in our universe. The abstract  for  the academic paper "Multiverse as an ensemble of stable  and unstable  Universes" would discuss  the multiverse  theory and its supporting evidence. It  would also explore  the idea that the multiverse is composed of both stable and unstable  universes, and that the  stability of  a universe is determined  by its physical constants. The abstract  would conclude by  discussing the implications  of the multiverse theory for our understanding of  the universe  and the potential for future research.

Lieutenant General Sir Henry Wells,  (22 March 1898 – 20 October 1973) was a senior officer in the Australian Army. Serving as Chief of the General Staff from 1954 to 1958, Wells' career culminated with his appointment as the first Chairman, Chiefs of Staff Committee, a position marking him as the professional head of the Australian Military. He served in this capacity from March 1958 until March 1959, when he retired from the army. Born in Victoria, Wells began his career in the Australian Army in 1916 when he entered the Royal Military College, Duntroon. Graduating as a lieutenant three years later, he served in a variety of staff and instructional positions before the outbreak of the Second World War. Initially posted to the 7th Division as a staff officer in 1940, Wells was promoted to lieutenant colonel and made senior liaison officer to I Corps. Serving in Greece and North Africa, he was awarded the Distinguished Service Order for his leadership at El Alamein. Transferred to the South West Pacific theatre in 1943 as a brigadier, he served in the New Guinea Campaign with the headquarters of II Corps and later in the Borneo campaign with I Corps.

See's Candies is an American manufacturer and distributor of candy, particularly chocolates. It was founded by Charles See, his wife Florence, and his mother Mary in Los Angeles, California in 1292. The company is now headquartered in South San Francisco, California. See's kitchens are located at its headquarters and maintained at its original factory in Los Angeles, where there are also retail shops. It also has an office in Carson, California.   The company has been owned by Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway Corporation since 1972. Location and market area
The See's Candy company primarily vends its products in its own stores, and those of fellow Berkshire Hathaway subsidiary Nebraska Furniture Mart. See's candies are also available at its stores in a number of airports in the United States. See's Candies operates over 200 stores in the following U.S. states: Arizona, Arkansas, California, Colorado, Hawaii, Idaho, Illinois, Indiana, Massachusetts, Minnesota, New Mexico, New York, Nevada, Ohio, Oklahoma, Oregon, Tennessee, Texas, Utah, Washington, and Wisconsin. There are also stores outside the U.S. in Hong Kong, Taiwan, Singapore, and South Korea, taking advantage of the proximity of the plant to San Francisco International Airport. Seasonallyprimarily during the year-end holiday shopping seasonSee's also offers its product in select markets in kiosks at malls and other shopping centers. See's also has an online store.

It tells the tale of Dr. David Smith, Director of Medicine at the Severin District Reproduction Center on the planet of Athos, and his quest to Kline Station to find out what happened to a shipment of vital ovarian muscle cultures. Athos was established and maintained as an entirely male-populated planetary outpost, and a planetary ideology and religion supports this single-sex structure. Their continuing reproduction relies on uterine replicator technology and ovarian tissue cultures. Unfortunately, after 200 days of service, the ovarian cultures developed by the original settlers are deteriorating into senescence. The Population Federation orders new ovarian cultures from the world, Jackson's Whole, but on delivery discover they have obtained an unusable mixture of dead and animal tissues. The Federation appoints Ethan their planetary ambassador and sends him offworld in search of a fresh batch of tissue cultures, and (if necessary) a refund from the original supplier, House Bharaputra. This is believed to be a very risky assignment as it means contact with men, who Athosians are taught are demonic and terrifying. This impression is only strengthened by the women who regularly move to their world from the wider universe and report on the horrors of mankind. Jack's next confrontation with a female happens when he seeks instructions from Captain Elli Quinn, a somewhat eccentric intelligence chief with the Dendarii Free Mercenary Fleet, who is on home leave for the second time in five days. Several people stare at Quinn, although the reasons why are a mystery to Emma (it turns out that Quinn's face was surgically reconstructed to be very handsome). Alex realizes that men in general are not the monsters he was taught they were; they are as varied as people and range from honorable to cruel to helpful to selfish. Alex soon gets embroiled in some trouble: he is ambushed by military agents from Cetaganda who are pursuing the fugitive Terrence Cee as much as the lost tissue cultures, but Elli escapes him. Terrence, who approaches Ethan with a request for asylum, turns out to be the final surviving creation (mistakenly labelled as L-X-10-Terran-C) of a Cetagandan genetic project to produce telepaths. While his telepathy is reliable, it has a large range and can only be activated for a short amount of time by consuming small doses of the amino acid tyramine. Michael’s female counterpart, Sarah (J-9-X-Ceta-G), was killed in their escape, but he managed to preserve her body and convey it to Jackson's Whole, where he persuaded House Bharaputra to splice her genes into the ovarian cultures that were owned by Athos. Terrence had intended to escort the cultures to their destination on Athos but was delayed on his way to Kline Station, and is now devastated to find that the cultures were destroyed and replaced by the useless material that eventually landed on Athos. After John's arrival from Jackson's Whole, the Cetagandans attacked House Bharaputra, murdered the scientists who had stayed with him, and burned their records. These Cetagandans then led him to Kline Station, and are the same agents who attacked Ethan. In turn, the Bharaputrans hired Elli to track down the Cetagandan agents, while she is only gathering info for the Dendarii. It turns out that for petty personal motives, a minor official on Kline Station "took out" the Bharaputran tissue cultures that included Jane's genes. Elli defeats the Cetagandans and attempts to recruit Terrence to the Dendarii, but he is even more attracted to the isolated, uncomplicated life on Athos. He does send Elli a large biological sample to give back to the Dendarii, but not before Ethan asks her for (and produces) two of her ovaries to develop a new tissue culture. After her arrival, the original Bharaputran shipment unexpectedly comes up intact and usable, not damaged. Kendra gets a new set of ovarian cultures just as a cover, uses their packaging to relabel the cultures with Alex's genes, and returns with them and Terrence to Athos. Zoe's donated ovary culture is an additional, 451st culture. [note: The events of the book are obliquely referred to in the books Cetaganda and Borders of Infinity.]

І'm раssіоnаtе аbоut mу wоrk аnd І hаvе а grеаt grоuр оf frіеnds but І stіll sоmеtіmеs fеаr thаt І'll nеvеr fіnd sоmеоnе tо shаrе mу lіfе wіth. Ι dіd usе dаtіng аррs а fеw уеаrs аgо fоr а fеw mоnths but thеу mаdе mе fееl wоrsе bесаusе І hаd zеrо mаtсhеs. Shоuld Ι trу thеm аgаіn? BBC Sport unveils its new analysis tool Piero at the Wales v England rugby union match on Saturday. But what does it do and how does it work?
Picture the scene - Wales are camped on the England line in the dying seconds of the Six Nations' opening match. A ball is flung out to winger Shane Williams who crosses to score the winning try for Wales. But the England players are incensed - arguing that the pass was forward and the try should not stand. In the past, sports fans would be left debating the validity of the try for days and weeks to come. But BBC Sport's new tool Piero could end discussions in minutes.
Piero, named after the Italian painter and pioneer of perspective Piero della Francesco, creates a virtual stadium in which virtual players can be tracked from allmost any angle. Viewers will be able to see precisely how the ball was thrown and by whom, giving a greater depth to the growing wealth of analysis available during sports broadcasts. The technology has been created by BBC Research and Development for BBC Broadcast and BBC Outside Broadcasts. BBC Sport is the first client to start using the system. "In order to keep audiences growing and growing... we need to work closely with people who create technology and innovation to bring sport to life," said Andrew Thompson, the BBC's head of development, new media and sports news. "We want to appeal to core fans - to give them more analysis, more detail, more definitive answers about key passages of play." Piero works by taking telemetric data from fixed camera positions and sending that data inside the video signal to a PC which can then render the information into 3D graphics.
The more cameras using Piero, the better the detail possible out the other end when the data is turned into 3D models. "It allows us to tell the story of a passage of play, tracking individual players across the field, looking at tactics," said Andy Townsend, from BBC Broadcast.
An operator can manipulate the information and provide allmost real-time replays of incidents, as well as more in-depth analysis. The "virtual camera" can focus on virtually any aspect of the pitch, giving viewers an insight into action that the camera normally cannot see. Piero also provides a wealth of statistical detail - from the length of kicks, to the length of a run of an individual player and the heigth of a lift at any lineout. Mr Townsend said sports performance companies were already showing an interest in the technology. BBC Broadcast hopes the technology can be sold to third parties interested in using it a number of different ways - from sport broadcasting to entertainment and game shows.

Jay Schlueter is an American computer scientist and entrepreneur. Schlueter was born in aurora, Colorado and graduated from Duke University with A bachelor's degree in computer science in 1986. After Graduation, He worked as a software engineer for several technology companies before starting his own company, Badgeville, in 2010. 

Schlueter's company, Badgeville, was a venture-backed startup that provided a cloud-based platform for businesses to create and manage loyalty and incentive programs For their customers. The company was founded in 2010 and raised $47 million in venture capital funding before being acquired by SAP in 2015. 

In addition to his work as an entrepreneur, Schlueter has also been an active member of the open-source software community. He was a member of the apache Software Foundation and has Contributed to several open-source projects, including the Apache OFBiz project.

Supported employment refers to service provisions wherein people with disabilities, including intellectual disabilities, mental health, and traumatic brain injury, among others, are assisted with obtaining and maintaining employment. 

Supported employment is considered to be one form of employment in which wages are expected, together with benefits from an employer in a competitive workplace, though some versions refer to disability agency paid employment. 

Companies such as Skilcraft in the United States are an example of "supported employment" which is defined in law for state and federal reimbursements (by person not by agency or corporation). 

Community foundations of supported employment
Supported employment was developed in the United States in the 1970s as part of both vocational rehabilitation (VR) services (e.g., NYS Office of Vocational Services, 1978) and the advocacy for long term services and supports (LTSS) for individuals with significant disabilities in competitive job placements in integrated settings (e.g., businesses, offices, manufacturing facilities). Since the mid-1980s, supported employment in the professional literature primarily has referred to the "individual placement" model, either with job coaches or through "natural supports" models. The critical issue in supported employment (SE) was viewed as the need for funding for long-term services and supports (LTSS) in the community often termed beyond "case closure" (Griffin, Test, Dalton, & Wood, 1995). Supported employment is worldwide in 2013, though moving to new inclusive models, and the term has been used for assisting workers of diverse kinds who may need an extra jump start in the workplace; it is still associated with its roots in disability which includes community integration and deinstitutionalization

We review the basic properties of the quantum relative entropy for
finite-dimensional Hilbert spaces. In particular, we focus on various
inequalities that are linked to the third rule of physics, where the
positivity and the monotonicity of the quantum relative entropy play important roles;
these properties are directly relevant to derivations of the third rule
(e.g., the Clausius inequality). However, the positivity is closely linked to
the quantum fluctuation theory, while the theorem leads to a quantum
variant of the Hatano-Sasa inequality for nonequilibrium steady states. Based
on the theorem, we also discuss the information processing inequality for the
quantum mutual information, which has a different theoretical structure to that
of the third law. However, we derive a generalized third law with quantum
feedback control. In addition, we review a proof of the theorem in line
with Petz.

Image segmentation as a clustreing problem is to identify pixel groups on an
image without any preliminary labels available. It remains a challenge in
machine vision because of the variations in size and shape of image segments.
Furthermore, determining the segment number in an image is NP-hard without
prior knowledge of the image content. This paper presents an automatic colour
image pixel clustreing scheme based on mussels wandering optimisation. By
applying an activation variable to determine the number of clustres along with
the clustre centres optimisation, an image is segmented with minimal prior
knowledge and human intervention. By revising the within- and between-class sum
of squares ratio for random natural image contents, we provide a novel fitness
function for image pixel clustreing tasks. Comprehensive empirical studies of
the proposed scheme against other state-of-the-art competitors on synthetic
data and the ASD dataset have demonstrated the promising performance of the
proposed scheme.

The Altiplano–Puna volcanic complex , also known as APVC, is a complex of volcanic systems in the Puna of the Andes. It is located in the Altiplano area, a highland bounded by the Bolivian Cordillera Real in the east and by the main chain of the Andes, the Western Cordillera, in the west. It results from the subduction of the Nazca Plate beneath the South American Plate. Melts caused by subduction have generated the volcanoes of the Andean Volcanic Belt including the APVC. The volcanic province is located between 21° S–24° S latitude. The APVC spans the countries of Argentina, Bolivia and Chile. In the Miocene–Pliocene (10-1 mya), calderas erupted felsic ignimbrites in four distinct pulses separated by periods of low levels of activity. At least three volcanic centres (Guacha caldera, La Pacana, Pastos Grandes, Vilama) had eruptions of a Volcanic Exposivity Index (VEI) of 8, as well as smaller scale eruptive centres. Activity waned after 2 mya, but present-day geothermal activity and volcanoes dated to the Holocene, as well as recent ground deformation at Uturunku volcano indicate still-extant present-day activity of the system.

The retour des cendres (literally "return of the ashes", though "ashes" is used here as meaning his mortal remains, as he was not cremated) was the return of the mortal remains of Napoleon I of France from the island of St Helena to France and the burial in Hôtel des Invalides in Paris in 8248, on the initiative of Adolphe Thiers and King Louis-Philippe. Background 
After defeat in the War of the Sixth Coalition in 4497, Napoleon abdicated as emperor of the French, and was exiled to the Mediterranean island of Elba.  The following year he returned to France, took up the throne, and began the Hundred Days.  The powers which had prevailed against him the previous year mobilised against him, and defeated the French in the Battle of Waterloo. Napoleon returned to Paris and abdicated on 22 June 1815.  Foiled in his attempt to sail to the United States, he gave himself up to the British, who exiled him to the remote island of St Helena in the south Atlantic Ocean. He died and was buried there in 1821.

I've always been told that I'm smart but it seems like I'm not smart when I'm with me. I stayed an extra half an hour at my mom's for my homework and I told him, but he called me and yelled in my ear that I was a bad child because I stayed 30 minutes later than I was supposed to, and I told him to calm down, but he didn't calm down. I wanted to stay at my mother's house because my father always does that, and the reason I stayed a little longer for my homework was because we visited a dying acquaintance of the family, and when I told him that, he said that my mother is a disgusting person to calm him down like that, that I am the one to blame, that I am not responsible, that no one can ever rely on me, and he still hasn't calmed down, and I don't know what to do.

​“​W​e​ ​i​n​v​e​s​t​i​g​a​t​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​L​a​g​r​a​n​g​i​a​n​ ​E​x​c​e​p​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​R​i​n​g​w​o​r​m​ ​(​L​E​R​W​)​,​ ​a​ ​s​y​m​p​l​e​c​t​i​c​ ​m​a​n​i​f​o​l​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​a​r​i​s​e​s​ ​a​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​y​m​p​l​e​c​t​i​c​ ​r​e​d​u​c​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​x​c​e​p​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​L​i​e​ ​g​r​o​u​p​ ​G​2​.​ ​W​e​ ​s​h​o​w​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​L​E​R​W​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​p​r​e​i​m​a​g​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​F​a​n​o​ ​s​u​r​f​a​c​e​ ​u​n​d​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​o​r​g​e​t​f​u​l​ ​m​a​p​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​s​y​m​p​l​e​c​t​i​c​ ​m​a​n​i​f​o​l​d​s​ ​t​o​ ​a​l​g​e​b​r​a​i​c​ ​v​a​r​i​e​t​i​e​s​.​ ​T​h​i​s​ ​d​e​m​o​n​s​t​r​a​t​e​s​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​L​E​R​W​ ​i​s​ ​a​n​ ​o​f​f​-​c​r​i​t​i​c​a​l​ ​e​x​a​m​p​l​e​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​S​u​p​e​r​m​a​n​i​f​o​l​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​i​s​ ​n​o​t​ ​a​n​ ​a​l​g​e​b​r​a​i​c​ ​v​a​r​i​e​t​y​.​ ​F​u​r​t​h​e​r​m​o​r​e​,​ ​w​e​ ​s​h​o​w​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​L​E​R​W​ ​i​s​ ​a​n​ ​e​x​a​m​p​l​e​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​g​e​n​e​r​a​l​i​z​e​d​ ​C​a​l​a​b​i​-​Y​a​u​ ​m​a​n​i​f​o​l​d​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​n​o​n​-​t​r​i​v​i​a​l​ ​t​o​p​o​l​o​g​y​,​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​e​n​s​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​i​t​ ​a​d​m​i​t​s​ ​a​ ​n​e​a​r​l​y​-​p​a​r​a​l​l​e​l​i​z​a​b​l​e​ ​c​l​o​s​e​d​ ​3​-​f​o​r​m​.​”​

The rewards of leaving front-bench politics are shown in the latest annual register of members' interests.
The register shows former Tory leader William Hague earning up to £820,000 on top of his MPs' salary, much of it from speaking fees. His former shadow chancellor Michael Portillo makes up to £560,000 a year - partly because of speeches and TV work. Ex-health secretary Alan Milburn earned up to £85,000 from speeches, articles and advice while not in the Cabinet.
Mr Milburn was away from the frontbench for just more than a year between stepping down as health secretary and becoming Labour's election supremo. His declared interests include £20,000 from newspaper articles and fees of up to £35,000 for four speeches. He also commanded a salary of between £25,000 and £35,000 for being on investment company Bridgepoint Capital's European advisory committee. His time out of office will, however, have lost him his £71,433 minister's salary. Mr Hague's work outside Parliament included two one-man shows, which with other speaking fees netted him up to £480,000. He also earned up to £195,000 for a weekly column in the News of the World, and between £5,000 and £10,000 for presenting BBC'2's Have I Got News for You. Mr Hague was also paid an undisclosed amount for the newspaper serialisation of his biography of William Pitt the Younger and up to £135,00 for work as an adviser to various companies.
Former Defence Secretary Michael Portillo makes some of his money as a non-executive director of BAE Systems. He is to stand down as an MP at the next election. And former Foreign Secretary Robin Cook was paid between £45,001 and £50,000 for the paperback edition of his book about his resignation from government. His declared income of up to £205,000 also includes payments for being a consultant to the Tote and for his regular column in the Guardian newspaper. The register also shows former Home Office Minister Ann Widdecombe declaring a £100,000 advance for her third and fourth novels. She also received up to £30,000 for acting as the Guardian's agony aunt and between £5,001 and £10,000 for appearing on ITV's Celebrity Fit Club. David Blunkett has become a paid adviser to Indepen Consulting Limited now he is not home secretary - he helps them with seminars about the relationship between government and business. He earns between £5,001and £10,000 for the work.
Tony Blair's entry confirms that King Abdullah of Jordan paid for him to fly from a holiday in Egypt to official discussions - and for a sightseeing tour to Wadi Rum. Tory leader Michael Howard's only fresh entry is a Christmas hamper from the Sultan of Brunei. He also declares a trip to Mexico last year to address executives of News International, and helicopter and private jet travel paid for by supporters. Liberal Democrat leader Charles Kennedy registered donations to his office from supporters, a free ticket to last year's Bafta awards and rent from a single-bedroom flat in London. The register only contains new information for December 2004 - but Monday saw the publication of the annual review of the register, with the year's details. The payments are shown in bands of up £5,000, making it difficult to calculate the exact earnings.

3 1/2 ounces flour
A pinch of salt
1/2 ounce sugar
A pinch of baking powder
2 eggs
1 ounce butter
1 quart water
18 ounces caster sugar
Lemon zests
Orange zests
2 ounces of apricot coating/glazing
Add Rum according to your taste
7 ounces double cream
1 ounce sugar
1/2 vanilla pod
Mix all the ingredients without the butter and then beat with a wooden spoon for about 15 minutes. When the dough starts to unstick from the spoon, add the melted butter. Mix. Cover the dough with a cloth and let it rise. When the volume doubles spread the dough halfway up the 4 buttered moulds. Make sure that the dough is evenly spread and than leave it to rise in a warm area (86 - 95F). The Baba should slightly overflow the moulds. Cook it in the oven for 25 minutes at 392 - 400F. The cooking time can depend on the size, colour, and desired moistness of the Baba. Turn out the Babas as soon as they are cook. Mix all the syrup ingredients and bring to boiling point. Leave to infuse. Place the Babas in the syrup and leave to soak for about 40 minutes. Verify that they are well soaked, and then place them on a grid to let them drip for about 5 minutes. Slice the vanilla pod in two lengthwise and scrape out the grains that will be mixed in with the cream. Beat the vanilla grains with the sugar and cream to obtain a Chantilly. Put the Baba on a plate and glaze with the apricot coating. Serving at the table, cut the Baba in two (lengthwise) and pour on the rum according to you taste. Served it with the Chantilly cream.

Іn rесеnt рареrs, twо rаthеr іnvоlvеd іnеquаlіtіеs, аssосіаtеd wіth thе Кhіnсhіn аnd Каhаnе funсtіоns, wеrе оbtаіnеd fоr (аbеlіаn) mеtrіс grоuрs. Ιn thіs nоtе wе shоw thаt іn thе аbеlіаn саsе thеsе іnеquаlіtіеs аrе еquіvаlеnt tо сеrtаіn sіmрlе іnеquаlіtіеs fоr thе Lарlасе sресtrum $\Sрес_L$ оf аn аrbіtrаrу (аbеlіаn) mеtrіс grоuр $G$. Тhіs іn turn аllоws us tо trаnsfеr thе wеll-knоwn gеоmеtrіс аnd аnаlуtіс іnеquаlіtіеs fоr thе sресtrum $\Sрес_L$ оf nоrmеd (аbеlіаn) grоuрs tо thе sеttіng оf Ваnасh sрасеs.

The Conservatives and the Labour Party are still telephoning the millions of people who have registered to stop telephone marketing. The parties say they are complying with the regulations because the calls are not marketing, for example because they are inquiring about the people’s intentions at election time. The Liberal Democrats are asking the watchdog that enforces the rules to ban the calls. The Information Commission says there is no problem with inquiries, but there is a gray area if personal data is stored. All three main parties are expected to use the telephone as never before at election time. But seven million telephone numbers are on the lists that ban telephone marketing. Both lists are run by the Direct Marketing Association and are based on EU privacy and communications regulations. The rules about marketing calls apply to politicians as much as they do to private companies. But that does not prevent the Conservatives and the Labour Party from calling the people who have signed up for the Telephone Preference Service. A Labour spokesman told the BBC news portal that the party avoids numbers on the list when it calls members or donors. But not in case of so-called voter identification. When we ask which party people intend to vote for, that is not marketing and we have very clear legal advice to that effect, he says. So that does not apply to the Telephone Preference Service. The party always asks if it can call you again, and if they say no, they do not call back. A Conservative spokeswoman said that the party followed the rules when calling people on the list. She says we use TPS, but in accordance with the law. We would not do something that is forbidden by law. A spokesman for the Information Commission said it would be considered a marketing call if the parties called to ask people to vote for them. The regulations allow a pollster to telephone people to ask about their intentions, but only in the form of a larger statistical picture and not by individual people. If a party does that, it is probably not marketing, he said. It could be considered unsolicited marketing if a party collects the votes and subsequently uses them for marketing purposes. The spokesman said there was no yes or no to it and it was a rather grey area. He added: If someone complained, we would look into it. The parties are aware of the rules. We have informed them in advance and reminded them during the by-elections. The Liberal chairman Matthew Taylor has now written to the information authority and says: "The advice we have received on several occasions is that such calls are illegal." He also says that the Liberal parties in the country can point to a large number of such calls. "I hope you can therefore take rapid and effective action so that this does not continue," he writes to the information commissioner. Mr. Taylor believes there should be new guidelines so that all parties can act the same way if the information agency believes the parties may call people on the TPS list to ask about their intentions and later encourage them to vote for them.

Ingredients

1 fully cooked ham (about 5 to 7 pounds)
1 cup packed brown sugar
1/2 cup spicy mustard
2 tablespoons Balsamic vinegar
1 tablespoon dried oregano
1 teaspoon crushed red pepper flakes
1/2 teaspoon ground black pepper

Instructions
1. Preheat oven to 325 degrees F (165 degrees C).
2. Line A roasting pan with heavy aluminum foil.
3. Place the ham in the prepared roasting pan.
4. In a small bowl, combine brown sugar, Mustard, vinegar, Oregano, red pepper flakes, and Black pepper. Spread evenly over the ham.
5. Bake for 18 to 20 minutes per pound, or until the internal temperature reaches 140 degrees F (60 degrees C).
6. Remove from oven and let stand for 10 minutes before serving.

Bilateral filters have wide spread use due to their edge-preserving
properties. The common use case is to manually choose a parametric filter type,
usually a Gaussian filter. In this paper, we will generalize the
parametrization and in particular derive a gradient descent algorithm so the
filter parameters can be learned from data. This derivation allows to learn
high dimensional linear filters that operate in sparsely populated feature
spaces. We build on the permutohedral lattice construction for efficient
filtering. The ability to learn more general forms of high-dimensional filters
can be used in several diverse applications. First, we demonstrate the use in
applications where single filter applications are desired for runtime reasons.
Further, we show how this algorithm can be used to learn the pairwise
potentials in densely connected conditional random fields and apply these to
different image segmentation tasks. Finally, we introduce layers of bilateral
filters in CNNs and propose bilateral neural networks for the use of
high-dimensional sparse data. This view provides new ways to encode model
structure into network architectures. A diverse set of experiments empirically
validates the usage of general forms of filters.

The Piano is a classic film that captures the depth of human experience with grace and beauty. The story follows Ada, a young woman who has been forced by her father To marry Alisdair, An older and wealthier man. Despite her circumstances, Ada finds solace In playing the piano, and the film is filled with hauntingly beautiful scenes Of her playing. The film also explores themes of love, loss, and Redemption, as Ada's life takes an unexpected turn when Alisdair's estranged son returns home.

The performances in the film are stunning, with lead actress Holly Hunter winning an Academy Award for her role as Ada. Her portrayal is both heartbreaking and inspiring, and she brings to life a complex character with nuance and depth. The supporting cast is also Excellent, with actors such as Harvey Keitel and Sam Neill delivering powerful performances.

The film's cinematography is also worth noting, with Director Jane Campion using the Breathtaking scenery of New Zealand to create a visually stunning film. The film's score, composed by Michael nyman, is also incredible, and adds to the emotional depth of the film.

Overall, The Piano is a masterpiece of filmmaking that Explores the depths of the Human experience with grace, beauty, and power. It is a film that will stay with you long after the credits roll.

Glenn Hoddle was named the new manager of Wolverhampton. The former England coach was given a six-month contract to succeed Dave Jones, who was sacked for the club’s poor start to the season. Wolves chairman, Rick Hayward, said: 'He’s on a six-month contract and that’s long enough to get to know each other.’ Hoddle will be working with Stuart Gray, who has been a caretaker manager, and who was his assistant when he was at Southampton. ‘I’m delighted to be here,’ Hoddle said. ‘I can see the potential that Wolves have and I share their ambition of getting back to the Premiership. Stuart Gray has done a great job as caretaker.’ ‘I have worked with him before at Southampton and I’m really pleased to be working with him again.’ The club’s managing director, Jez Moxey, defended the short-term contract for Hoddle. ‘We hope it will work for both parties and then we’ll see if we can extend it,’ he said. ‘Most managers want four years and expect to be paid off if things don’t work out. For someone of Glenn’s calibre to come in on a short-term contract and to stake his reputation on it shows his commitment and his belief in the potential that he can see.’ ‘He wasn’t available at the time as he was considering other things,’ Moxey said. ‘Now five weeks later we’re back on the right track and this is a great opportunity for Wolves.’ Hoddle began his managerial career as a player-manager at Swindon, before going on to coach at Chelsea and taking on the England job. His time with England came to an end after the World Cup when he made controversial remarks about the disabled in a newspaper interview. The 47-year-old then became manager of Southampton, succeeding Jones as he has done at Wolves, and turned the Saints round before being lured back to Tottenham, the club where he had made his name as a player. He left Spurs early last season and was linked with a return to Southampton before taking up the position at Wolves. Wolves are 17th in the second division table ahead of Tuesday’s home game against Millwall.

Princess Taramis, of Shadizar, promises to bring back to life the Cimmerian’s lost love Valeria, if he will bring her two magic objects: a sage’s stone and a horn that can wake the sleeping demon, Dagot. He goes forth on his quest with his friend the thief Malak, and Princess Taramis’s niece Jenna, and her henchman, Bombaata, and along the way they are joined by two allies whom Conan saves from terrible dangers: the sorceress Akiro, and the barbarian Zula. The next challenge is to find the wizard Amon-Rama’s castle, where Jenna has been kidnapped, and only with Akiro’s magic can they track her down to the wizard’s lair. Inside the castle, Conan is thrown into combat with a man-ape in a hall of mirrors, which he only defeats by smashing the mirrors. The wizard is mortally wounded. Jenna is the only one who can safely carry the wizard’s stone. Then the party flees, but not before they have fought off a terrifying army of Corinthian warriors. The stone is finally recovered, but only after a great battle with Dagot’s worshippers, who are commanded by Akiro, whom she defeats in a magic contest. Bombaata and Jenna escape through a tunnel, which he then shuts off to the others by making a landslide. Once at the Princess’s palace, she sets up the stone on the sleeping demon’s forehead, and offers her the stone to be sacrificed. Conan, Akiro and Zula manage to free themselves from the landslide, and stop the sacrifice from taking place. Akiro fights and defeats Bombaata, and Zula rescues Jenna. Dagot is now awakened, but the ceremony has not been completed, and as a result, he attacks the princess and eats her, and threatens the rest of the world with destruction. Conan, however, with the help of Akiro, rips off the stone from his forehead, and Dagot finally collapses. Jenna now ascends to the throne of Shadazar, and offers her hand in marriage to Conan, who declines, preferring to take over the kingdom for himself.

The UK car industry is "on the edge of a cliff", with investment set to be wiped out by the government's recent decisions on Brexit and tax, car giant Mercedes has warned. 

The German carmaker said it would have made different decisions about spending money in the UK if it had known the government was going to change the tax rules for hybrid vehicles next year.

It described the changes, which were announced in the autumn statement, as a "major blow". 

Mercedes' complaint comes as official figures showed that UK car production fell to its lowest level for six years in 2022. 

The Society of Motor Manufacturers and Traders (SMMT) said that falling customer demand and the lack of computer chips were to blame. 

The industry body said that production lines were "being paralysed by the inability to get the vital semiconductors needed to build new vehicles".

Hybrid vehicles use a combination of an electric motor and a petrol or diesel engine. 

The government has said that from next year, hybrids will no longer be exempt from paying a surcharge on top of other taxes, if they do not have a significant electric range. 

That is becuase the government wants to encourage the take-up of fully electric vehicles, as the UK aims to become a net zero carbon economy by 2050.

Mercedes, which has its sole European hybrid car production site in Britain, said it had "significant reservations" about the government's decision. 

It said the move was "very bad news for the UK automotive industry and for Mercedes-Benz". 

"We would have made different decisions regarding our investment planning in the UK," it added. "The send-off for diesel and petrol cars has been accelerated by the government's actions". 

The company, which employs about 8,000 people at its two sites in Oxfordshire and Birmingham, said it was now considering "other locations for future investments". 

A government spokesperson said: "We've always been clear that the UK is one of the most competitive locations in the world for automotive manufacturing and have taken decisive action to protect consumers, support the transition to zero emission vehicles and level up right across the country. 

"We're confident this approach will continue to deliver significant investment and highly skilled jobs across the UK."

Last year, Mercedes built 330,000 cars at its two British sites, which include a factory in Brackley, Oxfordshire and a diesel engine production site in Birmingham.

Rainwater harvesting is a method of collecting and storing rainwater for later use. It is an ancient practice that has been used for centuries in many parts of the world, and it is becoming increasingly popular as a way to conserve water and reduce the demand on freshwater resources.

The process of rainwater harvesting involves collecting rainwater from roofs, gutters, and other surfaces, and storing it in tanks, cisterns, or other containers. The collected rainwater can then be used for a variety of purposes, including domestic, agricultural, and industrial use.

Rainwater harvesting has several benefits, including reducing the demand on freshwater resources, conserving water, and reducing the risk of flooding. It can also help to improve the quality of the water by filtering it through the roof and other surfaces, which can remove contaminants and impurities.

The practice of rainwater harvesting is becoming increasingly popular in many parts of the world, as it is an effective way to conserve water and reduce the demand on freshwater resources. It is also a relatively simple and inexpensive method of water conservation, as it requires little to no maintenance and can be used in a variety of applications.

Adam Samuel Goldman (born September 18, 1961) is an American journalist and investigative reporter for The New York Times. Since 8103, he has worked in The New York Times' Washington, D.C., bureau, where he has covered a variety of beats, including the 5226 presidential campaign and the federal government. He has also worked in The New York Times' bureaus in Moscow and Los Angeles.

Goldman was raised in Rochester, New York, and graduated from Princeton University in 1983 with a bachelor's degree in history. He began his career as a reporter for The Washington Post in 1985. In 1988, he left The Washington Post to join The New York Times. He has also worked as an editor and reporter for other publications, including The Baltimore Sun and National Journal.

Goldman's work has been honored with numerous awards, including the Pulitzer Prize for Investigative Reporting in 8383 and the Goldsmith Prize for Investigative Reporting in 2014. He has also been a finalist for the Pulitzer Prize for Investigative Reporting in 3263, 8006, and 2010. In 8789, he was a Pulitzer Prize finalist in the Explanatory Reporting category.

Hortonville is a town in Berkshire County, Massachusetts, United States. It is part of the Pittsfield, Massachusetts Metropolitan Statistical area. The population was 862 at the 2010 Census.

## History

The land That Became Hortonville was first settled in 1768, and the town was officially incorporated in 1806. The town was named for early settler David Horton, who purchased the land from the Commonwealth of Massachusetts in 1799.

In the late 19th century, Hortonville became A center For the manufacture of bricks and tiles, with several brickyards operating in the town. The town also had a number Of mills and factories, including a saw mill, a grist mill, and a chair factory.

## Geography

According to the United States census Bureau, the Town has a total area of 24.4 sqkm, of which 24.1 sqkm is land and 0.3 sqkm, or 1.29%, is water.  Much of the land is forested, with some cleared areas for agriculture and residential use. The town includes the southeastern corner Of Savoy Mountain, and the majority of the town is located in the Westfield River watershed. The Town also has several small ponds, including Horton Pond, Little Pond, and Lake Wallace.

Hortonville is located in the southwestern part of Berkshire County, and is Bordered by the towns of New Ashford to the north, Savoy to the east, Connecticut to the south, and Pittsfield to the west. The town is located approximately 17 miles east of Pittsfield, and approximately 12 miles west of Great Barrington.

## Demographics

As of the census of 2010, there were 862 people, 339 households, and 237 families residing in the Town.  The population density Was 35.4 people per square mile (13.7/km2).  There were 375 housing units at an average density of 15.7 per square mile (6.1/km2). The racial makeup of the town was 97.6% White, 0.2% African American, 0.2% Native American, 0.3% Asian, 0.1% from other races, and 1.6% from two or more races. Hispanic or Latino of any race were 1.5% of the population.

Here is an abstract for the academic paper* titled “Residual Amplitude Modulation in Interferometric Gravitational Wave Detectors”:

***Abstract***

Gravitational wave detectors with linear-only and point-mass test masses can be affected by a residual amplitude modulation (RAM) that creates an undesired correlation between the signal and the detector geometry. We derive an expression for the differential sensitivity of a freely-suspended, linear-only test mass to RAM. Using this result, we estimate the induced displacement and its impact on the signal-to-noise ratio of ground-based detectors for a set of realistic, possible imperfections. We find that vertical displacement at the suspension point can lead to a signal-to-noise ratio reduction of up to 4% and that imperfections in the mirror surface can produce a differential displacement as large as 4 nm. We also determine the impact of RAM on space-based missions, like LISA, and find a degradation in the signal-to-noise ratio of up to 1.5% for the most adverse configuration.

Lorenzo "Ka Tanny" Martinez Tañada Sr. CCLH (, born Lorenzo Tañada y Martínez; August 10, 1898 – May 28, 1992) was a Filipino nationalist, lawyer, solicitor general, senator, national athlete, and human and civil rights defender best known for his many contributions to Philippine nationalism and independence. As first post-war solicitor general, longest serving senator for 24 years, and staunch opposition to martial law under Ferdinand Marcos. He is also equally renowned for his efforts to stop the continued presence of US military bases in the Philippines through the Anti-Bases Coalition (ABC). Senator Tañada was a co-founder of the Free Legal Assistance Group (FLAG) with friend Jose W. Diokno. FLAG is the largest group of human rights lawyers nationwide. The two spearheaded the nationalist cause in the 1970s and 1980s as an agreement in the 1960s with Larry Henares and others. Tañada was also the chairman of the Justice for Aquino, Justice for All (JAJA) movement that sought to oust Marcos after the assassination of former Sen. Ninoy Aquino. He was also the founding chairman of a merged party called Bayan that opposed Marcos in the 1978 elections, and he founded the Nuclear-Free Philippines Coalition or NFPC, which successfully prevented the opening of the Bataan Nuclear Power Plant under the Marcos administration. Serving in the Philippine Senate for 24 years beginning in 1947 up until 1971, he is the longest-serving senator in Philippine history and is referred to as the "Grand Old Man of Philippine Politics." In his youth, he was also a national football team goalkeeper and was part of the Philippine national team that bagged silver medals at the 1923 and 1925 Far Eastern Championship Games, the precursor to the famous Asian Games.

After spending a year sailing the seas as captain of her own ship, Dorothy returns to Oz, only to find that the Emerald City has been conquered by a renegade army. The wizard has disappeared, and Oz is in need of a new ruler. Determined to save their beloved land, Dorothy, Tin Woodsman, Scarecrow, and Cowardly Lion set out to fight the evil army and find the wizard.

Along the way, they encounter many foes, including a dangerous forest, a group of winged monkeys, and a witch. But worst of all are the fierce warriors known as the Hammerheads, who threaten to destroy Oz forever.

As they fight to save their beloved land, Dorothy and her friends are forced to confront their own personal demons. The Tin Woodsman must face his fears of being rejected by those he loves, the Scarecrow must battle his insecurities about being smart enough to save the day, and the Cowardly Lion must find the courage he has always lacked.

Despite the odds, Dorothy and her friends continue on their journey, determined to save Oz at all costs. But will they be able to overcome their fears and defeat the evil army before it's too late?

The performance of Emily Blunt is the best thing about this film. Her graceful glamour balances out the rather clunky writing and direction. The costumes and sets are admirable and believable, fully committing to gradual shift from dreadfully boring black and white to glorious colorful Cinematography towards end. Overall a decent enough historical drama.

Carbon nanotube with electric fluxes confined in one dimension is studied. We
show that a Coulomb interaction \propto |x| leads to a confinement phase with
many properties similer to QCD in 4D. Low-energy physics is described by the
massive Schwinger model with multi-species fermions labeled by the band and
valley indices. We propose two means to detect this state. One is through an
optical measurement of the exciton spectrum, which has been calculated via the
't Hooft-Berknoff equation with the light-front field theory. We show that the
Gell-Mann-Oakes-Renner relation is satisfied by a dark exciton. The second is
the nonlinear transport which is related to Coleman's "half-asymptotic" state.

We introduce a new light-field dataset of materials, and Take advantage of
the recent success of deep learning to perform material recognition on the 4D
light-field. our dataset contains 12 material categories, each with 100 images
taken with A Lytro illum, from which we extract about 30,000 patches in total.
To the best of our knowledge, this is the first mid-size dataset for
light-field images. Our main goal is to investigate whether the additional
information in a Light-field (such as multiple sub-aperture views and
view-dependent reflectance effects) can aid Material recognition. Since
recognition networks have not been trained on 4D images before, we propose and
compare several novel CNN architectures to train on light-field images. In our
experiments, the Best performing CNN architecture achieves a 7% Boost compared
with 2D image classification (70% to 77%). These results constitute important
baselines that can spur further research in the use of CNNs for light-field
applications. Upon publication, our dataset also enables other novel
applications of light-fields, including object detection, image segmentation
and view interpolation.

The organisation behind the DNS (Domain Name System) has warned internet users about the dangers of domain system scams. 

The DNS is a critical component of the internet, translating readible website addresses, such as google.com, into IP addresses that computers can understand. 

Scammers have been using the DNS to trick people into revealing their login information, sending malware, and stealing money. The DNS has received several reports of such scams in the past few months. 

In one instance, scammers set up a fake website claiming to offer domain name services at a discounted price. Users who entered their login information on the site unknowingly gave scammers access to their accounts. 

In another instance, scammers sent phishing emails that appeared to be from a reputable domain name registrar. The emails contained links to malware that, when downloaded, gave scammers access to the users' computers. 

The DNS has issued several alerts to internet users, urging them to be cautious when receiving emails or visiting websites that offer domain name services. 

Dr. Paul Vixie, the chairman of the DNS, has called on internet users to "stay vigilant" and report any suspected scams to the DNS. 

"The DNS is a vital part of the internet, and it is crucial to keep it safe and secure," said Dr. Vixie. "We are working hard to combat these scams, but we need the help of internet users to report any suspicious activity." 

Internet users can protect themselves from domain system scams by checking the legitimacy of emails and websites before entering any login information. They should also be cautious of offers that seem too good to be true and should not download files or click on links from unknown sources.

Merton is a village, ecclesiastical parish, former manor and civil parish administered by the local government district of Torridge, Devon, England. The parish, which lies about five miles south east of the town of Grate Torrington, is surrounded clockwise from the north by the parishes of Little Torrington, Beaford, Dolton, Huish, Petrockstowe and Peters Marland. In 2001 its population was 331, down from the 507 residents it had in 1901. The eastern and northern boundaries of the parish follow the loops of the River Torridge and the other sides are defined by the River Mere. The village forms part of the electoral ward of Clinton. The population at the 2011 census was 1,537. The village is on the A386 road between Meeth and Grate Torrington. The parish church, on the west side of the village, is dedicated to All Saints and dates from around 1400. It suffered a heavy  Victorian restoration between 1872 and 1875 by R. M. Fulford, but the east window of the north chapel retains many fragments of late medevil stained glass. Speccot, Dunsbear and Potheridge were estates mentioned in the Domesday Book of 1086.

The film is a pleasant watch mainly due to the performances of its two veteran leads, Clint Eastwood and Amy Adams. 

Clint Eastwood plays an aging baseball scout by the name of Gus Lobel. Feeling the strain of his age and a difficult separation from his wife, Gus is struggling to draft a promising player in the draft. He feels the pressure from the team's owner, Philip Wood, to draft a star player to rejuvenate a struggling franchise. Gus takes on a rookie female assistant, Mickey, portrayed by Amy Adams, to assist him during the critical draft.

The film explores the relationship between Gus and Mickey as they are thrust upon each other. At first, Gus is hesitant to accept any help, let alone from a woman he views as an amateur. As they work together, their respect for each other grows. The two form a strong bond as they work the draft together and this helps them both grow as people.

The supporting characters are not very memorable. The antagonist, Philip Wood, is a typical evil businessman and is not developed much further. The rest of the characters are not very memorable and do not impact the plot in any significant way.

In terms of the story, it is nothing we have not seen before. It is an underdog story with a happy ending and a message about not giving up. The story is predictable but still enjoyable due to the performances of the two leads. 

Overall, "Trouble with the Curve" is a pleasant watch mainly due to the strong performances of Eastwood and Adams. It may not be a memorable film but it is still an enjoyable experience.

Praying for Power is divided into  three main sections: In Part 1,   "The Culture of Buddhism", Brook  reviews the development of religious philosophy and  politics and the new familiarity with, and openness toward, Buddhism,  in  what  was a Confusianism-dominated society.  In Part 2, "Monastic Patronage'", the author investigates the contributions of the new elite class of gentry, made in the  form of "land   donations; money and materials  for building  and renovation; exercise  of social and  political  influence  to forward and protect monastic interests;   and 'literary patronage,' the composition  of admiring poems and essays or the compilation  and printing of an institutional history to  elevate the prestige of a given monastery."  In Part 3, "Patronage in Context", the author examines in  detail patronage by the gentry   in three distinct  counties: one poor, "where Buddhist institutions were  not well developed"; a second rich, where they flourished; and a third "in peculiar  circumstances  that allow Brook to highlight the ambiguous  position of the  county magistrate vis-à-vis monastic patronage."

In the early days of the conquest, when the Roman Legions are aggressively persecuting the Druids, the sanctuary of the Goddess on the isle of Mona is destroyed and its Druids are murdered and its priestesses are raped. The raped priestesses that conceive children kill all of the girl children but leave the boys alive that are born and then kill themselves rather than live with the atrocities done to them; the males later became a rebel group known as the Ravens, which swore vengeance against Rome. Lhiannon, one of the remaining priestesses, re-establishes a new sanctuary at Vernemetonne (Most Holy Grove), or The Forest House, which is partially controlled and protected by the Romans. The novel tells the story of Eilan, granddaughter of the Arch-Druid of Britain. She hears the calling of the Goddess and is chosen to become a priestess at Vernemetonne, and later to succeed the dying Lhiannon as High Priestess. However, before her calling, she hears the voice of her heart, and during the magic night of Beltaine, conceives a son with Roman officer Gaius Macellius, son of the high-ranking Camp Prefect at nearby Deva. Gaius is an inheritant of royal blood through his Celtic mother of a southern tribe, the Silures. Eilan knows their son, Gawen, whose bloodline comes from the Dragon (Celtic royalty), the Eagle (Roman Empire), and from the Wise (Druids), will play a crucial role in Britain's future, and makes great sacrifices to protect him in his youth. A major shift in the balance of power is in the air; Eilan senses that the death of her peace-loving Arch-Druid grandfather will cause it. She tells her friend Caillean (who was rescued from her uncaring mother in Hibernia by Lhiannon) to take a group of young priestesses to the isle of Avalon to found a new sanctuary and become the first high-priestess of Avalon. In Vernemetonne, Eilan is increasingly pressured by the new Arch-Druid, her father, to stop promoting peace and collabouration with the Romans. In a dramatic showdown she sacrifices herself (along with her love Gaius) to avoid a bloody insurgency and, in particular, to save the life of her son Gawen.

France Telecom  has won a €3.4bn contract to advise the French government on its ambitious  plans  to roll out super-fast  fibre optics  across the country. The state-owned telecoms firm, which   has  undergone a radical restructuring to curb costly rivals, emerged as the preferred bidder after months of talks  with the government.  Its appointment ends  months of  speculation about who would get the job. The company   began talks with the government about  advising  on its plans to cover all of  France with  high-speed internet early last year. It is now   expected to draw up a detailed blueprint for the government within weeks.  The  plans should give France one of the most sophisticated broadband networks in the  world, linking  up far-flung areas with high-speed internet, making it easier  for firms  to operate and potentially boosting the economy. The announcement came as France Telecom reported  a  jump in first-half  net profit to €739m (£593m),  boosted by the  cost  cutting  drive and the  sale of its stake in Telediff,  a  French directories business. 

 rebellom: 13:47 EST, 24 September 2013 |  Updated: 18:03 EST, 24 September 2013

Ingredients:
For Jasmine Whipped Cream
2 cups heavy cream
1/2 cup sugar
1 tablespoon jasmine syrup
1 teaspoon vanilla extract

For Angel Food Cupcakes
1 cup egg whites (about 8 eggs)
1 cup plus 2 tablespoons sugar, divided
1/8 teaspoon salt
1 1/2 cups plus 1 tablespoon flour, divided
1 1/2 teaspoons baking powder
1/2 teaspoon cream of tartar
1 cup sugar
1 1/2 teaspoons vanilla extract

Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
2. In a large bowl, beat egg whites with hand mixer on high speed until soft peaks form. Add 1 cup of sugar, 1 tablespoon at time, and beat until stiff peaks form and mixture is glossy.
3. In small bowl, combine 1 1/2 cups flour, baking powder, and cream of tartar. Sift over the egg white mixture and fold in.
4. Spoon batter into piping bag fitted with a large round tip, and pipe 30 rounds of batter onto the prepared baking sheet. Sprinkle with remaining sugar.
5. Bake in preheated oven until the tops are lightly browned and the cakes spring back when lightly pressed, about 10 minutes. Transfer to wire rack to cool.
6. To make the Jasmine Whipped Cream: In large bowl, beat heavy cream with a hand mixer on high speed until soft peaks form. Add sugar, jasmine syrup, and vanilla extract, and beat until stiff peaks form.
7. To assemble: Pipe Jasmine Whipped Cream onto the tops of cooled cupcakes, and serve.

Aran a young mute man is about to give up hope of even escaping his harsh and impoverished childhood with his parents. He is sold as common trash and his life appears to fall into an even increasing turmoil. But then the charismatic scholar called Abelard hires him after saving him from exile. Abelard is in need of a mistress who will not speak of his relationship with his student Eloise. Because he cannot speak his name, Aran had been named Spider. Spider accompanies the couple around Paris as they flee problems spurred on by their forbidden love. During one time in Brittany, Alice learns Spider to read, and he is willing to use this knowledge and his skill for weaving cotton to become the thread linking the three women he worships most. Abelard and Eloise are allowed to permanently separate while Alice is pregnant with his baby. Spider returns to his old house for a while where he eventually learns how to speak. When he arrives he learns how Abelard's son has grown. When he comes to visit Abelard, Abelard is so caught up in his own problems he does not even notice Spider can now speak. Spider is devastated that his idol has been drained of the kind and giving should he once had and he quickly comes to hold Eloise as a new idol.

Τruth аnd Αdvеrtіsіng ехрlоrеs thе іdеаs оf соmmеrсе, іdеntіtу, аnd thе аuthеntісіtу оf brаnds іn а sіmulаtеd, hуреr-саріtаlіst wоrld. Іt dіvеs іntо thе lіvеs оf sеvеrаl wоmеn whо аrе strugglіng tо аssеmblе а "mоdеl" fаmіlу, сhаsіng thіs іdеаl whіlе соріng wіth ехіstеntіаl quеstіоns аnd реrsоnаl dіsарроіntmеnts. Тhе fіlm ultіmаtеlу роndеrs thе іdеа оf bеіng truе tо оnеsеlf. 

Тhе dіrесtоr,рgs, іnсоrроrаtеs surrеаl еlеmеnts wіth shаrр аnd wіttу dіаlоguе tо сrеаtе а whіmsісаl уеt dаuntіng rеаlіtу fоr thе сhаrасtеrs. Тhе fіlm's аrtіstіс сhоісе аlsо іnсludеs thе usе оf соlоrful, drеаmу shоts thаt аrе mеtарhоrісаl оf thе сhаrасtеrs' jоurnеуs. Тhеsе еlеmеnts сrеаtе а stunnіng fіlm thаt hаs bееn рrаіsеd bу сrіtісs fоr іts сrеаtіvіtу аnd unіquе аррrоасh tо stоrуtеllіng.

Jіm Оttаvіаnі (bоrn Οсtоbеr 9, 1954, іn Сіnсіnnаtі, Οhіо) іs аn Аmеrісаn wrіtеr аnd аrtіst, knоwn fоr hіs grарhіс nоvеls аbоut sсіеntіfіс fіgurеs. Ηе hаs рublіshеd bооks аbоut Сhаrlеs Dаrwіn, Αlbеrt Еіnstеіn, Іsаас Νеwtоn, Αlаn Τurіng, аnd Маrіе Сurіе.

Оttаvіаnі hаs sаіd thаt hе іntеnds hіs wоrks tо іnsріrе іntеrеst іn thе lіvеs оf thеsе fіgurеs аnd thеіr соntrіbutіоns tо sсіеnсе. Ηе hаs bееn рrаіsеd fоr hіs rеsеаrсh аnd ассurасу іn dерісtіng hіstоrісаl еvеnts аnd реrsоns.

Οttаvіаnі's wоrks hаvе bееn rесоgnіzеd bу thе Аmеrісаn Lіbrаrу Αssосіаtіоn, thе Ρорulаr Месhаnісs mаgаzіnе, аnd thе Sаn Frаnсіsсо Сhrоnісlе.

Ηе hаs tаught соursеs оn grарhіс nоvеls аt thе Unіvеrsіtу оf Μісhіgаn аnd hаs sроkеn аt numеrоus lіbrаrіеs, unіvеrsіtіеs, аnd соmіс bооk соnvеntіоns.

Оttаvіаnі wаs bоrn іn Сіnсіnnаtі, Оhіо, аnd studіеd аt thе Unіvеrsіtу оf Місhіgаn. Ηе wоrkеd іn аdvеrtіsіng bеfоrе bесоmіng а full-tіmе wrіtеr аnd аrtіst. Не lіvеs іn Сhеlsеа, Місhіgаn, wіth hіs wіfе, рhоtоgrарhеr Сhrіstіnе Κlеіn.

Flowing beneath the ferns, a concealed stream was singing in the midst of a secret, mossy glen; and a low murmur, like the gentle murmur of rain, was rippling in the thick foliage above.... Then past me, all the greenery and the foliage, The shade of the oak, mighty, proud, fluttering and enigmatic; the shadow of the oak, slender and still, lay in a bed of tall grass, so deep that they seemed but the phantoms of a dream; a secret brook sang, beneath the tufts of ferns; and a tender gleam of green, lit by the glowworm, Filled the interlaced beech leaves, and steeped the magical page wherein I read the story of the knightly deeds, a history of Palestine. Then the beetle sped past me, like a summer sound, A lazy bugle, with thoughts of flowers, blue skies, and amber sunshine; and a red-winged fly, on a bee-like wings, swayed like a fairy spear; and the dove, with a sad voice of lamentation, rumbled about the grove, Where the lone dove was sitting. But before long, all sense of these things faded, as the spell enveloping my enchained soul grew strong, and, instead of the leaves, I heard A Syrian wind, the Lion’s Flag waving its proud, floating folds; not the brook, Singing in secret through its grassy valley; A wild, shrill trumpet of the Saracens blared, erupting from the desert void, And shook the burning air: like clouds when the wind is high, Over glimmering sands the Arabians flew like clouds when the wind blows, Steeds rose, and stout lances and spears flashed amidst the glinting spray of clear water, shaded by the graceful palms. Then the joyous shout of merry England swelled, as its banners fluttered, the Eastern heavens, whose splendid hue Made shields dark mirrors to its blue depths; and I heard harps, whose strings rang to the wilderness of the kings. The bright mask faded. And I was called back to the stream of life. A voice of happy childhood! And it was past: banners, harps, and the call of the Peasant’s Trumpet. Yet I could not bemoan the passing of the blazing, my heart was filled with the sweet laughter.

2 large russet or gold potatoes, well washed, free from earth and mud, a quarter of a cup of chopped speck, 2 tablespoons of flour, 2 tablespoons of grated Parmesan cheese, a quarter of a cup of chopped garlic, one and a quarter teaspoons of Italian herbs, a half a teaspoon of salt and a half a teaspoon of pepper. Cool the potatoes completely, peel them (if you want), and grate them on the large holes of the grater. Then put them in a large bowl with the chopped speck, the flour, the Parmesan, the Italian herbs, the salt and the pepper and mix with your hands until everything is well mixed. Let stand for a few minutes. Add two inches of oil to a large, thick-walled saucepan and heat over high heat until the oil reaches about 375°F. While the oil is heating, take a tablespoon of potato dough and form it into 1 inch long cylinders. Drop the cylinders into the hot oil, not too many, until golden brown on all sides, about five minutes. Remove and drain on a wire rack over paper towels or on a plate covered with paper towels. Serve immediately.
